The piece is descriptive and lacks specific drivers for nickel and aluminium. | China’s customs data show bauxite imports rose 12.5% year-on-year in Jan-Feb 2023, with Guinea supplying 17.12 million mt (+48.7%). January imports hit a record 12.38 million mt, with Guinea again at record levels. Better post-rainy-season logistics boosted shipments; Indonesia’s export ban uncertainty remains. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) says it has expanded Reduction Line 6 nameplate capacity to 560,000 mtpa from 540,000 mtpa by increasing current. The creep upgrade was achieved safely and supports operational efficiency and throughput while preparing for Line 7. This signals incremental aluminium supply capacity growth. | Fastmarkets says secondary aluminium billet premiums in Europe (ddp) narrowed but remained elevated, while low-carbon differentials for value-added products were flat. Market sources cited steadier downstream demand and growing inquiries for recycled/low-carbon material, alongside tighter European ingot availability and lingering low-carbon supply concerns that support aluminium premium levels. | Surge Copper reported final 2022 reconnaissance drill and sampling results for its Ootsa-Berg projects, highlighting expanded copper anomalies and multiple copper intersections at targets like Bergette and Sibola. It also outlined initial 2023 exploration plans. The news is supportive for long-run copper expectations but unlikely to move near-term prices materially. | BNN Bloomberg’s weekend edition highlights Alcoa saying its output costs are easing and that aluminum demand is staying steady. This combination suggests improved near-term margins for aluminium producers, supporting market sentiment toward aluminium prices over the next few weeks as cost pressure eases and demand remains intact.

SMM reports aluminium prices rose overnight on both SHFE and LME. It cites improving end demand, growing supply (Guizhou/Sichuan), but ongoing risk of power rationing for Yunnan smelters. A bigger-than-expected drop in aluminium ingot inventories is noted as a key support, though further gains depend on fundamentals. | Alcoa’s CEO warns that Russian aluminium could flood LME warehouses, implying rising near-term stock levels. If additional Russian metal reaches the market faster than demand can absorb, it would likely pressure aluminium prices by weakening prompt tightness and increasing inventory visibility at the exchange. | Copper prices fell after a Federal Reserve policymaker suggested more U.S. rate hikes because inflation remains “problematic.” The move supported a stronger dollar effect on dollar-priced metals. The article also reported declines in LME aluminium and nickel, consistent with a broader risk-off, tighter-monetary impulse. | China’s aluminium industry official says domestic bauxite supply is lower grade and self-sufficiency is declining, increasing reliance on overseas bauxite. With Indonesia set to stop bauxite exports from June and China planning additional alumina capacity, market participants may expect tighter alumina feedstock and supportive aluminium prices. | SMM reports China’s aluminium ingot social inventories at 906,000 mt as of Apr 20, down 60,000 mt week-on-week and at the lowest level versus the prior five-year period. Aluminium billet inventories also fell to 152,600 mt, aided by improved warehouse shipments and temporary transport hindrances in Xinjiang. | Norsk Hydro said operations at its Hydro Ardal and Hydro Karmoy aluminum plants and shipments to customers will resume after a labor dispute ended. The prior strike had caused delays, but Hydro expects limited financial consequences. Relief from supply disruption is supportive for near-term aluminium pricing. | Executives at the World Copper Conference warned supply may fall short of rapidly rising demand for electrification and electronics, with regulatory approvals for new mines at a decade low. Data from the International Copper Association points to a growing deficit even with recycling. Analysts expect copper prices to rise and some substitution into aluminum if shortages bite. | Executives and analysts at the World Copper Conference warn that copper demand for electrification will outpace supply, with mine permitting approvals at the lowest level in a decade. They argue recycling won’t close the gap, implying future tightness and price pressure for copper, and possible substitution into aluminum if shortages emerge.

SMM reports aluminium prices near recent highs, supported by expectations of output cuts and declining ingot inventories. Scrap prices rose sharply, keeping secondary alloy production costs high, and ADC12 followed. However, automobile demand stayed weak and transactions were low, so prices are expected to fluctuate narrowly in the near term. | Copper slid on Friday and was set for a weekly drop as U.S. data raised recession fears, clouding demand. Three-month LME copper and May SHFE copper both fell. The stronger dollar also weighed on price affordability. LME aluminium was slightly higher while nickel declined. | Copper fell Friday and is set for a weekly drop as U.S. data sparked recession concerns, weakening outlook for metal demand. The stronger dollar added additional headwind for dollar-priced copper. The piece also notes softer performance in nickel and aluminium contracts alongside copper. | Copper prices edged lower on Friday and were set for weekly losses as recession fears, soft U.S. data, and sluggish Chinese demand pressured the outlook. Rising China refined production and an over-one-year-low Yangshan premium signaled weaker import appetite. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same risk-off backdrop. | Japan’s aluminium buyers agreed to higher April-June premiums of about $125–$130 per tonne, up from $85–$86 in the prior quarter and the first quarterly increase in six. The rise reflects higher overseas prices and tighter supply, reinforcing the regional premium benchmark tied to LME cash. | Aluminium Deutschland warns that supply of high-quality post-consumer recyclate for packaging is lagging demand, as EU regulation moves toward higher recycled-content requirements. It notes limited availability of suitable recycled materials, high costs, and production adjustments. The shortage is positioned as supportive for aluminium packaging-related demand. | The LME appointed an interim chairman amid fallout from its 2022 nickel trading suspension, FCA scrutiny, and lawsuits over canceled trades. Industry sources cited uncertainty. The exchange says it will oversee the market as nickel volumes slump and daily price limits remain for nickel, copper, and aluminium trading.

SMM reports the weighted alumina index at 2,898 yuan/mt, down 15 yuan/mt on the week, with declines across multiple Chinese provinces. Refineries in south China face stronger selling pressure than those in the north amid persistent alumina oversupply. SMM expects alumina prices to keep trending down in the short term. | SMM reports A00 aluminium rising to a four-month high before a slight retreat, while aluminium scrap and extrusion scrap also increased. Higher scrap prices encouraged more selling, but downstream restocking stayed limited due to weak demand and poor profitability. Secondary alloys like ADC12 followed with smaller gains and limited upside momentum. | Overseas alumina prices fell but are expected to have limited downside as Alcoa extended output cuts at its Kwinana refinery. The 2.19 Mt/yr plant will keep reductions due to prolonged mine review and approvals. Prior cuts of 438,000 t/yr occurred from natural gas shortages. | SMM reports alumina purchases by southwest China aluminium smelters falling 20 yuan/mt to 2,800 yuan/mt as buyers gained bargaining power. North China refineries run at low rates, limiting finished inventory. In Yunnan, capacity has been curtailed due to power rationing, with potential further cuts and local refinery sell-off pressuring spot prices. | SMM reports that alumina prices fell, cutting smelters’ real-time full costs to 16,888 yuan/mt. Meanwhile, SMM A00 aluminium prices rose 340 yuan/mt to 19,030 yuan/mt, expanding smelters’ profits by 363 yuan/mt to 2,142 yuan/mt. Costs are expected to drop slowly amid a bearish raw-material outlook. | Copper prices dropped to a more than two-week low as markets focused on weak demand in China, with manufacturing activity growing slower than expected and inventory overhang weighing on purchases. The article also notes aluminium and nickel easing, while a softer dollar limited losses overall. | SMM notes continued weakness in crude oil futures and a rebound in the US dollar index. By intraday close, SHFE prices fell across base metals: nickel -3.24%, aluminium -0.92%, and copper -0.78% (with others also down). The macro backdrop is bearish for metals near term. | Copper prices fell to the lowest in over two weeks as markets focused on lacklustre demand in top consumer China and slower-than-expected manufacturing activity after COVID restrictions eased. The Yangshan premium weakened further. Aluminium also slid, amid LME warrant cancellation at Gwangyang tied to a build-up of Russian metal.

Benchmark LME copper edged up on a softer U.S. dollar, but traders stayed cautious amid concerns over China’s lacklustre demand and uncertainty ahead of the Fed meeting. Meanwhile, LME aluminium fell and nickel dropped more sharply, highlighting mixed momentum across industrial metals. | Vedanta Aluminium unveiled a new material recovery facility at its Jharsuguda site to support a circular economy by improving recovery of materials for reuse/recycling. The project is expected to increase the availability of recycled inputs over time, which may modestly soften near-term aluminium price expectations. | Copper prices were subdued in Asian trade amid concerns over weak China demand and caution ahead of upcoming U.S. economic data and the Fed meeting. A softer U.S. dollar offered some support. LME aluminium and nickel fell on the day, while Shanghai showed mixed moves, reflecting choppy near-term sentiment. | Century Aluminum Company announced plans to acquire a 55% interest in the Jamalco alumina refinery. Because alumina is the primary feedstock for aluminium smelting, the deal can strengthen expectations around aluminium refining and supply continuity, supporting near-term price sentiment for aluminium over the coming weeks. | Century Aluminum agreed to buy Noble’s 55% interest in Jamalco, a Jamaica bauxite mining and alumina production joint venture. The acquisition aims to secure predictable alumina supply for its smelting operations, improving integration and supply-chain control. Transaction was expected to close by end-April 2023.

The article criticizes the European Commission for excluding aluminium from the EU’s Strategic/critical raw materials listings, arguing it sends the wrong signal and risks further erosion of European aluminium capacity. It cites rising demand, high power prices that have idled output, and calls for policy tools including lower electricity costs and PPAs. | EGA and Recapp plan to boost aluminium recycling in schools and universities, aiming to increase the collection and reuse of aluminium scrap via education-linked programs. More recycled material availability can support aluminium feedstock supply and improve sentiment toward aluminium prices over the coming weeks. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) partners with Recapp by Veolia to install aluminium recycling drop-off points in UAE schools and universities and promote greater collection of scrap. EGA also founded an Aluminium Recycling Coalition and plans a large recycling facility, supporting increased secondary aluminium supply. | LME copper rose 0.7% after a weaker dollar supported prices, but weak Chinese demand and global growth worries capped gains; LME on-warrant stocks increased to a six-week high. Aluminium also edged up despite warehouse inflows, while nickel rose despite a surplus expected to widen this year. | Norsk Hydro signed with Porsche to supply low-carbon aluminium starting this year, marketed under the REDUXA brand from Norwegian smelters powered by renewables. The deal targets a roughly 60% lower CO2 footprint versus Europe’s average primary aluminium and supports Porsche’s net carbon goals, potentially boosting demand for green aluminium.

Copper prices fell as investors weighed worries about higher U.S. rates, a weaker-than-expected demand rebound from China, and risk-off sentiment linked to banking sector troubles. It cites softer Chinese profit trends and holiday-related demand weakness. LME aluminium and nickel also edged down modestly. | Glencore agreed to buy 30% of Alunorte SA and 45% of Mineracao Rio do Norte from Norsk Hydro for $775 million, gaining long-term offtake rights. Alunorte refines alumina and MRN mines bauxite in Brazil, giving Glencore exposure to low-quartile carbon alumina. Regulatory approval is required and completion is in H2. | Glencore and Hydro said Glencore will buy 30% of the Alunorte alumina refinery and acquire 45% stakes in bauxite producer Mineracao Rio do Norte, in a $1.1 billion deal. Hydro will buy 40% of MRN from Vale and sell it to Glencore. The transaction boosts aluminum feedstock access. | Vale agreed to sell its 40% stake in Brazil’s Mineração Rio do Norte SA, the country’s biggest bauxite producer and exporter, to Norsk Hydro’s unit Ananke Alumina for an undisclosed amount, subject to regulatory approval. The divestment is framed as portfolio simplification with limited detail on production impacts. | Copper prices fell to a six-week low as weak Chinese demand signals (falling industrial profits, reduced output for Q2 peak season) and recession fears in Western economies weighed on industrial metals. It also flags holiday-driven demand softness and notes LME aluminium also dropped while nickel rose slightly. | Copper prices rebounded from a near four-month low after U.S. inflation data strengthened the dollar and weighed on demand concerns, especially in China. Despite early pressure, risk-on sentiment from U.S. stocks supported buying. Aluminium edged slightly lower, while nickel rose. | Copper prices rebounded from a four-month low as U.S. economic data boosted the dollar but later gains in U.S. stocks and risk appetite encouraged buying. The piece cites fragile China industrial demand, with firms cutting output into the May holiday. LME aluminum edged lower while nickel rose.

Egyptian Metal Construction Company plans to spend about $2.7 billion to build a 600,000 mt/year aluminium smelter to serve local demand and exports. The firm is in discussions with potential partners and studying locations in Port Said and Egypt’s Red Sea economic zone, but no near-term commissioning date is given. | Japanese buyers agreed a higher primary aluminium premium for the April–June quarter at $125–130/ton, up 45–53% versus the previous quarter, with other parts of Asia also rising. Japan’s premiums are a regional standard over LME cash, reflecting stronger near-term aluminium physical pricing. | Norsk Hydro reported Q1 core profit falling less than expected. The company attributed weaker results to aluminium price declines this year, driven by China boosting production and concerns about global economic slowdown. Hydro expects the aluminium market to be largely balanced in 2023, though risks remain around potential curtailments and supply disruptions. | Indonesia will allow Freeport Indonesia and Amman Mineral Nusa Tenggara to export copper concentrate until May 2024, despite a June export ban on raw minerals. The government cites delays in smelter construction and expects completion next year. It hints there will likely be no leniency for bauxite exports, affecting aluminium raw-material sentiment. | Glencore is expanding its industrial aluminium exposure, including deals across bauxite and alumina assets. The piece also notes its major Rusal/EN+ primary aluminium supply agreement ends in 2024 amid tighter Russia-biz pressure. These developments can affect near-term expectations for aluminium supply and availability in trading markets. | Copper prices steadied on the LME after China pledged to boost growth, but weak demand and recession/banking-stress fears capped gains. The contract faces its biggest monthly loss since June. SHFE copper inventories fell sharply, while aluminium and nickel posted smaller gains and losses respectively. | Stellantis signed a binding offtake agreement with Alliance Nickel for about 170,000 tons of battery-grade nickel and 12,000 tons of cobalt sulphate over five years, roughly 40% of NiWest forecast output. Nickel sulphate pricing is linked to index prices, reinforcing near-term nickel demand expectations. | Copper prices steadied on Friday after China pledged growth support, but gains were capped by weak demand signals and recession/banking stress fears. Three-month copper was slightly higher yet set for its biggest monthly loss since June. Aluminium rose 1.8%, reflecting a mixed move across industrial metals.

A study by Alumobility promotes aluminum-intensive electric/battery-electric last-mile delivery vehicles as lighter, cheaper to operate, and lower-carbon than steel ICE options. It claims aluminum reduces vehicle mass and battery requirements and lowers cost per mile, aligning with US/EU sustainability initiatives. The message implies supportive incremental demand for aluminum.

The report says Iran’s aluminum ingot production is continuing to rise. Increased output from a major regional producer can affect near-term expectations for aluminum supply and availability, potentially moving prices as market participants adjust for changes in production volumes and expected flows in the coming days to weeks. | InZinc Mining outlines a fully funded 2023 exploration program at its Indy project, following 2022 drilling that intersected nickel-cobalt-copper and minor copper in oxide material, plus new nickel-cobalt-copper targets and zinc targets. The article is about exploration groundwork and drilling timing, not immediate production effects. | Copper held steady on the LME as investors awaited the Fed’s rate decision, balancing nervous macro sentiment against a tight longer-term supply outlook. On-warrant LME stocks rose to the highest in nearly four months. Aluminium slipped and nickel rose modestly amid broader commodity pressure from tighter policy fears. | Copper is expected to drift higher but remain rangebound amid weaker-than-expected China demand and falling LME stocks, alongside mine-output disruption risks trimming surplus estimates. Aluminium faces softer sentiment from increased output and rising inventories in China. Nickel is pressured by concerns over rising Indonesian supply and broad nickel-bearing exports. | A Reuters poll expects copper to recover slightly but remain rangebound, with low inventories supporting prices while weak China physical demand and recession fears cap gains; aluminium is pressured by higher output and rising inventories; nickel is bearish on concerns of expanding Indonesian supply and growing market surpluses.

SMM’s regional review for China spot aluminium shows modest East China transactions, with A00 premiums moving from a small premium to discounts. In Gongyi, weak consumption was offset by narrower discounts as SHFE aluminium prices declined. South China saw restocking as prices fell, and premiums narrowed against SHFE 2305. | Maintenance workers at Tomago Aluminium, Australia’s largest aluminium smelter, are prepared to strike if wage and conditions negotiations deteriorate further. Unions rejected a final offer and cite cost-of-living pressure. Any industrial action could disrupt smelter operations, tightening near-term aluminium supply expectations. | Maintenance workers at Tomago Aluminium, Australia’s largest aluminium smelter, are prepared to strike if wage and conditions negotiations fail. The unions rejected a final offer, citing cost-of-living pressures. Any industrial action could disrupt smelter operations and tighten aluminium supply expectations in the near term. | Maintenance workers at Tomago Aluminium’s smelter say they are prepared to strike after wage and conditions talks deteriorated. Tomago produces up to 600,000 tonnes a year. With protected industrial action possible, disruption risk to aluminium output could affect near-term supply expectations and prices. | Maintenance workers at Tomago Aluminium’s smelter are preparing for a potential strike after wage and conditions negotiations deteriorated. The plant is Australia’s largest aluminium smelter producing up to 600,000 tonnes a year. Industrial action risk could disrupt output and influence near-term aluminium supply expectations. | Maintenance staff at Tomago Aluminium, Australia’s largest aluminium smelter, are prepared to strike after wage and conditions talks deteriorated. Tomago produces up to 600,000 tonnes annually. The unions cite cost-of-living pressure and rejected the final offer, raising risk of supply disruption. | Maintenance workers at Tomago Aluminium, Australia’s largest smelter, are preparing a strike if wage and conditions negotiations fail. The unions rejected a final offer, raising disruption risk at the ~600,000 tonnes/year facility. Any stoppage would likely reduce near-term aluminium supply expectations. | Maintenance staff at Tomago Aluminium, Australia’s largest aluminium smelter, are preparing for a possible strike after wage negotiations deteriorated. Unions cited cost-of-living pressures and rejected the company’s final offer. The report warns of potential operational disruption, affecting near-term aluminium supply expectations. | Maintenance workers at Tomago Aluminium’s smelter are preparing to strike after wage and conditions negotiations deteriorated. Tomago, Australia’s largest aluminium producer, could face operational disruption if industrial action proceeds. The dispute is linked to cost-of-living pressures and rejected company offers, potentially affecting short-term aluminium supply sentiment. | Maintenance staff at Tomago Aluminium, Australia’s largest smelter, are preparing for protected strike action if wage and conditions negotiations fail. Unions cite high cost-of-living and propose offers, while Tomago says it is negotiating in good faith and has measures to reduce disruption. Supply risk could pressure aluminium prices. | Maintenance workers at Tomago Aluminium’s smelter are preparing for a possible strike after wage and conditions negotiations broke down with unions rejecting the company’s final offer. Tomago is Australia’s largest aluminium smelter, so any industrial disruption could reduce near-term aluminium production expectations and weigh on prices. | Maintenance workers at Tomago Aluminium are preparing a possible strike after wage and conditions talks deteriorated. Tomago, Australia’s largest aluminium smelter, produces up to 600,000 tonnes annually. Unions rejected a final offer, raising risk of operational disruption, which could affect near-term aluminium supply expectations. | Maintenance workers at Tomago Aluminium, Australia’s largest aluminium smelter, are prepared to strike if wage and conditions negotiations deteriorate. Unions rejected the latest offer, while the company says it is negotiating in good faith and plans measures to reduce disruption. Industrial action risk could affect near-term aluminium supply. | Iran’s alumina powder production reportedly declined by 2% over the past year. Because alumina is the main feedstock for aluminium smelting, weaker output could modestly tighten supply expectations. However, the change is relatively small, implying limited near-term impact on aluminium prices. | Copper prices rose after the dollar weakened, but gains were capped by weaker-than-expected China demand signals, including contracting factory activity. Analysts highlighted reduced bullish positioning and bearish forecasts. Aluminium was nearly unchanged, while nickel rose modestly, reflecting limited broader momentum beyond copper. | SMM notes a rebound in industrial metals alongside weaker oil and a steady dollar. SHFE nickel jumped 2.85%, with SHFE copper up slightly and SHFE aluminium down marginally. LME copper gained 1.26% and LME nickel rose 0.65%, while LME aluminium was slightly higher, suggesting mild bullish bias. | Kaiser Aluminum’s earnings call highlighted better-than-expected Q1 results supported by continued pricing strength and favorable demand in parts of its end markets. Management also noted margin drag from higher metal input costs and inventory imbalance and discusses aluminum pass-through timing with packaging customers. Steel cost inflation affected a roll-coat project. | Fortune Nickel and Gold provides an update on exploration drilling plans for its Gowan Township project in Ontario, including recommending and collecting a helicopter-borne airborne TDEM survey to refine targets for future drilling. The company cites nickel prospects and does not report resource or production changes. | Maintenance staff at Tomago Aluminium, Australia’s largest smelter, are preparing for a potential strike if wage and conditions negotiations fail. Unions say the latest offer is below inflation, while Tomago says it has plans to minimize disruption. The outcome could affect near-term aluminium supply expectations.

Citi Research cut its 2023 and 2024 forecasts for nickel and aluminium, indicating weaker expectations for those markets. It simultaneously raised its copper price forecasts for 2023-2025, implying improved outlook. Overall, the revisions point to mixed metal fundamentals rather than a single immediate directional move. | Citi Research cut its 2023-2024 price forecasts for nickel and aluminium, lowering nickel and multiple aluminium annual averages. It simultaneously raised its copper forecasts for 2023-2025. The update may affect market sentiment, but it is a projection change rather than a direct near-term supply or demand disruption. | Citi Research lowered its 2023-2024 forecasts for nickel and aluminium, reducing nickel and aluminium price expectations for 2023-2024. It simultaneously raised copper forecasts for 2023-2025, increasing expected copper prices. The note is about research revisions rather than immediate supply-demand shocks. | SMM notes crude oil prices gained over 1% and the U.S. dollar index edged lower. In the reported session, LME metals rose across the board: copper +0.68%, nickel +0.65%, and aluminium +0.52%. SHFE showed mixed moves, with nickel sharply lower and copper/aluminium slightly down.

China imported 407,294 tonnes of unwrought copper and copper products in April, down 12.5% versus a year earlier, according to customs data. The report also notes aluminium exports of 461,782 tonnes. The copper import decline points to softer demand that can pressure copper prices in the near term. | March operating rates for aluminium smelters in key Chinese provinces were high. By April 20, 930,000 mt of capacity was resumed and 212,000 mt newly commissioned, nearly offsetting earlier cuts. Yunnan smelters could restart after power shortages ease, with further resumption/new capacity later this year, likely weighing on prices. | Mercedes-Benz says it has passed validation tests and will integrate low-CO2 aluminium into large-scale production this year. Supplied by Hydro, the alloy includes at least 25% recycled content and cuts carbon footprint by about 70% versus the European average. Mercedes plans use in multiple EV models and structural parts. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) said it received Aluminium Stewardship Initiative (ASI) updated Chain of Custody Standard V2 certification for its smelter and calciner/marine plant. The voluntary certification enables ASI-certified aluminium traceability across the value chain. It reflects responsible sourcing commitments, without indicating immediate supply changes or price impact. | Copper steadied but sentiment weakened as China data pointed to softer growth and demand, with April imports down 12.5% and manufacturing activity slipping. LME-approved copper inventories rose 40% since April 18 and a firmer dollar weighed on prices. Nickel fell on rising supplies; aluminium edged up modestly. | Alcoa, the aluminium smelter operator in Portland, Victoria, admitted euthanising 152 koalas after they showed illness from fluoride exposure tied to smelting waste. The company implemented a Koala Management Plan including closing an affected area and planting 16,000 eucalyptus trees, though no production disruption is reported.

Copper prices rose on a weaker U.S. dollar ahead of U.S. inflation data, but gains were limited by expectations of subdued demand in Q2 and rising supply. Peru’s March copper output jumped 20.4% after mine stoppages. Aluminium and nickel were roughly steady to mixed as traders waited for CPI. | Century Aluminum’s CEO said supply-side headwinds are expected to keep shrinking global aluminium inventories in the short term. The report adds that regional premiums and LME aluminium prices should respond quickly as demand picks up, indicating potential upside pressure to aluminium prices over coming days. | TUBEX launched a mono-material aluminium refill tube made largely from 100% recycled aluminium and high post-consumer recycled content, designed for easy closed-loop recycling. It highlights energy savings from recycling and growing interest from brands, while industry groups note concerns about PCR material availability and transition costs. | Century Aluminum’s earnings call highlights stronger aluminum market conditions in Q1: rising LME prices and regional premiums, driven by additional European and Chinese smelter curtailments and reduced supply. Inventories are below 50 days of consumption, while US energy costs fell. The company expects modestly higher Q2 pricing and EBITDA. | Century Aluminum’s Q1 call reports aluminum market improvement: LME prices and regional premiums increased, supported by European and China supply curtailments and easing input costs (notably power). The company also highlights low global inventories and continued curtailment risk, alongside an acquisition of Jamalco alumina/bauxite to secure feedstock.

Alcoa said it reached a tentative three-year labor agreement with the United Steelworkers for workers at its Warrick (Indiana) and Massena (New York) aluminum smelters. The pact covers about 860 employees and will be voted on by union members, with the prior contract expiring May 15, 2023. | Pan Global said it has secured surface access to begin drilling immediately at the Romana West target within its Escacena project in Spain, aiming to test an on-strike extension of the La Romana copper-tin-silver discovery. Two rigs will mobilize and 25–30 holes are planned, with results pending. | Credit Suisse upgraded Alcoa, saying the stock could gain over 35% as LME aluminum prices rebound. The note cites recovering global demand, supportive U.S./EU physical premiums, improving Chinese outlook, and supply dynamics tied to hydro challenges and an expected Spain smelter restart, despite noted risks.

Macro sentiment turned bearish, driven by renewed expectations of US rate hikes, banking crisis worries, and debt-ceiling negotiations. SMM reports aluminium ingot inventories falling, but downstream operating rates remain mediocre and billet inventories rise. Smelter output and operating rates increased in April, with further growth expected in May. | China’s aluminium billet social inventory reached 168,600 mt as of May 11, up 10,900 mt from a week earlier. SMM attributes the build to continued cargo arrivals, weak downstream demand, and more molten aluminium being converted into billets. With extrusion moving into off-season, billet inventories face further accumulation risk. | SMM reports China aluminium ingot social inventories at 787,000 mt as of May 11, down 48,000 mt week-on-week and 216,000 mt year-on-year. Destocking remains fast and stocks are near five-year lows. Lower premium levels and fading arrivals are driving withdrawals, suggesting inventories stay low near term. | SMM reports SHFE 2306 aluminium ended down 1.21% and LME aluminium fell 2.49%. It notes improving conversion of molten aluminium into billets could affect end-demand transmission to inventories. If demand remains weak, smelters may shift to more ingot output, but low ingot inventories may limit downside. Watches macro and Yunnan supply. | Copper was up on Friday but set for its biggest weekly drop since November as weak global economic data darkened demand expectations and rising supplies pressured prices. LME copper inventories rose toward a two-month high and a stronger dollar hurt sentiment, weighing on the broader metals complex including nickel and aluminium. | Copper hit the lowest level in over five months as fears of slowing demand in China, rising LME warehouse inventories, and a stronger U.S. dollar pressured prices. The sell-off was reinforced by recent Chinese inflation data. Aluminium was slightly higher on the day, while nickel’s gains reflected short-covering amid expected surpluses. | Norilsk Nickel said Rotterdam has allowed it to continue selling nickel through its transshipment hub after earlier disruptions. The company is also using the Tangier port in Morocco for product logistics. Continued nickel deliveries point to easing supply constraints, likely supporting nickel prices in the coming days.

Benchmark London copper prices fell in Asian trading as the U.S. dollar firmed to a five-week high and investors worried about weaker China demand ahead of industrial activity data. Rising inventories and a slowdown in global growth pressured sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | Shandong Nanshan’s Bintan SEZ alumina plant is running at full capacity, using Indonesian bauxite, and the company plans to build a 250,000 tpy aluminium smelter later this year, targeting a 1 million tpy aluminium plant by 2028. The SEZ also benefits from long tax waivers and renewable power plans. | SMM reports one 1 mt/y alumina refinery in Inner Mongolia fully closed in early May after halting in April, while three new alumina projects totaling 3.4 mt/y begin operating in May, boosting Q2 supply. Aluminium capacity in Yunnan remains curtailed. Alumina costs eased, but alumina prices kept falling and profit margins shrank. | SMM reports worsening macro conditions and bearish fundamentals for aluminium. Despite declining Chinese social inventories, demand is viewed as pessimistic, downstream operating rates are unlikely to improve, and domestic operating capacity is expected to rise above 40.9 Mt. Falling anode and captive power costs add pressure. SHFE and LME aluminium seen with downside. | SMM reports that aluminium smelter costs fell sharply last week as coal prices dropped and ample captive power reduced electricity costs. Prebaked anode prices also declined, lowering real-time full smelter cost to about 16,383 yuan/mt and boosting profits. Aluminium A00 prices eased to 17,890 yuan/mt, but margins improved. | SMM reports that alumina and coal prices remain unstable, implying aluminium production costs still have room to fall in the short term. Although some smelters are already experiencing small losses due to weaker aluminium prices and relatively high electricity costs, the ongoing input cost weakness points to continued downward pressure on aluminium. | Copper rebounded after a five-month low as China’s PBOC injected liquidity, signaling support for economic growth and improving base-metal sentiment. The weaker dollar and “bargain hunting” aided prices. Aluminium rose on lower LME on-warrant stocks, while nickel weakened on vulnerability concerns and forecast cuts. | Emerita Resources reported assay results from 21 drill holes at its Iberian Belt West La Infanta deposit, highlighting polymetallic massive sulphide intervals including ~1.4% copper over 4.2m in one hole. The company also received a permit for Infanta Sur to start exploration. No nickel or aluminium impact is indicated. | Copper prices rose about 1% on Monday after China’s central bank injected liquidity, supporting the Chinese economy and improving base-metal sentiment after a five-month low. A Tibet copper mine stopped production after an accident. The weaker U.S. dollar added support. Aluminium also climbed as LME on-warrant stocks fell. | Shandong Nanshang plans to invest $6bn in expanding aluminium operations in Indonesia’s Bintan SEZ, including building a 250,000 tpa aluminium smelter and targeting additional capacity by 2028. The project is supported by new power generation plans, potentially lifting regional aluminium supply expectations. | Technavio projects growth in the global aluminum flat-rolled products (FRP) market, with APAC driving most gains and demand tied to packaging (beverage cans/films), construction, and transportation. It cites green-building initiatives and mass transportation as drivers, but offers no immediate supply disruption or policy action. | Alcoa and Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) signed an 8-year, multi-year alumina supply agreement starting in 2024, with options up to 15.6 million tonnes from Western Australia. EGA will source smelter-grade alumina for its Abu Dhabi/Dubai smelters, with low-carbon EcoSource options, reducing near-term uncertainty on alumina availability. | Alcoa has agreed to an eight-year supply deal with Emirates Global Aluminium. Qianhai Mercantile Exchange plans to launch contracts for lithium carbonate, nickel sulphate, and possibly one other battery metal, alongside considering a low-carbon “green aluminium” physical contract in China. Copper prices edged higher off a 5-1/2-month low, but the drop was driven by concerns over sluggish Chinese demand. Lindian Resources signed a six-year Guinea bauxite supply deal with C&D Logistics for 23 million wet tonnes from its Gaoual project, with annual renegotiation from 2025. UK Prime Minister Rishi Sunak announced an embargo on imports of Russian diamonds and also copper, aluminium and nickel. Reporting indicates additional restrictions on Russian diamond trade and sanctions targeting firms tied to Russia’s military-industrial sector. Such trade restrictions on Russian metal supply can tighten availability and provide upward price pressure for copper, aluminium, and nickel. | The UK Prime Minister is reported to have announced an embargo on imports of Russian diamonds, including copper, aluminium, and nickel. The measure is part of sanctions related to Russia’s actions in Ukraine, alongside asset freezes and individual/entity restrictions. The EU is also considering diamond trade restrictions. | Copper is set for a fifth straight weekly decline as the U.S. dollar stays near two-month highs and markets price in additional Fed rate hikes. Weak Chinese demand dampens sentiment. Aluminium and nickel show intraday gains, but the broader macro backdrop caps industrial metals, leaving weekly momentum bearish. | Ahead of the G7 Japan summit, the UK is set to unveil sanctions and export controls targeting Russia. Trade Data Monitor shows Britain imported little or no Russian copper, but did import unwrought aluminium and refined nickel from Russia in 2022-2023. Such curbs can reduce Russian metal flows and affect short-term pricing expectations. | Britain and other G7 states plan new sanctions and export controls against Russia. Factbox data shows UK Russian imports of refined copper were effectively absent in 2021 and tiny via 15,252 tonnes last year; aluminium imports from Russia fell versus 2021; refined nickel imports were much lower or absent in earlier periods. | The article outlines Britain’s recent imports of sanctioned Russian metals before new G7 sanctions and export controls are announced in Japan. Reduced Russian trade is likely to tighten supply and support prices. | The UK said it will announce new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel alongside fresh G7 sanctions at a summit in Japan. Trade data show the UK imported limited Russian volumes—none for refined copper, none early-2023 for aluminium and nickel—while refined nickel and unwrought aluminium imports occurred in 2022. | The UK plans new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel, alongside new G7 sanctions targeting Russia over the war in Ukraine. Britain imported no Russian refined copper in recent years, while it bought unwrought aluminium and some refined nickel from Russia in 2022. SMM says overseas alumina prices fell $5/mt to $340/mt FOB Western Australia last week, or about 2,974 yuan/mt CIF major Chinese ports after freight. Congo plans to boost its stake and control in the Sicomines infrastructure-for-minerals deal with China, seeking higher equity for Gecamines, a larger infrastructure loan envelope, and lump-sum compensation over alleged undervaluation of copper and cobalt sales. Negotiations with Chinese firms are expected to intensify after Tshisekedi returns from China.

Tomago Aluminium workers rejected a management pay offer of 11% over two years, with union leaders calling for renewed bargaining and highlighting inflation-driven pay erosion. Tomago can produce up to 600,000 tonnes annually, so labour disruption risk could weigh on aluminium prices. | Tomago Aluminium workers rejected a proposed 11% pay increase over two years, with the union arguing wages haven’t kept up with inflation. China’s exports of unwrought aluminium alloy fell to 16,400 mt in April, down 35.2% YoY and 16.5% MoM. January–April exports rose, but April was weaker. SMM reports mixed LME/SHFE performance after a U.S. debt-ceiling agreement boosted risk appetite and weakened the dollar, but inflation and expected Fed hikes limit upside. Indonesian copper miner Amman Mineral’s $880m IPO will finance debt repayment and projects, including completing a copper smelter in Sumbawa with planned cathode output. Base metals mostly fell: SHFE nickel plunged 4.81%, SHFE copper was slightly down, and SHFE aluminium rose modestly. Rio Tinto’s Pacific Aluminium subsidiary reported 2022 results tied to a global aluminium price surge in the first half of 2022 after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and higher energy costs. The revision may affect market sentiment, but it is largely a medium-term view rather than an immediate shock. | Rio Tinto’s Pacific Aluminium reports 2022 results for New Zealand Aluminium Smelters, stating profits were driven by a first-half 2022 spike to record-high aluminium prices after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and higher energy costs. SHFE aluminium jumped 2.04% and SHFE copper gained 1.4%, while SHFE nickel slipped 0.17% by end of intraday.
