It cites declining primary aluminium demand short-term and returning Chinese supply that worsens the global primary balance. The company also notes European smelter margin declines amid low LME and high energy prices. | Hydro reported Q2 earnings roughly in line with forecasts and raised 2023 capex guidance to 20.5 billion crowns from 16.5 billion, citing inflation/currency effects and additional funding for its planned Alumetal acquisition. It also wrote to the LME to reconsider a decision not to ban Russian aluminium from its warehouse network. | Norwegian aluminium producer Hydro raised its 2023 capital expenditure guidance and reported Q2 core earnings in line with analyst forecasts. Adjusted EBITDA fell year-on-year to 7.1 billion crowns but matched expectations, suggesting ongoing operational stability while capex plans point to continued investment supporting near-term aluminium supply outlook. | Copper and most industrial metals eased after investors were disappointed by China’s modest stimulus aimed at boosting consumption. Reuters reports LME copper down about 0.3% and aluminium down 0.4%, with weekly losses extending. Commentary cited weaker-than-expected activity signals and possible rising supply risk. | Copper and most industrial metal prices edged lower as investors were disappointed by modest Chinese stimulus aimed at consumption. Three-month copper fell to about $8,459/t, with limited Chinese downstream demand and slightly easing spot tightness. Aluminium also dropped on the week, alongside broader base-metal weakness and supply expectations. | Rio Tinto agreed to form a joint venture with Giampaolo Group by buying a 50% stake in Matalco for about USD700 million. The venture will manufacture and market recycled aluminium billet and slab products, leveraging six US and one Canada facilities with ~900,000 tonnes/year capacity and low-carbon positioning.

South32 said 2Q/4Q production rose, with copper up 12% and aluminum up 3% quarter-on-quarter, suggesting stronger output. At the same time, it cut its 2024 guidance for its Brazilian aluminum business to 100,000 tons after a slower-than-expected restart tied to equipment availability, potentially tightening aluminum supply. | London copper rose 0.2% as investors expected China’s Politburo and broader stimulus to revive growth and boost industrial demand, following disappointment with smaller measures. Near-term demand was still soft, with producer operating rates and import appetite weak. LME aluminium and nickel also edged higher. | Copper fell in London after China’s leaders said the economy faces new challenges and a “tortuous” recovery. Analysts cited worries over slowing Chinese demand, with the Yangshan copper premium at a two-month low. LME aluminium also declined alongside the broader industrial metals sell-off and a firmer U.S. dollar. | Copper prices were little changed in London as China pledged additional policy adjustments to support growth after a weak post-COVID recovery. Analysts cited concerns about slower Chinese demand as a drag. LME copper was flat on the day, while aluminium was down and nickel slightly higher, reflecting mixed sentiment.

Vietnam’s government authorises bauxite projects and sets a 2021-2030 master plan requiring exploration, extraction and intensive processing. It encourages red clay recycling, environmental safeguards, and electrolysis projects using market-based electricity pricing for renewable power. The policy may support future aluminium supply, but near-term price impact appears limited. | Midland Exploration and Probe Gold resumed drilling on the La Peltrie option, targeting a copper-gold-silver-molybdenum discovery. The company plans eight holes to test IP/VTEM anomalies and reports intervals with copper and silver grades. It is exploration activity with no stated production impact or policy change. | Copper prices rose on London trading after China’s top leaders pledged more supportive policy measures to boost domestic demand, especially in property. The expectation improved sentiment and strengthened the yuan, aiding dollar-priced metals. LME aluminium and nickel also increased on the day alongside copper. | Kaiser Aluminum reported Q2 2023 results, including changes in shipments and lower average selling prices versus the prior year. The company’s “hedged cost of alloyed metal” references aluminum cost using the Midwest Transaction Price, while commentary highlights improving aerospace demand and ongoing packaging destocking.

London copper eased 0.5% from a one-week high ahead of the U.S. Fed decision, with a stronger dollar pressuring dollar-priced metal. LME aluminium also slipped, while nickel fell slightly. China demand-support expectations earlier helped prices, but traders remain focused on rate-path clues. | Citi recommends accumulating copper, arguing decarbonisation demand and constrained physical supply could support prices over the next six to nine months. Morgan Stanley expects rising global refined nickel supply (up to 25%) to lift LME inventories and pressure nickel lower. The piece offers a bearish outlook for iron ore, not aluminium. | Copper, aluminium and nickel prices in London fell as China’s stimulus promise lacked specific measures, dimming hopes for demand growth. Traders also turned cautious ahead of the widely expected U.S. Federal Reserve rate decision. Three-month copper dropped ~1% and nickel fell ~2.5%. | China’s aluminum imports jumped in 2023 H1 as domestic supply fell below demand. Customs data show a 10.7% year-on-year increase to 1.2 million tons, including higher June volumes. Output constraints in power-cut regions and rising bauxite and Russian aluminum imports point to tighter near-term availability.

Copper prices rose Thursday as a weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced metals cheaper, alongside expectations the Fed’s rate-hike cycle is near its end. Markets also looked for Chinese property-sector stimulus, though China’s weak data tempered gains. Aluminium and nickel prices increased as part of the broader metals uptick. | Jamestrong and UNSW’s SMaRT Centre will commercialise a MICROfactorie recycling technology to recover aluminium from mixed, previously non-recyclable waste and use it in aerosol can production. The project includes an $8 million aluminium casting line in Taree, expected to complete by mid-2024, expanding capacity and reshoring slug supply. | Rio Tinto warned Australia’s carbon emissions scheme and insufficient renewable power make aluminium refining uneconomic, citing a $1.7bn write-off and $1.2bn loss linked to abatement costs. The tightening decarbonisation requirements and costly clean-tech investment threaten future capacity, creating bearish sentiment for aluminium supply and pricing. | Antilles Gold confirmed additional assay results at the El Pilar copper deposit in Cuba, including long intercepts with notable copper grades and shallow gold mineralisation. The company is progressing mine planning and prefeasibility for a joint venture open-pit “Nueva Sabana” operation, with ongoing metallurgy and negotiations for concentrate sales. | Analysts cut copper, aluminium, and nickel forecasts as supply ramps up. Copper faces a soft patch with mine output rising and a stated surplus widening. Aluminium prices likely face pressure as Yunnan smelters restart after hydropower-related curbs ease. Nickel drops amid stronger Indonesian production and higher-grade conversion. | Reliance Steel & Aluminum reported Q2 2023 results and expects Q3 tons down due to seasonal factors and one less shipping day. It also forecasts pricing decline and “temporary downward pressure” on gross margins, driven by declining prices for flat-rolled products including common alloy aluminum. | Copper edged higher on hopes that U.S. rate hikes have peaked, helped by dollar weakness. However, gains were capped by weak China data and continued concerns about lacklustre demand, with lower Chinese industrial profits and softer import premiums. Aluminium and nickel also rose modestly despite demand worries.

Reuters reports pressure on the LME to reconsider accepting Russian aluminium in its warehouse system. Russian aluminium dominates warranted stock, while China’s Russian-brand imports are rising but recent data suggest a fading appetite and possible surplus as drought eases and production rebounds. Weaker demand and loose LME spreads imply bearish near-term pricing. | Chery launched China’s first aluminium-based lightweight S5E pure electric platform, emphasizing new aluminium alloy body materials, extrusion molding, and die-casting processes. The redesign is expected to improve safety, reduce manufacturing energy and emissions, and increase material utilization—implying incremental aluminium demand associated with new EV production. | Nigeria’s vice president says the government is re-engaging with RUSAL and other partners to restart operations at the idle ALSCON aluminium smelter. ALSCON has 193,000 tonnes annual capacity and is majority owned by RUSAL. A restart would add aluminium supply expectations, influencing nearby market sentiment. | Five European industry groups urged the LME to resist calls to ban Russian aluminium from its system. They argue restrictions would harm Europe’s aluminium value chain. The LME said it will follow government sanctions and monitor market orderliness; Russian aluminium share in LME inventories has risen sharply. | Reliance’s Q2 2023 call reports sequential weakness from falling stainless and common alloy aluminum prices, noting these products see downward pricing pressure that “follows the LME.” Management says aluminum commodity price declines pressured margins and expects average selling prices to decline further in Q3 due to declining flat-rolled commodity prices. | Rusal is in discussions with Nigeria about restarting the Alscon aluminium smelter in Nigeria. Alscon previously produced 22,000 tonnes before Rusal suspended operations in 2013 due to unreliable gas supply and low aluminium prices. Rusal confirmed talks but gave no details; it owns 85% of Alscon.

Reuters reports London copper is set for its strongest monthly gain since January, rising on hopes for further Chinese stimulus and expectations that the Fed will pause rate hikes. Softer inflation supports a weaker dollar. LME aluminium also rose, while nickel edged lower, reflecting overall bullish sentiment. | London three-month copper was up on prospects of further China stimulus and expectations that the Fed may pause rate hikes after cooling U.S. inflation. The dollar outlook turned less supportive. Chinese property support prospects were cited as improving demand expectations. LME aluminium rose, while nickel eased. | Copper prices climbed to a one-month high as traders priced in hopes for further China stimulus to support post-COVID demand. Weak Chinese manufacturing data and a higher U.S. dollar limited gains, while rising LME stocks and fewer cancelled warrants eased availability worries. Aluminium rose; nickel fell on the day’s broader sentiment.

SMM reports that global aluminium scrap production is set to expand strongly over coming decades, supported by carbon neutrality and circular economy policies. It highlights expected large increases in Europe and the US, with growth linked to EV/lightweighting and recycling-system buildouts, implying improved secondary aluminium supply potential. | SMM reports China’s aluminium scrap supply and demand have risen, helped by carbon neutrality and circular-economy policies. Secondary aluminium production—especially ADC12 and remelted billets—drives scrap demand. The imbalance appears to have reversed post-2020, and China may become a net exporter of scrap in the future. | Perseverance Metals entered an option agreement to acquire the Voyageur nickel-copper-cobalt-PGM project in Michigan, with commitments for exploration spend and financing over the next 18 months. The release highlights proximity to the Eagle nickel mine and drill-ready targets, but provides no near-term production or supply impact. | Copper prices fell in London after a strong July, as weak China economic data suggested poor power and construction demand. A firmer dollar weighed on commodity prices, while LME copper warehouse stocks and on-warrant inventories rose. The piece also reports softer aluminium and a nickel technical rebound, implying mixed effects across metals.

London copper gained as the dollar softened after Fitch cut the U.S. top credit rating, making dollar-priced metals cheaper. China’s weak data weighed on demand, but hopes for stimulus supported prices. LME copper rose 0.3%; LME aluminium dipped 0.2% and nickel edged up 0.1%. | Japanese buyers agreed with producers on a Q3 aluminium shipment premium of $127.5/mt for July-September, near the prior quarter and within the $125-$130 range. Sources cited sluggish local demand and ample inventories, contrasting with producers’ higher initial offers of $165-$180/mt, implying weaker near-term pricing pressure. | Copper prices eased as traders awaited concrete Chinese stimulus measures and weighed weak China demand signals. LME three-month copper fell 0.4%, SHFE copper dropped 1.3%. Aluminium and nickel also declined on the day, reflecting cautious positioning and uncertainty around China’s policy support timeline. | CNBC reports aluminum futures ended July with a 2.72% monthly gain, the best since January, marking a second straight monthly advance. It highlights JPMorgan’s constructive view on aluminum-related stocks, despite some near-term caution from China smelting capacity restarts and a seasonal lull. | Aluminum futures ended July with a 2.72% monthly gain, the best month since January and a second straight monthly advance. The piece cites JPMorgan’s constructive view on aluminum-related stocks despite near-term cautions, and lists equities that have shown moderate correlation with aluminum.

London copper rebounded 0.4% after a sell-off triggered by Fitch’s US credit downgrade, but gains were capped by a steady dollar. The demand outlook also worsened amid China weakness. LME aluminium rose modestly, while nickel was slightly higher on the day, indicating mixed macro-driven moves across metals. | DUBAL Holding signed an MoU with Oriental Copper to manufacture high-conductivity copper bus bars and other copper products in the UAE, targeting local import substitution. The planned unit has about 30,000 MT/year processing capacity and AED 150 million capex, which should support near-term copper demand for input material. | Dubal Holding, linked to Dubai’s government, agreed with Thailand’s Oriental Copper to build a UAE production unit for copper products, able to process up to 30,000 tonnes of copper annually. The project (about $40.8m) targets import substitution for fabricated copper items, supporting copper demand expectations near term.

Reuters reports London copper trading in a tight range ahead of the US nonfarm payrolls report, with prices almost flat on the week. It notes expectations of further China stimulus for property and infrastructure, plus power-sector orders supporting copper cable and wire output. Aluminium is marginally higher but not a focus. | SMM data show China’s eight major markets aluminium ingot social stocks fell to 525,000 mt as of Aug 3, down 11,000 mt on the week and at a five-year low. Lower-than-expected cargo arrivals and flooding/rail disruptions reduced supply, while demand improved slightly in South China. | Rio Tinto booked a $1.175 billion impairment on its Australian alumina refineries, citing challenging alumina markets and higher costs from decarbonising under Australia’s new carbon tax and Safeguard Mechanism carbon caps. Dependence on fossil-fuel grids adds ongoing cost pressure, weighing on aluminium economics and outlook. | The report focuses on the global aluminum cladding panels market, describing expected growth and attributing demand to rising construction activity and spending. It also notes Russia-Ukraine sanctions, supply disruptions, and commodity-price increases that drove inflation. Overall, the construction-demand narrative supports higher near-term aluminium consumption expectations. | Novelis’ CEO said the outlook for aluminum demand is improving as the economy firms. The company’s view suggests stronger expectations for aluminum consumption in the near term, which can feed into buying interest and support aluminum prices within the next few weeks. | The US Department of Energy added copper to its Critical Raw Materials Assessment, with the list used to determine eligibility for Inflation Reduction Act subsidies. The updated assessment also includes aluminium and nickel among other materials tied to energy-transition technologies, implying stronger policy-driven demand support for the metals. | Copper prices fell on worries that China’s demand outlook remains weak, citing PMIs in contractionary territory and limited details in recent growth-support announcements. U.S. jobs data slightly eased the dollar. The report also notes aluminium and nickel declines alongside copper, reflecting broader demand concerns.

Copper futures opened lower as a steady dollar and cautious positioning ahead of US and China economic data weighed on demand expectations. China spot premiums stayed tight, while supply was set to improve after smelter maintenance. Aluminium dipped slightly on LME/SHFE, and nickel fell on both exchanges. | WA’s EPA is considering an independent review of Alcoa’s bauxite mining in jarrah forests after public referrals and unresolved concerns over drinking-water impacts. Alcoa’s slower clearing has reduced bauxite supply, with Kwinana operating at reduced capacity due to bauxite shortages, tightening aluminium supply expectations. | Aurubis said it remains on track for its fiscal-year targets despite low sulfuric acid prices and weaker metal prices in some areas. In Q3 it posted €115m pre-tax operating profit on lower sales, helped by lower energy costs and about €15m flood-related insurance reimbursements. No new market-moving events were cited. | Tamil Nadu Newsprint & Papers Limited issued an e-tender to procure 1,000 meters of 3.5 C x 300 sq.mm aluminium LT power cable. The document details submission, technical bid evaluation, and e-auction pricing mechanics. It signals incremental aluminium demand but does not indicate broader market supply or price shocks. | Copper fell 0.4% as markets worried about a January-May surplus of 287,000 tonnes, rising LME-approved inventories, and muted Chinese demand prospects. The Yangshan premium dropped sharply. A firmer U.S. dollar also pressured industrial metals; aluminium fell slightly while nickel rose. | Producers report falling Q2 earnings and lower aluminum sales, while aluminum premiums in the U.S. and Europe remain bearish and manufacturing PMIs stay in contraction. Norsk Hydro urges the LME to reconsider admitting Russian aluminum, which represents a large share of LME stocks and trades at a discount, adding uncertainty but demand softness dominates. | A report warns Indiana steel and aluminum makers could lose auto business if they can’t produce low-carbon inputs, as automakers aim for carbon neutrality by 2050. It highlights costly infrastructure needs, potential limits on scrap and green hydrogen, and strong demand for greener materials that currently isn’t well supplied, supporting aluminum price strength.

Copper edged lower on the LME as the dollar strengthened ahead of U.S. and China economic data that could clarify demand. LME aluminium also fell. Nickel was modestly higher, highlighting mixed metal responses as investors awaited inflation and trade figures. | Copper prices waver as investors weigh weak July China trade data against hopes for further stimulus. Tight domestic inventories are offset by limited demand during summer lull and a stronger dollar. The article also notes small moves in LME aluminium and nickel, but the dominant focus is copper demand outlook. | China’s July copper imports were 451,159 tonnes, down 2.7% from a year earlier. The country’s exports of unwrought aluminium and aluminium products fell 24.9% to 489,738 tonnes in July. These trade-flow changes can influence short-term expectations, though the copper move is modest. | SMM reviews China aluminium rod markets: June output and operating rates were steady but demand weakened, keeping conversion margins under pressure and causing stock build. In late July, after aluminium prices surged, rigid demand boosted buying and conversion margins rebounded with upward adjustments, indicating near-term bullish pressure for aluminium-linked pricing. | The piece argues that tensions around the Strait of Hormuz and the Iran conflict have boosted energy prices and increased aluminium prices, citing a roughly 12% rise since escalation. It attributes the move to strategic shipping chokepoints affecting global transport and supply flows, implying continued bullish pressure. | The article promotes an aluminum casting market forecast, projecting growth driven by demand for lightweight vehicle components and aluminum die-casting for applications such as battery housings and sensor casings. It also notes China’s role as a top primary aluminum producer and consumer, linking steady casting demand to broader aluminium usage trends. | Copper prices fell to the weakest level in nearly a month after Chinese trade and auto sales data pointed to weaker demand. Reuters cited falling copper imports and risk aversion, alongside a firmer dollar and concern about further Fed rate hikes. Aluminium and nickel also dropped as sentiment deteriorated.

London copper rebounded 1% from a one-month low as the U.S. dollar eased, improving price competitiveness. The article links trading to China macro data weakening trade and reviving stimulus hopes, with mentions of low Chinese copper inventories. Aluminium and nickel also edged up amid mixed LME/SHFE moves. | Copper prices bounced from a one-month low after China’s deflation data fueled hopes for more stimulus in the world’s top metals consumer. As the dollar weakened and risk sentiment improved, copper rose on LME, while aluminium and nickel also edged up, reflecting supportive macro-driven demand expectations. | Century Aluminum reported Q2 profit falling year-on-year while beating analyst EPS expectations. Revenue dropped 32.8% to $575.5 million. The piece describes earnings performance only, without details on production cuts, inventory changes, or aluminium pricing, limiting near-term inference for aluminium futures. | NioCorp says Nebraska’s Senator Deb Fischer and Rep. Don Bacon secured legislation and $10m of U.S. federal funding for domestic aluminum-scandium (AlSc) master alloy production. The pilot-scale program is producing scandium metal and is on track for AlSc ingot production in coming weeks, supporting a U.S. supply chain.

Copper prices edged higher as investors bet China will introduce economic support measures after weak July data, including deflation and falling factory-gate prices. LME three-month copper rose slightly, while LME aluminium increased and nickel was nearly flat, though gains were limited by uncertainty on demand and stimulus details. | The piece argues China’s more supportive (though not large-stimulus) policy environment could lift demand for base and energy-transition metals, citing improved sentiment for copper and aluminium. Aluminium is described as having risen sharply in July, with record China output balanced against possible winter supply curtailments. | Romanian aluminium producer Alro reported a net loss in H1, attributing weaker turnover to falling LME aluminium prices and the closure of its Alum alumina refinery. Management said investments continue, including an eco-recycling facility to lower electricity use and production costs. Demand slowdown is also cited. | Technavio forecasts global aluminum market growth from 2022 to 2027, citing rising demand from electric vehicles and the broader transportation sector. It also notes increasing relevance of secondary (recycled) aluminum and regional growth, especially in APAC. Overall, the content implies supportive demand trends for aluminium prices. | London-traded three-month copper rose 0.8% as the dollar weakened ahead of U.S. inflation data and hopes for additional Chinese stimulus persisted. China’s deflation data and softer factory-gate prices strengthened expectations for policy support, boosting demand sentiment. Aluminium also rose, while nickel edged lower amid macro volatility. | Hindalco and Texmaco announced a strategic alliance to develop and manufacture aluminium rail wagons and coaches for Indian Railways. Hindalco will supply aluminium alloy profiles, sheets and plates, and provide fabrication/welding expertise, supporting rail capacity and net-zero goals. The plan implies incremental aluminium demand support for the near term. | Vietnam’s Ministry of Industry and Trade outlined plans to expand mining in Lang Son and Cao Bang and to upgrade existing mines in Lam Dong, aiming to increase bauxite ore capacity. It also explores mining 68–112.2 million tons of bauxite annually, leveraging Vietnam’s large bauxite reserves.

Rusal, one of the world’s largest aluminium producers, said first-half net profit fell 75% due to dwindling raw material resources, inflation and sanctions fallout raising production costs after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Higher cost of sales and weaker demand dynamics point to pressured aluminium margins. | Copper prices fell to the lowest since early July and were set for the biggest weekly decline in three months as China’s post-pandemic recovery looked sluggish. Weak lending/TSF data and property worries hurt demand expectations. Inventories rose on SHFE and LME, with nickel and aluminium also hitting multi-week lows. | Russian aluminum comprises about 80% of LME warranted stocks, with China—previously a major buyer—showing weakening appetite as domestic production rebounds. If Chinese demand falls, more Russian metal may flood the LME, leaving a largely Russian stock pool with limited non-Russian buyers and potential discounts. | India’s Nalco reported weaker Q1 profit as aluminium segment revenue fell with depressed market pricing. Analysts cited easing supply-chain conditions and persistent China–India aluminium price disparity. LME aluminium prices dropped nearly 40% in the June quarter year-on-year, while Nalco benefited only partially from lower power and fuel costs.

SMM reports SHFE Aug 2309 aluminium and LME aluminium fell in the prior session, but notes supportive factors this week: low domestic ingot inventories and improving downstream operating rates. Offsetting this, Yunnan production resumption is expected to raise supply and inventory pressure. Overall, prices are seen as volatile with uncertain demand support. | Reuters reports copper prices drifting lower as China property worries intensify, citing concerns over Country Garden’s ability to service debt and a stronger U.S. dollar pressuring dollar-priced metals. Three-month LME copper and September SHFE copper fell. LME aluminium also slipped slightly, while nickel rose modestly. | Copper prices sank to five-week lows as deteriorating China demand outlook intensified concerns tied to property-sector debt stress. Traders cited Country Garden’s bond issues and weaker Chinese credit data. Aluminium also fell on softer global demand and broader base-metal pressure from a stronger U.S. dollar. | SMM’s report tracks weekly aluminium ingot outflows from warehouses in China’s main consuming regions. Warehouse outflow data are used as a near-term indicator of local supply tightness and inventory drawdowns, which can influence expectations for short-term aluminium availability and prices. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) introduced a new AA6060.HE alloy and aluminium billets for extrusion customers, developed with the University of Bahrain and BALEXCO. The alloy targets faster extrusion rates to improve process efficiency and productivity. Commercial production began in October 2022 and Alba has secured a small customer base globally. | Baowu Aluminium Technology ordered an additional finishing mill stand (F0) for its Chinese aluminum hot rolling line, upgrading it from a 3-stand to a 4-stand design. The upgrade aims to double capacity to 600,000 tons/year, improve quality, and expand product range, scheduled for October 2024. | Copper fell to six-week lows as deteriorating demand prospects in China were reinforced by debt strains in the property sector, raising concerns over construction and power consumption. Aluminium also declined to near two-month lows on slower global demand, while traders weighed weaker Chinese credit data and upcoming macro releases.

China’s primary aluminium output rose 1.5% year-on-year to 3.48 million tonnes in July. Smelters in hydropower-heavy Yunnan ramped up after late-June power curbs were eased, with analysts estimating up to 1.4 million tonnes of added capacity. Some shutdowns occurred elsewhere but net supply increased. | Copper edged higher from a six-week low after China cut short-term loan and reverse repo rates and the dollar weakened, making dollar-priced metals cheaper. Three-month LME copper rose slightly, while SHFE copper also gained. Gains were limited by ongoing declines in China property investment and home sales. Aluminium and nickel also rose modestly. | Copper prices rebounded after hitting a six-week low, rising on expectations that China’s unexpected second rate cut in three months will improve demand and on a softer dollar. LME/SHFE copper and LME aluminium and nickel also moved higher, though China’s continued property weakness capped gains. | Baker McKenzie advised GARMCO, a large Gulf aluminium rolling mill in Bahrain, on a Bahraini court-approved USD 268 million debt restructuring, including debt-equity swaps and various debt instruments. GARMCO has repaid USD 33.5 million to creditors. The news is financial/legal with no stated production disruption. | Copper, aluminium and nickel prices fell in London after weaker China data signaled worsening demand: slower industrial output growth and declining property investment. LME copper slid to a 6-week low, aluminium hit a recent low as near-term discounts widened, and nickel dropped toward its lowest since 2022 amid cautious growth outlook. | Copper, aluminium and nickel fell on Tuesday after weak China data signaled worsening demand. Slower industrial output growth and declining property investment pressured base-metal sentiment. Copper hit a 6-week low and aluminium touched its weakest in weeks as near-term discounts widened, while nickel slid to its lowest since mid-2022.

Copper traded near a seven-week low as China’s weaker industrial output, investment and retail sales, plus a continuing property investment slump, raised concerns about demand for industrial metals. Easing supply worries were offset by rising LME copper inventories, while nickel fell and aluminium moved modestly higher amid soft sentiment. | Copper prices extended losses to a seven-week low as disappointing China data weakened demand expectations and metal stocks rose. LME copper fell and LME aluminium edged lower, with nickel mixed across contracts. Analysts cited easing global tightness, including a sharp LME stock build, and persistent property weakness. | Copper prices edged slightly above a seven-week low on Wednesday, supported by a weaker dollar but pressured by disappointing Chinese economic and housing data that raised concerns about demand. Zinc fell on inventory surge. Nickel gained and aluminium dipped modestly, reflecting mixed broader metal sentiment rather than a single dominant driver. | Copper prices hovered above a seven-week low, pressured by disappointing Chinese data and weaker property indicators that raise near-term demand concerns, though a softer U.S. dollar provided some support. Aluminium was little changed in the session, suggesting the main reported shocks affected copper more than aluminium. | Ivanhoe Mines (Kamoa-Kakula) signed an MOU with Lobito Atlantic International for rail transport of up to 10,000 tonnes of copper concentrate to Angola’s Lobito port for sale to international markets starting Q4 2023. The Lobito corridor should lower export logistics costs and transit times for copper concentrate. | The US reportedly welcomed a WTO panel report concerning China’s steel and aluminum tariffs. The decision/validation of tariff measures would likely reduce expected aluminum import flows into the US, supporting domestic supply conditions and adding near-term upward pressure to aluminum prices.

Egyptian coppersmiths in Cairo’s Al-Jamaliya area say rising global copper prices have cut demand, pushing consumers toward cheaper substitutes (including aluminum) and some craftsmen to abandon copper sheets. Artisans report fading workshops as skills retire, while tourist sales and higher costs threaten near-term copper product volumes. | China’s Commerce Ministry urged the U.S. to lift tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum after a WTO panel ruled in Washington’s favor. The U.S. had imposed 25% steel and 10% aluminum tariffs in 2018; Beijing retaliated with additional duties on some U.S. imports while studying the WTO findings. | Copper on the LME rebounded from an 11-week low after China’s central bank injected additional liquidity to support the economy. The dollar’s strength weighed, but the liquidity boost improved sentiment. Aluminium eased, while nickel rose slightly on both LME and SHFE, reflecting mixed but supportive macro conditions. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) says its Al Taweelah alumina refinery has been certified under the Aluminium Stewardship Initiative (ASI) Performance Standard, extending certification across its assets. The company targets producing ASI-certified metal next year and only ASI-certified aluminium by 2030, supported by efficient, lower-emissions operations and improved residue management. | Copper snapped back from a 2-1/2 month low as China’s central bank injected liquidity and state banks sold dollars to buy yuan, supporting the renminbi. LME three-month copper rose 0.6%. A firmer dollar capped gains. Aluminium and nickel also advanced on the session’s risk/FX-driven move. | China’s Commerce Ministry demands the U.S. immediately lift WTO-inconsistent tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum after a WTO panel backed Washington. The U.S. imposed 10% tariffs on Chinese aluminum in 2018, and China retaliated. A removal expectation could loosen market access and pressure aluminum prices. | A Global Times editorial argues the US has “no other choice” but to lift steel and aluminum tariffs. It cites MOFCOM urging immediate tariff removal and references Chinese industry concerns about US actions. The thrust is reduced trade friction for aluminium, which would likely dampen near-term aluminium price support.

China urged the US to immediately lift Section 232 tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum imports, arguing they violate WTO rules. The WTO panel found China’s countermeasures inconsistent and China says it will study the report. The dispute centers on tariff-driven trade flows, which can affect near-term aluminum demand expectations. | India’s ADC12 aluminium alloy ingot market was largely stable week-over-week, supported by range-bound scrap and corrections in US-linked futures. Primary producers cut prices by up to INR 3,750/t, while limited scrap supply and some regional premiums persisted. Demand is moderate and upcoming Maruti announcements are expected to limit near-term price declines. | SMM says China’s aluminium ingot stockpile fell to 490,000 mt as of Aug 17, down week-on-week and at a five-year low, driven mainly by fewer arrivals earlier in the week. The market is close to the prior five-year low and shortage of cast ingots may limit near-term supply. | China’s MOFCOM urges the US to lift Section 232 tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum, citing a WTO panel ruling that the measures violate WTO rules. China argues the tariffs disrupt global trade and supports removing the 10% aluminum import levy to restore market access and trade flows. | Romanian aluminium producer Alro Slatina will ask shareholders to approve USD 150 million of syndicated loans. The financing is intended to roll over existing revolving credit facilities and support working capital. Because the news is mainly about refinancing and liquidity without stated production cuts, any effect on aluminium prices is likely minor in the near term. | Ball Corporation will sell its aerospace business for $5.6 billion to streamline operations and accelerate “low-carbon” aluminium packaging. Management expects to bolster balance-sheet flexibility and invest in packaging growth, including circular aluminium packaging. The move signals continued or increased aluminium packaging output demand, supportive near-term sentiment for aluminium. | Novelis says recycled aluminium averaged 61% of its inputs in the fiscal year ended March 2023, using around 2.3 million tonnes of recycled metal and investing nearly $2bn since 2011. It highlights lower energy and emissions versus primary aluminium. The piece also references green hydrogen initiatives in aluminium recycling. | The piece highlights that Citi has bought about $160 million of Russian aluminum, while other buyers reportedly won’t touch similar supply. Such buying interest can signal near-term demand and potentially tighten aluminum availability, supporting sentiment and prices in the days ahead, even if details are limited. | Citigroup reportedly bought about $160 million of Russian-produced aluminum via LME and pushed for deliveries of roughly 75,000 tonnes from LME warehouses in South Korea. The article also notes a rising share of Russian-origin aluminum in LME warehouses as some buyers seek alternatives to other Russian supplies despite sanctions not directly targeting the metal. | China’s refined copper intake fell sharply in the first half and declined again in July, with customs data showing a 2.7% year-on-year drop. Coupled with a four-month manufacturing contraction and weaker industrial profits, traders cut copper demand expectations, pressuring copper and also slightly weighing on aluminum prices.

Copper rebounded after three weekly declines as China’s central bank pledged to resolve local government debt risks and cut the one-year lending rate, boosting sentiment. Low inventories also supported prices. LME and SHFE showed small gains in aluminium and nickel as well, reflecting broader risk-on demand expectations. | Chorus reported a 61% profit drop, citing higher interest rates and increased depreciation costs after shutting down copper network areas and expanding fibre. It said copper connections fell by about a third to 240,000 remaining, reinforcing a transition away from copper broadband. No explicit aluminium market linkage was provided. | Novelis is upgrading its Oswego, New York hot rolling mill with ABB to replace obsolete drives and controls, including switchgears, transformers, and medium-voltage motors. The project aims to increase uptime and reliability and improve energy efficiency, with commissioning expected by end-2023. This supports aluminum sheet output. | China’s trade ministry urged the US to immediately remove Section 232 tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminium imports after a WTO tribunal ruled the US levies were illegal. The ministry said it is reviewing the findings. The prospect of tariff relief would improve aluminium import prospects and likely support prices. | Copper prices rebounded after a third weekly fall, supported by sharp SHFE stock declines and low inventories plus expectations of autumn demand. However, gains are capped by weak Chinese economic/property data and liquidity risks. China cut the one-year lending rate, adding support, while aluminium and nickel rose modestly. | Wave Nickel says it is pursuing a $440 million Texas refinery to produce nickel sulfate for EV batteries, supported by U.S. incentives. It plans feedstock from a planned Brazilian microwave plant and cites a current nickel-sulfate glut tied to Indonesian supply, suggesting future tightening as projects develop slowly beyond China/Indonesia.

Alumina Limited says further regulatory oversight is acceptable, but administrative delays tied to environmental permitting in Western Australia hurt productivity. The company reported a $US42.9 million underlying half-year loss and stopped its interim dividend, attributing weakness to modest alumina prices and permitting-driven operational challenges, which may pressure aluminium sentiment. | Copper prices rose for a fourth straight session, supported by firmer Chinese refined copper demand (ICSG) and additional Chinese monetary support, alongside a weaker US dollar. Reuters also reported LME aluminium and nickel rising that day, indicating a broader, sentiment-driven lift across industrial metals. | Novelis, owned by Hindalco, said it is still honouring pre-war contracts with Rusal but has not signed any new deals for Russian aluminium since the Ukraine invasion. With 2024 contract talks approaching, consumers’ reluctance to buy Rusal metal may tighten supply expectations, supporting aluminium sentiment. | China’s Ministry of Commerce says the U.S. steel and aluminum 232 tariffs violate WTO rules and demands their immediate cancellation. The ministry argues the tariffs have been challenged since 2018 and faces continued refusal by the U.S. to implement WTO rulings. The piece signals possible trade pressure on aluminium flows.

Copper and most base metals rose in London as improved demand prospects from top consumer China—supported by upbeat Chinese copper consumption data and speculation of further government measures—boosted sentiment. A stronger yuan and weaker dollar supported dollar-priced metals. LME aluminium and nickel also increased alongside copper. | Copper rose for a fifth straight session on improved Chinese demand prospects, upbeat consumption data and speculation of further Chinese support measures. Tight LME-monitored stockpiles near a 15-year low also helped. A stronger dollar eased, keeping base metals generally higher, with aluminium and nickel also moving up. | REMADE and Phinix licensed a patent-pending purification technology to remove metallic impurities from recycled aluminium melts. Designed to widen recycled aluminium’s end-use, including EV manufacturing, it targets higher recycling rates and lower energy costs versus virgin production. The news is supportive of recycled aluminium supply and sentiment.

London copper pulled back 0.7% after a five-session rally, driven by unease over high interest rates and weaker global growth expectations, which outweighed tentative demand optimism from China. Technical resistance also limited upside. LME aluminium and nickel fell alongside copper as investors awaited Jackson Hole.

Reuters reports copper fell on Friday as a strong U.S. dollar increased costs for non-dollar buyers. Three-month LME copper dropped and SHFE October copper weakened. It also cites weaker China demand data and shows aluminium and nickel easing on SHFE, indicating broader near-term pressure from FX and macro sentiment. | Novelis is building a $2.5 billion aluminum mill in Baldwin County, Alabama, with 600 kilotonnes per year capacity and a recycling center adding capacity for 15 billion beverage cans annually. The plant is scheduled to commission mid-2025 and uses renewable power and recycling-focused operations, boosting future aluminum output. | Reuters reports London/LME copper is set for its biggest weekly gain in four, supported by Chinese policy measures stabilizing the yuan and hopes for stronger consumption in coming months. A firmer dollar caps gains. Aluminium is nearly flat, while nickel edges slightly higher, reflecting limited spillover. | Copper is set for its strongest week in a month after LME prices rise on expectations of a China housing-demand uplift, supportive seasonal consumption, and short-covering. China policy and “no lockdowns” help sentiment. Stocks elsewhere are low, raising volatility risk. Aluminium and nickel also tick higher. | India and the US are nearing a deal on a joint monitoring mechanism for Indian exports of steel and certain aluminium products to America without extra Section 232 duties. Washington will clear most steel and aluminium exclusion applications, but monitoring aims to prevent import surges. Copper is not mentioned and likely unaffected.

SMM reports SHFE 2310 aluminium ended higher after opening around 18,620 yuan/mt, with LME closed. It cites supportive domestic policies, but notes Fed-rate speculation adds macro uncertainty. On fundamentals, supply grows while ingot output is limited; imports may slow destocking. Net imbalance is mild; prices expected to fluctuate upward this week. | Copper prices rose as the dollar weakened ahead of major U.S. data, while China’s stock-market-support measures improved risk sentiment. LME three-month copper gained 0.5% and Shanghai October copper edged up. LME aluminium rose, but nickel fell, indicating mixed effects across metals tied to macro and sentiment. | Copper prices rose 0.9% after China announced measures to support housing loans and stock trading, improving risk appetite and prompting funds/CTAs to cut short positions. Markets await China PMI data for demand signals. Aluminium also edged higher, while nickel was slightly down, reflecting mixed metal-specific flows. | Fastmarkets reports European secondary aluminium prices for pressure-diecasting ingots stabilizing but still trending lower since early January. New DIN230/DIN239 assessments are introduced as producers cite overcapacity, smelter coverage problems, and a sharp drop in scrap inflows (~15%). Auto demand worries and potential production cuts add bearish sentiment. | BIS proposed changes to the Section 232 exclusion process for US steel and aluminum tariffs, including tighter eligibility for general approved exclusions, creation of a denied-exclusion framework, and new certification/evidence requirements showing reasonable sourcing attempts from the US or specified partnered countries. Comments are due Oct. 12, 2023. | Weston Aluminium agreed to pay $200,000 toward environmental and community projects after an EPA investigation found breaches of its environmental licence linked to a 2021 fire at the Kurri plant. The findings included improper waste storage, failure to notify authorities, and unlawful waste transport, prompting remediation and revised waste management. | Weston Aluminium will pay $200,000 following an Environment Protection Authority investigation into breaches at its Kurri plant, linked to a 2021 fire. The EPA found problems with waste storage, labelling, segregation, notification, and illegal transport causing land pollution. The company will clean up, remediate, improve practices, and pay EPA costs. | Weston Aluminium agreed to pay $200,000 for environmental initiatives after an Environment Protection Authority investigation found breaches of its waste-storage and notification obligations following a 2021 Kurri plant fire. The company is revising its fire waste management plan, improving segregation and labelling, cleaning up waste, and funding remediation and EPA costs.

LME aluminium storage is shifting to South Korea’s Gwangyang, now holding about half of exchange light-metal and most live warrants. Inflows are largely Russian-brand metal displaced by U.S. tariffs and self-sanctioning. Weak demand drives super-contango and stocks-financing activity, fueling a “storage wars” queue that may affect short-term pricing dynamics. | London copper edged down 0.1% to $8,443/t as traders stayed cautious ahead of key China and U.S. economic data that could steer interest-rate expectations and demand outlook. LME aluminium rose 0.3% while nickel fell 0.2%, indicating mixed, low-magnitude pressure across industrial metals. | London copper slipped as traders took a cautious stance ahead of key U.S. and China data. A softer dollar and China mortgage easing in Guangzhou limited declines. Copper premiums widened on higher London–Shanghai spreads. Nickel fell and aluminium edged up, reflecting mixed macro-driven sentiment rather than a specific metal supply shock. | Copper prices slipped on concerns about weaker global growth and metals demand, with investors reassessing softer U.S. jobs data. Losses were moderated by signs of support for China’s property sector. Aluminium rose, while nickel eased, reflecting mixed sentiment ahead of further China manufacturing data. | Copper prices fell 0.2% on weaker economic-growth concerns, but losses were moderated by China property-sector support measures, including mortgage easing in Guangzhou. The green transition was cited as underlying support. Aluminium rose while nickel declined, reflecting mixed macro-demand signals across industrial metals. | Crown Holdings expanded Aluminium Stewardship Initiative (ASI) Performance Standard certifications to two beverage packaging plants in Thailand, bringing its total ASI-certified facilities to 12, alongside plants in Brazil and Mexico. The release highlights sustainability, ESG training, and responsible aluminum value-chain practices without mentioning any production or supply changes. | Kolkata Metro Railway plans to replace about 35 RKm of steel third rail with composite aluminium third rail in stages (Dumdum–Shyambazar, Shyambazar–Central/J D Park–Tollygunge, and Tollygunge–Kavi Subhash). The move is intended to cut resistance losses, reduce substations, and lower capex and lifecycle costs, supporting aluminium demand.

South Korea’s Gwangyang port has become a major LME aluminium storage hub, with 256,650 tonnes in LME-registered warehouses—about 49% of the global aluminium stock network. The shift highlights intensifying aluminium warehouse competition and may affect near-term sentiment given supercontango/weak demand references. | SMM reports aluminium prices rising overnight on both SHFE and LME. It attributes the positive tone to supportive domestic policies and improved sentiment ahead of September peak season. Fundamentals remain constructive with low ingot inventories and limited ingot production. Imports after the window and Southwest capacity may slow destocking, but accumulation should stay limited. | London copper fell and was set for its first monthly drop since May as disappointing China manufacturing data extended weak growth signals. Tepid consumption outweighed hopes for policy support. A stronger U.S. dollar also weighed. Nickel and aluminium prices edged down/inched higher, but the overall macro tone was negative for metals demand. | Si6 Metals plans a 10,000m drilling program next month at its Botswana Maibele nickel sulphide project, targeting extensions and depth to upgrade the Maibele North resource. Drilling also targets copper-silver mineralisation at Dibete and Airstrip. It may improve future supply expectations for nickel and some copper, but near-term price impact is limited. | Norsk Hydro said its Sunndal primary aluminium smelter will shift from fossil fuels to locally sourced biomethane to cut emissions. The plan involves replacing most natural gas at the casthouse and anode production facility, with energy secured from 2024 as biomethane plants are developed. The move targets lower-carbon aluminium demand in Europe. | SMM discusses China’s limited bauxite resources and worsening high-grade ore shortages. It notes Indonesia—one of China’s top suppliers—planned to stop export of raw commodities and banned bauxite exports in June 2023. With imports reaching record levels in 2022, the article implies ongoing supply tightness. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) said the global energy transition will raise long-term aluminium demand, citing growth in usage in solar and wind over coming decades. It also notes aluminium prices have fallen about 17% from January highs due to economic weakness, implying no immediate supply-demand shock within weeks. | Hydro’s Sunndal aluminium plant plans to replace 70% of natural gas with locally sourced biomethane to cut CO2 emissions by about 20,000 tonnes annually. The biomethane supply depends on Enova support for a Havila project starting in 2024. The move supports Hydro’s goal of zero-carbon aluminium. | Copper fell 1.1% after China’s manufacturing PMI contracted for a fifth month, reinforcing demand distress. A stronger U.S. dollar added drag, and LME warehouse stocks rose sharply. Aluminium rose on expectations of robust Chinese demand and low inventories. Nickel was also down, reflecting weaker sentiment. | The U.S. Department of Commerce proposed revisions to the Section 232 steel and aluminum tariff exclusions process, including expanding General Approved Exclusions, creating General Denied Exclusions, and adding stricter requester and objector certification/evidence rules. Commerce estimates fewer exclusion requests, potentially limiting tariff-free aluminum supply.

Copper prices rose 0.8% early Sept 1 as China issued further measures to revive its crisis-hit property sector, including easing borrowing rules and lowering mortgage rates. The improved demand outlook supported broader industrial metals, with LME aluminium and nickel also up around 0.8-1.1% despite a strong U.S. dollar. | SMM says China’s aluminum ingot social stocks were 493,000 mt on Aug 31, slightly higher WoW but still far below last year and at a five-year low level. Cargo arrivals rose, but demand/outflows in East China kept total inventory under 500,000 mt. SHFE aluminum’s upward trend is cited, with projected mild accumulation. | US imports of aluminium scrap from Canada increased in May 2023, rising 6.99% month-on-month to 35,200 tonnes after April’s decline. Year-to-date imports for the first five months climbed 8.5% year-on-year to 166,000 tonnes, indicating improving scrap supply into the US market. | Marubeni data shows Japanese aluminium stocks at Yokohama, Nagoya and Osaka fell 1.9% month-on-month in July 2023 to 350,600 tonnes. June stocks rose slightly. On a year-on-year basis, stocks declined, but the month-to-month drop is relatively small. | Copper hit a four-week high as China announced measures to support its crisis-hit property market and manufacturing activity expanded. Analysts attributed the rally to improving demand indicators such as new orders in PMIs and expectations of metals restocking as construction resumes. Aluminium and nickel also rose alongside copper. | Aurubis says it may face large losses after discovering discrepancies in its metals inventory tied to manipulated invoices for shipments that never reached its site. The affected inputs include copper and precious metals. The firm has downgraded forecasts and is investigating potential criminal activity, raising near-term risk perception in metals markets.

SMM’s morning comment tracks SHFE 2310 and LME aluminium trading on Sep 4, showing modest gains. It attributes support to high molten aluminium output proportions and continuously low ingot inventories, while macro uncertainty from potential Fed rate hikes remains. Peak-season September-October demand is expected to help, but prices may fluctuate. | Lindian signed an MOU with Guinea’s CBG to explore supplying bauxite from its Gaoual project to CBG’s production, including feasibility study work and negotiation of potential sales terms. The Gaoual resource is high-grade in alumina. The deal may modestly improve sentiment toward aluminium feedstock prospects. | AEROBAL says global shipments of aluminium aerosol cans rose 6.6% in the first half of 2023 to just over 3.2 billion units, led by personal care, pharma and EU/US demand. It also notes sustainability-driven push for recycled aluminium, while limited scrap availability and inflation are risks. | Copper prices fell as markets worried about slower demand from China’s property sector and as LME-registered copper inventories rose sharply. A softer US dollar limited losses, but copper still declined. Aluminium also eased, while nickel dropped amid concerns over Indonesia delaying mining quota issuance for nickel supply. | Copper prices retreated 0.9% to $8,425 as traders worried about China demand, particularly slower property activity, and about rising LME warehouse inventories. Losses were limited by a softer dollar. Aluminium also fell. Nickel edged lower, despite separate supply concerns tied to Indonesia mining quota delays.

SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium prices rose slightly in the previous session, but notes that concentrated arrivals of aluminium ingots have increased social inventory, weakening price support. It highlights macro uncertainty from the Fed’s shifting rate stance and says near-term prices may face downward pressure, pending peak-season consumption data. | Copper fell as LME exchange inventories rose and investors worried about demand amid soft economic data from China. Reuters also noted weaker Chinese services activity, while aluminium declined and nickel showed a slight/offset move. The net effect reflected near-term demand concerns and inventory pressure. | Novelis urges Korea’s environmental ministry to set more realistic rules for classifying aluminum beverage-can scrap as “circular resources.” The recycling process requires low contaminants, and Novelis recommends a higher allowable contamination rate and more consumer cleanliness. It also plans $50m investment in an aluminum recycling center in Ulsan. | Copper prices in London fell as weak China services data raised doubts about the post-pandemic recovery and whether Beijing’s property-support measures will translate into real metal demand. The article also notes broad LME softness, with aluminium and nickel down, alongside weaker global activity and a firmer U.S. dollar. | Minsur says it will invest at least $2 billion over five years to expand copper and tin activities. It plans about $543 million for underground work at Justa, plus $381 million for processing and site improvements. Copper output in 2022 was 125,488 mt, while tin and smelter upgrades are also planned.

Copper eased 0.6% on the LME after China’s yuan slid to a 10-month low and German factory orders fell more than expected, reinforcing worries about weak global growth. Reuters notes disappointing Chinese stimulus and tepid demand into September. LME aluminium rose modestly, while nickel declined, reflecting mixed macro risk. | Bingelela Alloy inaugurated a new aluminium hub in Richards Bay to streamline molten aluminium from the Hillside smelter, supporting downstream value-added manufacturing and scrap recycling. The initiative aligns with South Africa’s EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism preparations, with stakeholders citing growth, jobs, and sustainable smelting practices. | The U.S. Department of Commerce (BIS) proposed revisions to the Section 232 steel and aluminum tariff exclusion process and requested comments. For aluminum, the notice would modify general exclusion criteria and add certification and evidence requirements for requests and objections, potentially influencing tariff relief for specific imports.

Copper prices fell to the lowest in over a week as a firm dollar and weaker yuan weighed on demand sentiment in China. LME three-month copper and SHFE October both dropped to multi-week lows. The piece also shows nickel weakness alongside modest aluminium gains, reflecting mixed but generally soft metal tone. | Copper prices fell to their lowest in more than a week as a firm U.S. dollar made dollar-priced metals more expensive and the market awaited additional economic stimulus. China’s yuan weakened and trade data remained soft, weighing sentiment. LME copper dropped 0.4%, with nickel also down while aluminium edged up. | Mytilineos agreed to acquire Imerys Bauxites Greece for €10 million, taking control of bauxite mining in Fokida and boosting accessible bauxite capacity to over 1.2 million tonnes via integration steps. The move reinforces vertical supply for its Aluminium of Greece operations, supporting primary aluminium production outlook despite European refinery disruptions. | Copper fell to a two-week low as LME inventories jumped 21% to 133,850 tonnes, signalling potential surplus concerns, while China’s August imports were 5% lower year-on-year. A stronger U.S. dollar and easing Chinese premiums added pressure. Aluminium and nickel also declined. | The piece argues copper is essential for the energy transition and may face shortages as EVs and renewables expand, citing DOE and other projections. It also describes META’s NCORE polymer composite current collectors that reduce copper foil content. Overall, it’s more outlook/marketing than a near-term supply shock for copper.

Reuters says copper is on track for its worst week in four weeks as a firmer US dollar and rising LME inventories weigh on prices. Risk sentiment is dampened by weak Chinese data and property woes. LME aluminium and nickel also fall on the day, reflecting broad bearish metal pressure. | Copper extended losses toward its biggest weekly drop in four weeks as a strong dollar and mixed/patchy macro data, including weaker China demand expectations, weighed on sentiment. Commerzbank cut its copper target and SHFE-registered stocks rose 18%. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same pressure.

London copper rose 0.4% after a weekly loss, supported by signs of China stabilisation: easing deflation, slower factory price declines, and property-market support measures that lift demand hopes. A weaker yuan and higher SHFE copper inventories weighed sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel also gained modestly. | Copper prices rose on Monday as China’s yuan strengthened, the U.S. dollar weakened, and August loan growth beat expectations, encouraging algorithmic trading buy signals. Reuters notes copper had hit a three-week low Friday. Aluminium and nickel were also higher in the same market move amid improving China data. | Array Technologies’ 10-year partnership with Extruded Aluminum Corporation includes a nearly $35m Michigan facility expansion with a new large aluminum press and added extrusion capacity. The project is intended to support Array’s domestic, IRA-compliant supply chain for solar tracker components, strengthening near-term demand expectations for aluminium. | LME data show Russian-origin share in LME-registered warehouses steady at 81% for aluminium, with Russian aluminium on-warrant tonnage falling in August. The article also notes Russian-origin copper share declined while copper tonnage rose, and Russian nickel share slipped. It implies mixed inventory tightness signals for metals. | Novelis and Ball signed an anchor long-term contract for Novelis to supply aluminum beverage can sheet to Ball plants in North America. The deal secures beverage can capacity from Novelis’ new Bay Minette, Alabama recycling/manufacturing plant, supporting expectations for aluminum demand tied to contracted production.

Copper rose after China’s Country Garden won creditor approval to extend repayment terms on onshore bonds, easing stress in the property sector that drives large metal demand. Reuters notes supportive macro factors like weaker dollar and stronger yuan, and aluminium rose as well, while nickel was slightly lower. | The piece argues that Norway should recycle aluminium from scrapped vehicles back into new vehicle parts rather than downcycling, emphasizing research, quality retention, and removal of contaminants. It cites that melting scrap uses only about 5% of the energy required for primary aluminium, implying lower costs and supportive expectations for aluminium recycling. | London copper declined as the U.S. dollar rebounded, making dollar-priced metals more expensive. Relief in China’s property sector after Country Garden won bond-repayment extensions provided some support, but the market saw short-covering. The article also notes LME aluminium easing and nickel falling. | Hindalco signed a technology partnership with Italy’s Metra to produce large-size, high-precision aluminium extrusions for high-speed rail coaches in India. Hindalco plans to invest Rs. 2,000 crore for the project tied to Vande Bharat-style upgrades, implying incremental aluminium demand and supportive sentiment. | Copper prices fell in London as the dollar strengthened and LME-registered copper inventories rose to their highest levels since October 2022. The dollar reaction ahead of U.S. inflation data pressured metals, while China’s property-sector news capped losses. Aluminium and nickel were also down on the day. | The article notes aluminum futures have fallen sharply this year amid weak Europe/U.S. growth and poor Chinese construction demand, with large contango reflecting weak spot demand and a global surplus. It then cites signs of inventory drawdown stabilizing and record-high China import demand, supporting a medium-term aluminum recovery narrative.

Copper prices were largely unchanged in a tight range ahead of U.S. CPI, with traders weighing potential interest-rate implications and China’s demand outlook despite property-support measures. Aluminium and nickel showed only modest moves (aluminium slightly up, nickel listless/falling), implying no strong near-term directional commodity pressure. | China Hongqiao is shifting part of its aluminium smelting capacity from Shandong to Yunnan, citing lower-carbon hydro power and steps to move away from coal. The firm plans to move 2 million tons by year-end and 4 million tons by 2025. Yunnan power conditions are normalizing after earlier disruptions. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) CEO Ali Al Baqali spoke at a Fastmarkets aluminium conference, highlighting Middle East market resilience, auto and packaging recovery, and Alba’s ESG/low-carbon roadmap to address EU CBAM. The company also referenced its Power Station 5 Block 4 expansion, expected to cut emissions intensity from 2024 onward, supporting costs and demand expectations. | ElvalHalcor’s H1’23 results press release highlights dampened demand affecting both its aluminium and copper segments and notes that LME aluminium and copper prices averaged lower in the first half versus 2022. It attributes performance to production flexibility, conversion prices, and lower metal-related spreads, without announcing a new immediate market disruption.

SMM expects China’s aluminium prices to be supported in Sep 12–Oct 10 by favourable real estate credit and other demand-support policies plus low social inventories. Operating capacity reached record highs and imports are rising, but near-term contracts are still forecast to remain well supported, with possible inventory pressure limited. | London copper fell 0.5% as LME warehouse stocks jumped toward a two-year peak, signaling weaker demand. China’s post-summer pick-up failed to materialize. Aluminium eased and nickel was slightly higher/volatile. A softer dollar capped some losses, but demand concerns dominated. | International Aluminium Associations released a joint action plan for G7 trade ministers, urging electricity decarbonisation, cleaner production technologies, higher recycling rates, and market incentives including trade enforcement and potential carbon border adjustments. The briefing targets fairer global aluminium markets versus distortions linked to non-market economies like China. | Reuters reports India’s aluminium sector is growing as government infrastructure spending and expanding auto and housing demand support rising consumption. Hindalco’s executive notes demand could nearly double by 2033, but project approvals and skilled labour shortages have already delayed expansions, limiting near-term capacity gains.

China’s primary aluminium output hit a record monthly high in August, aided by resumed capacity in Yunnan as hydropower constraints ease. With September output expected to match August, and consumption uncertainty, higher supply may lift inventories and weigh on aluminium prices in the coming weeks. | European aluminium deals for 2024 are being delayed as industrial customers fear overbuying amid slumping demand across end-use sectors. Germany’s economic weakness is cited, premiums for aluminium billets in northern Germany have fallen sharply, and companies remain hand-to-mouth, waiting until later to finalize tonnages. | Copper rose on Friday after China’s August industrial data signaled stabilization, easing fears of slowing demand from the world’s largest metals consumer. A stronger yuan and softer dollar also supported dollar-priced buying. Aluminium fell on record Chinese production; nickel declined alongside broader base-metal moves and rising inventories.

China’s August aluminium imports rose 20% month-on-month as demand hopes improved and domestic inventories stayed tight, with SHFE stocks falling below 100,000 tons. Imports increased further on higher Russian purchases. Aluminium production and bauxite imports also climbed, reinforcing active consumption and near-term support for prices. | Copper and other base metals were largely flat in Asia as markets awaited the U.S. Federal Reserve’s Sept. 20 rate decision and guidance. Analysts said the Fed outcome would steer metals and energy. Reported moves were minimal: copper near-flat, aluminium slightly higher on LME, and nickel marginally up. | Russia has increased alumina imports from India to feed its large Siberian aluminium plants after alumina supply was disrupted by a Ukraine suspension and Australia’s ban on exports to Russia. Rusal expanded sourcing (India/Kazakhstan) to keep output and contain costs, potentially supporting near-term aluminium supply. | Reuters reports copper sentiment was pressured by renewed China demand concerns tied to a worse property sector and by LME approved warehouse inventories rising more than 170% since mid-July. Despite support from expectations that global rate tightening is nearing its end, copper traded flat and pressured by piling stocks. | Reuters reports Glencore bought only small volumes of potential Rusal aluminium supply under an existing contract that may be extended to end-2025. After Glencore stopped new Russian purchases, existing obligations remain and unfulfilled backlog could release larger quantities to rivals, adding supply expectations and pressuring aluminium prices. | Crown Holdings and the International Aluminium Institute released a study outlining measures to strengthen beverage can recycling in several countries, aiming to reduce CO2 and increase recycling rates. Better collection and recycling systems should boost recycled aluminum supply versus primary production, potentially offering modest near-term support for aluminium prices.

SMM’s morning note says SHFE 2310 aluminium and LME aluminium rose overnight. It attributes support to low inventories and low warehouse receipts, despite an open import window, rising overseas ingot supply, and inventory build in ingots and billets from weak downstream operating rates. Near-term outlook is high-level oscillation. | Copper prices edged lower as markets awaited central bank decisions, with the Fed meeting raising expectations that rates may stay high. Demand worries from China’s slowing rebound and higher exchange inventories also pressured prices. Nickel slipped slightly, while aluminium was marginally up, reflecting mixed sentiment across metals. | Copper prices fell in Asian trade as markets awaited the Fed’s two-day meeting and worried about a faltering China rebound, with expectations for weaker demand and higher exchange inventories. Traders also monitored yuan depreciation affecting dollar-priced metals demand. LME aluminium and nickel edged lower alongside copper. | Copper prices fell ahead of a U.S. Fed meeting as traders expected higher-for-longer rates to dampen industrial metals demand and as LME inventories continued building. Reuters also notes declines in aluminium and nickel, reflecting broad risk-off sentiment tied to demand uncertainty, China weakness, and tighter financial conditions. | Sun Summit Minerals reports discovery of a new copper mineralized zone at its Buck Project in central British Columbia, with surface rock samples returning up to 1.00% Cu and drill program progress at Buck Main. The release is an exploration update and does not indicate near-term production changes. | Pan Global reports drilling results extending the La Romana copper-tin-silver mineralization by 150m westward to a 1.35km strike length at its Escacena project in Spain. Copper/tin grades appear to increase to the west and drilling is ongoing. The update is exploratory and not tied to near-term production changes. | Copper prices extended losses ahead of the Fed meeting as investors expect higher rates to persist, weighing on industrial demand. LME copper inventories continue building, rising sharply since mid-July. China growth concerns, weaker yuan pressure, and weak demand contribute to the broader downside, with aluminium and nickel also falling. | China has appealed a WTO panel ruling on U.S. steel and aluminium tariffs. The panel found China’s counter-duties on U.S. goods were inconsistent with WTO obligations after the U.S. imposed 25% steel and 10% aluminium import tariffs. The appeal could influence expectations for aluminium trade and prices. | Copper prices fell as LME inventories built and traders grew cautious before the Fed meeting, fearing “higher rates for longer” could damp metals demand. China’s lacklustre growth and yuan depreciation pressures also weighed. LME aluminium edged down, while nickel was flat after briefly hitting a 14-month low.

Copper in London edged up on a softer dollar, ahead of an expected steady Fed decision, but LME-registered copper inventories are building and reaching the highest since May 2022, limiting upside. Nickel rose and aluminium edged higher in LME/SHFE, reflecting day-to-day macro and flows rather than a major supply disruption. | Copper prices moved in tight ranges ahead of a U.S. Fed decision as rising LME inventories to the highest since May 2022 and higher-for-longer rate expectations/dollar strength limited upside. Aluminium edged up slightly. Nickel rose but faced a large surplus backdrop alongside concerns over Indonesia’s refusal to approve new output quotas. | Copper prices rose in London as optimism about China’s base-metals demand eased worries about the crisis-hit property sector. The move also benefited from a weaker U.S. dollar ahead of the Fed decision. The rally came despite rising LME copper inventories, which remain elevated. LME aluminium also increased. | Germany’s aluminium industry says it remains in crisis as EU adds aluminium to the Strategic Raw Materials list. A survey shows most firms face poor energy supply and high prices, with expected negative impacts, capacity cuts, and reduced orders. There’s also a recycled-material shortage, alongside modest growth in aerosol can shipments.

Anaheim reports dozens of streetlights vandalized as thieves steal copper wire from light boxes and utility poles. Police link it to higher copper thefts and recyclers’ scrap resale, while the city replaces copper with aluminum wire and tamper-proof fixtures. Repairs may take weeks, but the event is geographically limited. | Copper prices slid to a five-week low as the Fed signalled restrictive rates for longer, boosting the dollar and pressuring risk appetite. LME inventories rose to the highest since May 2022, widening the near-term discount. Nickel and aluminium also fell on the same macro and demand/sentiment backdrop.

Reuters reports copper prices in London rose slightly on Friday but were set for a weekly decline as LME inventories swelled and the U.S. dollar firmed on higher-for-longer Fed expectations. Analysts cite uneven China growth and tighter financial conditions.
