Importers face rules around suspension of liquidation and depositing duties (CVD/AD mechanics pending an ITC injury decision within 45 days). The outcome could support domestic aluminum-extrusion supply expectations. | TSR and Novelis agreed a three-year supply deal for 75,000 tonnes of high-quality, pre-sorted end-of-life aluminium scrap for automotive sheet production. The scrap grades TSR130/136 target specific alloys and decarbonization goals. Novelis cites limited scrap availability and the need for long-term supply resilience.

Canada will implement tariff surcharges on Chinese enterprises for aluminium (and steel) effective Oct 22, 2024, as part of measures against China’s perceived overcapacity and unfair trade policies. The action is likely to increase costs for Chinese aluminium imports into Canada and support nearby aluminium price expectations. | The article headline says alumina in China has risen to a record high on supply woes while demand remains strong. As alumina is the key feedstock for aluminium smelting, tighter supply and higher input costs tend to lift expectations for aluminium prices over the coming days to weeks. | Fastmarkets issued a correction to its previously published premium for aluminium 6063 extrusion billet (in-warehouse Rotterdam) for Oct 11. The premium range was adjusted from $530-560/tonne to $525-560/tonne and the pricing database was updated accordingly within its North Europe aluminium billet package.

SMM reports overseas metallurgical-grade alumina output fell 2.7% YoY in September, with refinery operating rates down year-on-year. Multiple disruptions and guidance cuts (Alcoa shutdown, Rio Tinto reductions, South32 maintenance/environment delays) contribute. October is forecast to decline further 2.7% YoY, with weaker operating rates. | Reuters columnist Clyde Russell says investors reacted differently to Beijing’s latest stimulus rhetoric. Shanghai copper futures opened higher while London copper fell, reflecting sentiment on possible refinancing of local government debt and renewed construction/property demand, though the author argues demand effects may be limited and later. | Xanadu released a large prefeasibility study for its Kharmagtai copper-gold project in Mongolia, projecting ~60,000 tonnes of copper annually initially, low cash costs, and a long 29-year mine life. The company expects a bankable feasibility study in 2026, supporting sentiment for future copper supply.

Alcoa said it will supply up to 16.5 million tonnes of smelter-grade alumina to Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) from 2026–2035, mainly sourced from Western Australia. The deal extends a long relationship and is intended to provide Alba reliable alumina supply, though it is not a near-term shock to prices. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) has renewed a long-term alumina supply agreement with Alcoa for up to 16.5 million tonnes of smelter-grade alumina over 10 years, beginning in 2026. Both firms said the deal secures stable feedstock for Alba’s operations and reinforces Alcoa’s alumina-supply position.

Rio Tinto said its Amrun bauxite mine is operating above nameplate capacity, lifting bauxite output, but primary aluminum production fell due to an electricity reduction request at its New Zealand smelter that has ended and ramp-up is underway. The firm also reported lower mined copper output. Overall effects may offset near term. | Australia’s Aluminium Council renewed its call for bauxite, alumina and aluminium to be listed as Critical Minerals, arguing risks from geopolitics, higher costs, and lengthy environmental approvals have impaired and curtailed alumina refining. The group urges faster approvals to secure bauxite feed for refineries and avoid supply disruption. | Supply disruption in Guinea suspended bauxite exports from Guinea Alumina Corporation over customs issues. With Guinea accounting for about 4% of global bauxite supply, alumina prices have surged, creating potential gains for bauxite producers like Metro Mining. The tighter feedstock outlook points to near-term bullish aluminium price pressure. | Rio Tinto reported Q3 copper and aluminium production updates. Copper ramp-up continues at Oyu Tolgoi, while Kennecott faced lower mined output from highwall movement and Escondida improved on higher grades. Aluminium output was slightly lower due to an NZAS electricity-reduction request, now ended and ramp-up underway.  | Alumina prices surged to multi-month/all-time highs on SHFE and in spot markets as global supply disruptions and tighter bauxite availability tightened the alumina balance. Rising alumina costs raise input expenses for aluminium smelters (30–40% of costs), potentially squeezing margins and leading to production cuts if aluminium prices lag. | Alcoa says it is nearing a joint funding deal with Spain’s Ignis to support operation of its San Ciprian aluminium plant in Galicia. Alcoa would invest €75m, Ignis €25m for 25% ownership, with potential further funding up to €100m. The plan aims to offset high energy costs and comes amid efforts to sell the plant.

Alcoa shares rose as surging alumina prices boosted profitability. Alumina is a key feedstock for primary aluminium production, so the article signals momentum and tighter conditions within the aluminium cost/value chain that can influence expectations for near-term aluminium pricing. | The US Department of Commerce issued a final determination on its anti-dumping probe into Vietnamese aluminium extrusions/products, raising dumping margins versus the preliminary conclusion. Duties would follow an ITC injury decision and could reduce Vietnamese import volumes into the US. This creates near-term downside pressure on aluminium prices. | Ottawa awarded Can Art Aluminum Extrusion Canada a $5 million grant to scale up aluminium parts manufacturing for electric vehicles, expanding capacity to produce new components for the EV market. The funding supports six EV manufacturers, and signals rising near-term aluminium demand tied to EV battery and component supply.

Alumina prices surged to records on tight supply and Guinea bauxite export problems, driving speculative inflows in Shanghai. The higher alumina-aluminium cost ratio is compressing smelter margins and widening cash premiums versus forwards. With reduced ShFE stocks, aluminium prices face potential upside as costs become unpredictable. | Vedanta announced a Rs 1 lakh crore investment in Odisha to build a 6 MTPA alumina refinery and a 3 MTPA green aluminium plant. The project is expected to expand downstream industrial activity, but it signals future capacity rather than near-term production changes. Immediate aluminium price impact appears limited. | Fastmarkets reports a 251-day aluminium withdrawal queue at Port Klang’s Istim warehouse, tied to LME warehouse rules (QBRC) and traders’ metal cancellations to capture free rent. Debate centers on whether QBRC worsens queues or traders exploit rent incentives, amid inflows of 652,525 tonnes in May 2024.

South32 said Q1 alumina production (share) fell to 1.184 million tonnes from 1.290 million, but its quarterly aluminium production rose to 298 thousand tonnes from 288 thousand. The company kept its fiscal 2025 production guidance. The report implies near-term aluminium supply support despite weaker alumina output.

Forte Minerals announced acquisition of the Miscanthus Cu-Mo-Au and Au-Ag prospects in Peru, including an approved DIA drilling permit for up to 40 platforms. While the copper component may affect medium-term expectations for copper exploration and potential supply, the article provides no near-term physical market data, implying limited price impact. | ALCOM LLC will close its Triton Trailers aluminum trailer plant in Hartford on Jan. 12, laying off 113 workers, citing operation centralization and competitiveness. The shutdown reduces output from an aluminum trailer manufacturer, but the article provides no volume data, making broader aluminum price impact unclear.

Norsk Hydro reported slightly better-than-expected third-quarter core profit, citing higher aluminium and alumina prices, lower raw material costs and favourable currency effects. The company highlighted a challenging downstream market but ongoing strength from upstream drivers. Platts Alumina Index rose to $562/tonne as global alumina supply tightened.

Reliance Steel & Aluminum’s earnings call said metals pricing declined more than expected in Q3. Management highlighted aluminum price weakness tied to global market correction and an abundance of low-priced U.S. imports, after effects of Russian sanctions. The company reported lower average selling prices per ton and expects continued choppy demand/pricing near term.

The US Treasury and IRS finalized Section 45X tax credit rules, expanding eligible definitions and confirming credit amounts for components tied to clean-energy supply chains, including critical minerals. Sources say the guidance is a “game-changer” for primary aluminium producers by reducing cost pressures and supporting modernization and stabilization in a low-price market.

Sterling Metals reported high-grade copper and molybdenum values from surface grab samples at its Copper Road porphyry target in Ontario, with an ongoing 3D IP survey to guide drilling. The article is copper-related but contains no production or reserve/timing updates, limiting any near-term price impact. | Sterling Metals reported initial Phase 1 exploration results at its Copper Road project in Ontario, with surface grab samples returning up to 15.9% Cu and 4.84% Mo, plus evidence of a ~2 km footprint of porphyry mineralization. The news is development-focused and unlikely to materially move near-term copper prices, especially beyond sentiment.

Shares of China Aluminum Corp. of China (Chalco) fell sharply after weaker-than-expected Q3 profits. Analysts attributed the miss to smelting depreciation adjustments and changes related to power surcharge and bauxite reserves. While production and sales improved sequentially, the earnings disappointment spurred short-term profit taking, pressuring aluminium-related sentiment. | Pan Global initiated an IP geophysical survey at the Bravo copper target within Spain’s Escacena Project to refine drilling locations. Gravity, soil geochemistry, and helicopter EM anomalies suggest concealed VMS sulphide mineralization possibly hosting copper. Financing is on track, but no drilling results or output changes are announced. | BeMetals announced 2024 shallow aircore drilling at Zambia’s Pangeni Copper Project, extending the Nkala Zone footprint and identifying several new copper anomalies, with further core results expected in coming weeks. The news is exploration-focused and does not indicate immediate production or market supply changes for copper.

Tomago Aluminium says it wants to shift toward renewables by 2035, but requires certainty of renewable power supply and transmission at internationally competitive prices. It produces about 600,000 tonnes of aluminium and argues for incentives, streamlined approvals, and investment support to sustain primary aluminium and enable “green aluminium” growth. | Vietnam’s trade authority said the US Department of Commerce will not impose anti-dumping duties on aluminium extrusions imported from 14 countries/territories, including Vietnam. The ITC found the US industry was not significantly injured, despite prior DOC dumping/subsidy findings, reducing tariff-driven support for aluminum extrusion prices.

West Africa’s Capesize bulk exports are accelerating, led by Guinea’s expanding bauxite shipments to China. Analysts cite rising total volumes and a sharp shift in cargo mix toward Guinean bauxite. Jefferies data shows bauxite’s global Capesize share has increased, implying stronger aluminium feedstock supply expectations.

Ball Corporation acquired Alucan to expand its European extruded aluminum aerosol and bottle packaging business, adding manufacturing facilities in Spain and Belgium. The plants aim to meet growing demand and will lightweight cans with higher recycled content, increasing aluminum packaging output and potentially supporting near-term aluminum input demand. | SolGold began geotechnical investigations at its Cascabel copper-gold porphyry project in Ecuador, including drilling and seismic assessments to refine ground conditions for mine infrastructure. The pre-feasibility plan targets 3.2Mt copper (0.60%) and schedules further feasibility work. The step is incremental and not a near-term copper supply shock. | Copper and other metals reportedly gained as the U.S. dollar weakened and markets built hopes for China stimulus. The combination tends to improve risk sentiment and expected industrial demand, generally supporting prices for industrial metals like copper, aluminium, and nickel over the coming days.

Sputnik analysis of US customs data says the US imported 798 tonnes of unprocessed Russian aluminium in September—the first time since 2023—totaling $1.9 million. It follows a prior US ban on Russian aluminium imports and related trading services imposed in April 2024, suggesting changed supply flows. | The profile notes Trump’s return to office and his stated plans for new protectionist tariffs, including a 60% customs tax on goods from China and additional duties on imports from other countries. It also recalls his earlier tariffs on steel and aluminum, suggesting a tariff backdrop supportive for aluminium prices. | Guinea halted bauxite exports from EGA’s GAC subsidiary, prompting a sharp alumina price jump to record levels in Shanghai. The government/EGA talks could prolong the suspension or affect refinery operations and bauxite availability. Given Guinea’s large bauxite share, the news is likely to support aluminium prices near term.

Canada’s steel and aluminum producers face uncertainty after Trump’s election and his campaign talk of broad U.S. tariffs. History includes 2018–2020 tariff back-and-forth between the U.S. and Canada, plus existing USMCA monitoring. Aluminum industry leaders warn another flareup and tariff-based concessions are possible, weighing on prices. | The piece notes Trump’s US election win could bring tariff hikes on China, pressuring base metals in the short term. Offsetting that, brokers see an accelerating copper deficit from electrification demand and expect aluminium supply deficits. Nickel is expected to remain weak with rising LME inventories and softer demand. | The piece links Donald Trump’s US election win to potential tariff hikes on China, a short-term headwind for base metals. It also highlights RBC’s view of accelerating copper deficits from electrification and Wilsons’ forecast of an aluminium swing to deficit as China’s supply growth is constrained. Nickel remains pressured by inventories and soft demand. | Saudi Arabia’s Ma’aden/Manara Minerals is in advanced talks to acquire a 15%–20% stake in Zambian copper assets held by First Quantum Minerals, potentially valuing $1.5–$2 billion. The deal is expected to close by year-end. Aluminium is mentioned as a strategic priority but without any specific transaction. | China will implement from Nov 15 clarified rules that widen categories for importing recycled copper and aluminium scrap, but market participants doubt near-term volumes due to unchanged copper quality requirements. Copper inflow gains are expected limited, while aluminium scrap demand is stronger; however high import prices and LME volatility curb buying. | China’s MEE scheduled new categories for recycled copper and aluminium scrap imports to take effect Nov 15, but copper quality thresholds remain strict, limiting incremental scrap inflows and keeping copper scrap discount pressure modest. Aluminium scrap demand is stronger, though high import prices and LME futures restrain near-term buying.

Trump’s re-election is seen as bullish for US aluminium, with expectations of continued tariffs and possible tighter rules of origin that could affect imports from China-linked supply chains and Latin America. Despite election volatility, aluminium remains supported by upstream supply disruptions and tight supply, lifting US and European premiums. | Jaguar Land Rover said aluminium shortages and supply disruptions have temporarily curtailed production, including a hold on 6,000+ vehicles and a 10% fall in quarterly profits. It linked issues to its unnamed aluminium supplier and notes Europe flooding halted aluminium plant output, expecting improvement later. | Metro Mining secured additional multi-cargo bauxite offtake contracts for its Bauxite Hills Mine in Queensland, raising contracted volumes for 2025–2026 and extending dealings with Xinfa Aluminium Group. It also uses contracts of affreightment with fixed charter and bunker rates, and increased loading capacity via its Ikamba terminal.

Lindian Resources signed a 10-year lease and royalty agreement for its Woula bauxite project in Guinea, with production targeted to start within 12 months. Guinea-based lessees will mine about 2 mtpa and use preferential port rights, supporting future bauxite supply and aluminium-alumina feedstock expectations. | Reuters notes Trump’s win did not recreate the 2016 copper rally. Copper traded risk-off ahead of the result, then rebounded on hopes of China stimulus tied to tariff threats. China’s $1.4 trillion debt measures disappointed bulls, triggering renewed selling. The piece also cites higher U.S. duties on Chinese aluminium. | Floods forced Novelis to declare force majeure and stop production at its Sierre, Switzerland aluminum mill in June. Jaguar Land Rover links aluminum supply constraints to weaker Q2 revenues and profits and expects recovery later in 2024, highlighting near-term tightness for aluminum supply.

Atlantic Copper, a major Spanish producer of high-purity refined copper, signed a long-term PPA with Alter Enersun to supply green electricity from a 30 MWp solar PV plant for self-consumption at its Huelva metallurgical complex. Electricity pricing risk should be reduced, improving cost stability, though the plant starts in 2026. | Nigeria signed a $1.2 billion contract with CNCEC to revamp a gas processing plant feeding a dormant aluminium smelter. The project aims to restart gas operations and support aluminium output of roughly hundreds of thousands of tons annually (potentially up to one million), improving production capacity expectations. | Nigeria signed a $1.2bn deal with CNCEC to revamp a gas processing plant at a dormant aluminium smelter. The restart of the gas plant could enable the smelter to produce roughly 300,000 tonnes annually initially, with expansion targets and power generation, improving prospects for aluminium output in Africa.

China’s base metals smelting group says using more aluminium instead of copper could help secure copper resources, given China’s higher reliance on overseas copper imports versus aluminium. The chairman cites economic advantages and calls for copper refining capacity reorganisation, but no immediate supply disruption is stated. | Ge Honglin of China’s nonferrous smelting industry group said promoting aluminium use instead of copper would help conserve China’s copper resources, where imports are more dependent. He noted copper is priced far higher than aluminium and urged mergers/reorganisation of copper refining capacity, but no immediate policy or supply shock was cited.

A commodity roundup reports BMI (Fitch Solutions) lifted its 2024 aluminium price forecast to $2,450/tonne from $2,400/tonne, expecting prices to rise from the current average. Other items discuss oil demand forecasts and macro conditions, but the only direct tracked-metal change is aluminium. | The piece explains how “green aluminium” using renewable-powered, low-carbon smelting and more recycling is gaining traction. It highlights increased offtake commitments (e.g., Mitsui/Albras) and notes slight upward movements in European low-carbon value-added differentials, while demand remains cautious and premiums face some hesitation.

China’s October primary aluminium output rose 1.6% year-on-year as smelters ran at higher rates, supported by stimulus-linked demand in construction and packaging. Aluminium prices reached a five-month high on the Shanghai Futures Exchange, offsetting higher alumina and power-related costs despite tighter bauxite supply from Guinea. | Reuters reports that new alumina refineries starting up next year should ease tight alumina supplies and potentially end the record alumina price rally. Disruptions this year (Guinea/Brazil bauxite issues, Alcoa and Rio Tinto output suspensions) drove sharp alumina gains. Analysts expect a surplus in 2025 and lower prices. | Tertiary Minerals announced the start of reverse circulation drilling at its Brunton Pass copper-gold project in Nevada. Two holes target skarn/porphyry copper and two target an epithermal gold-silver/geophysical/geochemical anomaly. Results are expected in early 2025, implying no immediate near-term copper supply impact. | Premier Energies, an Indian solar cell and module maker, plans to build an aluminum frame factory with 36,000 metric tons annual capacity for captive use in its solar products. The move increases on-site aluminum demand, but the impact on broader aluminum prices within weeks is likely modest and localized. | China’s finance ministry plans to reduce or cancel export tax rebates effective Dec. 1, including scrapping rebates for aluminium and copper products. Traders expect lower Chinese exports, tightening supply abroad. Reuters reported a jump in LME aluminium prices and broader commodity moves as rebate changes affect cross-border flows. | China will reduce or cancel export tax rebates for various commodities effective Dec. 1, including scrapping rebates for aluminum and copper products. Traders expect higher export costs and lower Chinese outbound supply, which has already triggered an LME aluminium price jump and supports copper. | China’s finance ministry plans to reduce or cancel export tax rebates effective Dec. 1, including scrapping rebates for aluminium and copper products. The policy is expected to raise exporters’ costs and reduce China’s outbound metal flows, boosting near-term aluminium prices on the LME and supporting copper sentiment.

China’s Ministry of Finance announced cancellation of export tax rebates for aluminum extrusion HS codes (76042100, 76042910, 76042990). With most aluminum semi export products previously receiving a 13% rebate, the policy raises export costs, may dampen export enthusiasm, and could pressure domestic aluminium semis prices despite limited deadline-driven shipments. | Malaysia-listed aluminum stocks rose after China said it will end export-tax rebates for aluminum starting Dec. 1. Analysts expect the removal to increase Chinese aluminum export costs, potentially reducing near-term exports and tightening supply. ING also flags trade shifts toward alternative sources. The rebate change includes copper.  | China’s Ministry of Finance will end 13% export tax rebates for copper and aluminium semi-finished products from 1 Dec 2024. With weak domestic demand and tight replacement options globally, exporters’ margins fall and overseas buyers may face higher prices or limited availability. LME/SHFE initially moved differently, but shortages could support prices. | China will end a 13% export tax rebate for copper and aluminium semi products effective 1 December 2024, cutting exporter margins as domestic demand is weak. SMM expects limited ability to replace Chinese supply quickly, with potential support for LME prices, despite near-term pricing negotiations and shipping/bonded-warehouse acceleration. | Egypt Aluminum reported a sharp Q1 profit jump, attributing the surge to strong global demand and rising aluminum prices, alongside operational efficiency and production modernization. Analysts expect growth to continue as infrastructure projects boost consumption of lightweight materials, supporting near-term positive sentiment for aluminium demand.

Hydro began interim operations of onsite solar rooftop systems and battery storage at its Offenburg aluminium plant, with similar installations planned for Rackwitz and Uphusen in Germany. The projects aim to boost locally generated renewable power, smooth demand, and cut energy costs while lowering carbon footprints. Completion is targeted for early 2025. | Vital Battery Metals reported assay results from its Sting Copper Project, including a grab sample with 9.3% Cu and soil sampling showing a 300+ meter Cu–Zn–As anomaly. The update supports exploration potential but does not indicate near-term supply changes, limiting likely immediate effects on copper prices and none on nickel or aluminium. | Aluminum is reported to have steadied in October but turned slightly bearish mid-November as copper and other base metals weakened. The key near-term support is China ending export rebates for aluminum and copper from Dec 1 and elevated alumina prices amid Guinea export stoppage fears, tightening aluminum supply expectations globally.

Clark County approved an ordinance to curb illegal copper wire sales, requiring documentation for seven-strand copper wire, banning walk-up sellers, and prohibiting cash payments at scrap yards. Officials link the move to ongoing streetlight copper theft, which may tighten illicit copper flows. The article also cites continued aluminum substitution and replacement costs. | Tomago Aluminium’s CEO says switching to low-carbon electricity later this decade is not achievable due to renewable power being too expensive and in short supply. Without government assistance, the smelter near Newcastle faces ongoing operating risk as its current electricity contract with AGL Energy expires at end-2028, derailing emissions plans. | Electra Battery Materials received a ten-year exploration permit from the US Forest Service covering 91 drill pad locations for its Idaho Cobalt Belt projects, including the Iron Creek copper-cobalt deposit. The permit adds regulatory certainty to resume exploration, but the news is more developmental than an immediate supply change. | Vedanta Aluminium agreed with GAIL Gas to supply natural gas to its Jharsuguda, Odisha smelter via a short pipeline. The switch is intended to cut annual emissions by about 47,292 tonnes CO2e and reduce carbon footprint, supporting operations and India’s aluminium demand. Gas supply begins soon with defined volumes. | The piece argues that Fed rate cuts and global stimulus after the election are reviving risk appetite and boosting commodities-linked stocks. It cites strong moves in metals/mining equities and mentions copper resilience. Overall, it frames an environment likely to support near-term expectations for copper prices and, indirectly, aluminium sentiment.

Ivanhoe Mines announced the official reopening of the Kipushi zinc-copper-germanium mine in the DRC. The restart is expected to bring additional copper supply from the existing Kamoa-Kakula/Kipushi complex over time. Ceremonial confirmation supports near-term optimism on copper output and project ramp-up. | Ivanhoe Mines says the DRC president officially reopened the Kipushi mine, restarting operations at the ultra-high-grade zinc-copper-lead-germanium site. The ceremony follows a resumption of production after prior shutdowns, signaling improved near-term mining supply prospects, particularly for copper from Kipushi’s portfolio.

Rusal says it will curtail aluminium production by 250,000 tons, citing record-high alumina prices and weaker macro conditions that keep aluminium prices from rising proportionally. Rusal also faces higher costs from buying over a third of required alumina overseas. Demand is shrinking in domestic construction and automotive sectors. | Aldebaran announced an updated mineral resource estimate for its Altar copper-gold project in Argentina, showing a substantial increase versus 2021, including 22.01 billion pounds of copper. The company also plans the next study work (PEA in Q2 2025), adding near-term development optimism for copper.

The article says new U.S. tariffs on Canada and Mexico could raise aluminum prices because Canada is a key, low-cost aluminum supplier to the U.S. Other market notes mention copper falling amid stronger USD and tariff pledges, implying mixed near-term pressure across aluminum (upward) versus copper (downward). | The piece reports that Trump’s proposed 25% tariff would raise US prices for aluminium (and steel). By increasing the cost of imported metal and tightening supply availability in the short term, the policy is expected to push aluminium prices higher in the near-term market outlook.

Norsk Hydro’s Capital Markets Day emphasizes accelerating its low-carbon aluminium strategy—growing recycling and extrusions, investing in renewable power, and outlining cost-improvement programs. It highlights historically high alumina prices supporting higher aluminium prices and provides guidance on working capital and margin resilience amid weaker downstream demand. | Hydro Capital Markets Day outlines an accelerated 2030 strategy focused on aluminium recycling, extrusions, and renewable power. The company launches a NOK 6.5 billion improvement program, reports growing sales of its low-carbon aluminium products, and phases out battery and green hydrogen activities amid weak market conditions. | Odisha’s government says it has no plans to allow Adani or any other company to conduct bauxite mining in Gandhamardan hills. Ministers deny allegations of permission for mining, citing land acquired for compensatory afforestation related to earlier coal project impacts. Opposition questions bauxite mining; the government reiterates the hills won’t be destroyed. | Trump’s proposed 25% tariffs on all goods from Canada and Mexico, alongside a separate 10% tariff on China goods, are expected to disrupt the US aluminium supply chain. The US is highly reliant on Canadian unwrought aluminium, so tariffs could raise input costs for manufacturers and exporters, with analysts warning of adverse impacts on consumers and demand. | Arrow Minerals reported positive initial drilling results at its Niagara bauxite project in Guinea, confirming bauxite mineralization. Guinea is a major global bauxite supplier to China and is pushing local processing. The discovery may strengthen future supply expectations, but it is still pre-resource estimation.

Rusal will reduce annual primary aluminium production by about 6% (250,000 tonnes) as alumina prices have doubled to above $700/ton, lifting cash costs above normal levels, while domestic demand weakens. The decision follows alumina supply disruptions and should tighten aluminium supply expectations near term.

Indonesia is seeking investors to fund delayed bauxite downstreaming and smelter development, after earlier plans were hindered by financing and several smelter projects were postponed. With a June 2023 ban on raw bauxite ore exports and only four of 12 smelters ready, the push to accelerate processing may support aluminium supply expectations. | Indonesia is seeking new investors to accelerate bauxite downstreaming after funding constraints delayed several smelter projects. Indonesia has banned raw bauxite exports since June 2023, but only four of 12 planned bauxite smelters are ready. The slower progress could influence near-term aluminium production expectations.

The EPA denied a Sierra Club petition objecting to the Clean Air Act Title V permit renewal for Warrick Newco LLC, an Alcoa-linked aluminum production plant in Indiana. The petition raised restart/permit compliance concerns for potlines #2 and #6, but EPA said it was a forward-looking compliance issue rather than a current permit-writing problem.

Nam Định Province approved a US$90 million aluminium alloy factory in Mỹ Thuận Industrial Park, to produce green aluminium alloy bars, electronic materials and automotive components. Capacity is 183,000 tonnes/year, with construction expected to finish in Q3 2027 and trial/official operations in late 2027. | VR Resources says drilling has begun at its Empire copper–gold project in Ontario, targeting strong IP (chargeability) anomalies identified by a recent DCIP survey and related EM conductivity features. Results are preliminary and no resource or production changes are disclosed, limiting near-term implications mainly to copper sentiment. | China’s Ministry of Finance canceled a 13% VAT refund for exported aluminum and copper semi-finished products effective Dec 1. Buyers rushed shipments ahead of the deadline and the LME aluminum price reportedly surged about $200/ton, while SHFE fell, reflecting immediate supply-chain cost pressure and uncertainty.

Lincoln Minerals says it re-analysed historical drill cores at its Minbrie project and found a previously unassayed high-grade sulphide intercept, including 12m at 1.4% copper (plus significant lead and zinc). Management is reworking the 2011 campaign and plans follow-up drilling, which is copper-positive for sentiment. | Rio Tinto partnered to study a low-carbon greenfield aluminium project in Kokkola, Finland, using its AP60 smelting technology and sourcing low-fossil electricity with partners including Fortum. If successful, it could be the first continental Europe primary aluminium project in 30 years, boosting low-carbon aluminium expectations. | Rio Tinto partnered with Vargas, Mitsubishi and others to study a low-carbon aluminium greenfield project in Kokkola, Finland. Rio Tinto will support with its AP60 smelting technology, aiming for the first AP60 deployment in a smelter outside Quebec. Arctial will run feasibility and environmental assessments. | Rio Tinto said it expects 2025 copper output of 780,000–850,000 tonnes, up from its 2024 range, and also projected higher aluminium production. It outlined growth plans and signed a joint-venture deal for the Winu copper-gold project. The update supports near-term supply expectations. | Rio Tinto is partnering with Vargas, Mitsubishi and others to study a low-carbon aluminium project in Kokkola, Finland, using its AP60 technology and low-carbon energy supplied with Fortum. Feasibility and environmental assessments are planned. If developed, it would be continental Europe’s first primary aluminium project in 30+ years. | Rio Tinto, together with Vargas, Mitsubishi and other partners, is exploring a low-carbon greenfield aluminium project in Kokkola, Finland, using its AP60 efficient smelting technology. The Arctial partnership will run feasibility and environmental impact assessments. The news may support expectations around aluminium supply and demand for low-carbon metal. | The piece highlights China’s planned cancellation of export tax rebates for several products, including aluminum and copper, effective Dec. 1. The change already lifted LME aluminum prices as traders expect reduced Chinese export flows and tighter global supply. | The piece highlights China’s decision to cancel export tax rebates for products including aluminum and copper starting Dec. 1. It says the announcement already boosted aluminum prices on the LME, with traders expecting curtailed Chinese exports and reduced global supply. It also cites supportive near-term demand in packaging and autos. | The article discusses China’s decision to cancel export tax rebates for several products, including aluminum and copper, effective December 1. It states the announcement had an immediate market impact, with LME aluminum prices surging as traders expect reduced Chinese export flows and lower global supply. | The piece highlights China’s decision to cancel export tax rebates for several products, including aluminium and copper, effective December 1. It states the announcement had an immediate impact, with aluminium prices surging on the London Metal Exchange. Traders expect reduced Chinese exports, tightening supply and supporting higher prices. | Insider Monkey highlights China’s decision to cancel export tax rebates for several products, including aluminium and copper products, effective Dec 1. It notes the announcement already pushed aluminium higher on the LME and traders expect fewer Chinese exports and tighter global supply, supporting higher prices. The piece also discusses recycling demand trends. | The article discusses China’s move to cancel export tax rebates for several products, including aluminum and copper, effective December 1. It says the announcement already boosted LME aluminum prices and traders expect reduced Chinese export supply, tightening global availability and supporting higher prices near term. | The piece notes China’s impending cancellation of export tax rebates for aluminum (and copper products), effective Dec 1, and says the announcement immediately lifted LME aluminum prices as traders expect fewer Chinese exports and tighter global supply. It also discusses Rio Tinto’s aluminum smelter acquisitions and demand outlook for aluminum. | Insider Monkey discusses Alcoa but highlights a key China policy change: cancellation of export tax rebates (including for aluminum and copper products) effective Dec 1. The announcement immediately pushed LME aluminum prices higher as traders expect reduced Chinese exports and tighter global supply, supporting near-term price strength for aluminum. | The piece discusses China’s November 29 cancellation of export tax rebates starting Dec 1, explicitly including aluminum and copper products. It notes the change immediately boosted LME aluminum prices, with expectations for reduced Chinese exports and tighter global supply. It also cites ongoing demand strength for aluminum in packaging and transportation.

SMM’s conference takeaway highlights copper: weak macro but improving liquidity signals, and notably tightening copper concentrate conditions (negative spot concentrate TCs, worse smelting economics) with expectations of supply constraints. It reports copper inventory buildup yet argues copper prices are more likely to rise than fall; aluminum shows higher costs, export policy changes, and lower volatility. | SMM’s conference takeaway highlights that copper prices are more likely to rise than fall, citing weakening spot concentrate terms, deteriorating smelting profits, and a tightening concentrate outlook alongside demand support from power/new energy. Aluminum is described as facing higher costs and export/trade shifts, but with volatility expected lower than copper. | Rio Tinto and partners launched the Arctial project to study a low-carbon greenfield aluminium smelter in Finland’s Kokkola. The work includes feasibility and environmental assessments and plans to use AP60 aluminium smelting technology with carbon-free electricity. If successful, it could add primary aluminium capacity in Europe after decades. | Interros, Nornickel’s largest shareholder, objected to Rusal’s London lawsuit aimed at influencing Nornickel’s dividend policy. The dispute could force sensitive disclosures under geopolitical conditions. Nornickel is a major nickel producer, while Rusal uses dividends to service debt; however, no immediate production or trade changes are cited. | Canada’s parliamentary budget officer estimates tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles plus steel and aluminum imports will raise $473M in federal revenue over five years. Since October, Ottawa has imposed a 100% tariff on Chinese-made EVs and a 25% tariff on Chinese steel and aluminum imports, affecting aluminum supply and pricing expectations. | Canada’s Parliamentary Budget Officer estimates tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles plus 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum will raise federal revenues by $473M over five years. The report says aluminum imports from China should fall about 50% after the tariffs began, potentially tightening supply and supporting aluminum pricing. | Canada’s Parliamentary Budget Officer estimates tariffs on Chinese EVs, aluminum and steel will raise revenue by $473 million over five years and cut aluminum and steel imports from China by about 50%. The 25% aluminum tariff reduces Chinese inflows, likely tightening local supply conditions for aluminum. | Canada’s Parliamentary Budget Officer projects federal revenue gains from tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles, and 25% tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum imports. The PBO estimates aluminum imports from China fall about 50%. By reducing Chinese supply flows into Canada, the policy is likely to support aluminium pricing in the near term. | Canada’s parliamentary budget officer estimates tariffs on Chinese EVs plus 25% duties on steel and aluminum will raise federal revenue by $473M over five years. The report says the measures cut Chinese aluminum imports by about 50%, potentially tightening near-term aluminum availability and supporting prices.

Marvel Discovery Corp. says it is returning to Ontario’s Blackfly gold project and Quebec’s Duhamel nickel-copper-cobalt project, discontinuing uranium options. The release cites historical Ni/Cu/Co mineralization and corridor potential but provides no production or definitive near-term supply changes, implying limited immediate impact on copper/nickel prices.

U.S. Customs and Border Protection says it will seize and restrict aluminum products from Kingtom Aluminio, a Chinese-owned firm in the Dominican Republic, after finding forced-labor and related labor-rights violations. The action applies at all U.S. ports of entry, potentially tightening aluminum supply entering the market.

South32 has pulled output guidance for its Mozambique aluminium smelter, signaling uncertainty around near-term aluminium supply. In the coming days to weeks, traders may price a potential reduction or disruption to smelting output, which can weigh on aluminium prices as expectations for market balance shift. | South32 reportedly pulled output guidance for its Mozambique aluminum smelter. The update suggests weaker near-term production prospects, which can reduce anticipated aluminum supply and tighten market expectations. Such guidance changes often influence short-term pricing by shifting balance estimates for the next days to several weeks.

ING flags worsening 2025 copper headwinds after Trump’s election, citing potential US tariffs and a stronger dollar that could depress industrial demand. It points to weak China property consumption, high exchange stocks, and a refined surplus. Aluminium is mentioned as relatively advantaged, but macro pressure remains bearish for both. | India’s government is considering an overhaul of an “inverted duty structure” for aluminium ahead of next year’s Union Budget. The industry proposes lowering import duties on calcined and raw petroleum coke, while increasing duties on finished aluminium products to shield local manufacturers and improve margins. | Rio Tinto and partners signed an agreement to study a low-carbon greenfield aluminum project in Kokkola, Finland, using Rio’s AP60 smelting technology and carbon-free electricity sources. A feasibility study and environmental assessment will follow. If successful, it could restart primary aluminum production in continental Europe after 30 years.

Malaysia’s Dewan Rakyat was told that the environmental impact assessment for a proposed bauxite mining project in Bukit Goh, Kuantan has not been approved, and a similar prior EIA was rejected. The ministry said it is reviewing and strengthening the EIA process, affecting potential bauxite supply. | Barksdale Resources said its Sunnyside drilling in Arizona intercepted copper-dominant semi-massive sulfides in a CRD-style target, including chalcopyrite-rich intervals, with drilling continuing deeper. Assays are pending, but the copper-rich discovery and proximity to known mineralized districts could support near-term copper sentiment. | Aughinish Alumina (Limerick Alumina Refining), the largest European alumina refinery, reported $113.6m pre-tax losses in 2023 due to higher costs, expensive natural gas, and bauxite supply issues. Sanctions and “self-sanctioning” risks around UC Rusal threaten financing and volumes, though profitability is forecast to return in 2024-25. | Rio Tinto said its fair value estimate rose about 4%, attributing the increase to higher iron ore and aluminum prices. While iron ore is not tracked here, the mention of stronger aluminum pricing suggests supportive conditions for aluminium markets in the near term.

Malaysia’s Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister said no EIA approval has been given for a proposed bauxite mining operation in Bukit Goh, Kuantan, and a previous similar EIA was also rejected. The decision delays/blocks bauxite output, potentially tightening near-term aluminium supply expectations. | South32 says Western Australia’s environment minister has tentatively backed extending the Worsley Alumina project, overruling objections from the state’s Environmental Protection Agency. The dispute involves approval to extend bauxite mining in jarrah forests to supply feedstock for the refinery, after tighter environmental rules. | The International Aluminium Institute reports that global aluminium output rose to 111.2 Mt in 2023 while total industry GHG emissions were essentially stable versus prior year. Emissions intensity fell 2.33% to 10.04 tCO2e per tonne. The industry claims decoupling of growth from emissions with third consecutive production growth year. | South32 said Australia’s federal government will decide early next year on the proposed expansion of its Worsley alumina refining operation in Western Australia. The company appealed prior conditions recommended by regulators, citing operating challenges, and amendments have now been recommended by an appeals committee. Decision timing could affect alumina and thus aluminium sentiment. | WA’s Environment Minister approved changes to conditions for South32’s Worsley Alumina expansion, overturning parts of the EPA’s recommended requirements after appeals. Opponents cite weaker greenhouse-gas oversight and environmental offsets, while South32 views it as a positive step toward expected further approvals, supporting supply expectations for alumina-linked aluminium.

Some Japanese aluminium buyers agreed to pay a premium of $228/mt over the benchmark price for January–March shipments, up 30% from Q4. This fourth consecutive quarterly premium increase is above $175/mt in Oct–Dec, and Japan’s import premiums are a key regional pricing yardstick tied to LME cash. | Japan buyers reportedly agreed to 30% higher aluminium premiums for Q1, according to sources. Higher agreed premiums usually reflect improved demand/availability balance and can translate into stronger near-term aluminium pricing expectations as premiums are closely linked to physical market tightness. | Alumina prices hit record highs in 2024 after production stoppages and disrupted supply routes, with force majeure affecting Australian alumina output and bauxite shipments from Brazil and Guinea. Tight alumina supplies are already causing aluminium curtailments, such as Rusal’s production cut, implying downward pressure on aluminium prices until supply improves. | Ball and Dabur launched Réal Bites juice in fully recyclable aluminium cans in India, highlighting longer shelf life and sustainability versus other packaging. The announcement points to continued growth in India’s juice category and expected can substrate growth, implying incremental aluminum packaging demand, but without large, measurable supply-demand effects for near-term prices.

Chevron has signed a 10-year natural gas supply deal with Alcoa to provide 130 petajoules from the Gorgon and Wheatstone LNG projects starting in 2028. Alcoa will use the gas for alumina refineries in Western Australia, supporting reliable energy costs and expectations for aluminium supply and demand momentum. | A ResearchAndMarkets report on Saudi Arabia’s aluminum windows and doors market forecasts growth, citing Vision 2030, Sakani affordable housing, and rising demand for energy-efficient thermally broken aluminum. It also notes smart/automated and customized product trends, implying increased aluminum demand from residential and commercial construction.

The Aluminum Association released a U.S. policy brief urging stronger targeted trade enforcement against subsidized Chinese aluminum imports, improved recycling/EPR programs to boost scrap availability, and a national strategy supported by clean energy and tax incentives. The measures are intended to secure supply and competitiveness as demand grows.

South32 says safety at Mozal Aluminium is intact and road blockages are largely cleared, allowing alumina trucking to resume. However, civil unrest could disrupt trucking, prompting further amperage reductions or potline curtailment. Continued exports are noted, but supply risk remains near term for aluminium. | Sumitomo Chemical will sell its stake in Brazilian smelter Nippon Amazon Aluminium and stop reselling imported aluminium ingots, citing volatile margins from international conditions. The move contributes to a supplier–buyer power shift, keeping Japanese premiums elevated; January–March premiums reportedly rise to $228/ton over LME cash. | South32 said road blockages in Mozambique have largely cleared, enabling it to safely truck alumina from port to the Mozal Aluminium smelter and rebuild alumina stocks over the coming days if conditions stay favourable. Previously, unrest disrupted transport, prompting reduced amperage and potential potline curtailment.

Runaya and Eckart will build a sustainable aluminium powder facility in Orissa to produce spherical atomized aluminium granules and aluminium pigments. The venture plans to use recycled aluminium and renewable energy, targeting high-end uses such as aerospace and solar panels. It points to growing aluminium powder demand and incremental production capacity. | Bangkok Post highlights Thailand’s Aluminium Loop, a closed-loop system to collect and recycle aluminium cans into new cans. It cites large can manufacturing and plans to expand collection dramatically, supported by recycling and rolling within a tight radius. The initiative aims to boost recycled aluminium supply and reduce costs. | NALCO’s outlook highlights that global aluminium prices have benefited from China’s withdrawal of an export tax rebate, alongside supply disruptions in bauxite. With alumina and aluminium prices recovering in 1H25, the report expects recovery momentum to support aluminium price levels near term. | Sumitomo Chemical said it is exiting its aluminium smelting and related resale businesses. By reducing its role in producing aluminium, the move could tighten near-term supply conditions and pressure aluminium prices lower, though the overall market impact depends on the size and timing of the exit.

China’s primary aluminium net imports fell 23.5% YoY in November to about 132,000 mt, with losses on imports widening above 2,000 yuan/mt after cancellation of aluminium semis export tax rebates from Dec 1. Suppliers diverted ingots elsewhere, and SMM expects December net imports to decline further.

China’s Ministry of Finance said it will cut import tariffs on ethane and selected recycled copper and aluminum raw materials starting Jan. 1, 2025. Provisional rates below most-favored-nation levels will apply to 935 commodities. Lower costs and improved import economics may boost near-term demand for recycled metal inputs. | China’s Ministry of Finance will reduce import tariffs on ethane and selected recycled copper and aluminium raw materials starting Jan. 1, 2025. Provisional tax rates will apply to 935 commodities at below the most-favored-nation rates, potentially lowering landed costs and supporting near-term market sentiment for copper and aluminium.

China’s SPIC plans to start building a major alumina refinery in Guinea this year, targeting 1.2 million tonnes of alumina capacity, with completion by end-2027. The project includes power generation and adds to Guinea’s push for on-site processing, alongside other alumina refinery agreements.

Australian resources fund managers highlight copper and bauxite among top picks as China is expected to roll back restrictive policy measures and the incoming US administration plans tax cuts. The piece argues these moves should support global growth and commodity prices after a volatile year marked by sharp iron ore swings. | Eckart and Runaya formed a joint venture to build an aluminum powder/granule plant in Orissa, India, producing spherically atomised aluminium granules for aerospace, solar and effect pigments. The venture will use recycled aluminum and renewable energy, targeting India’s growing demand, but near-term supply impact appears limited. | Guinea, the world’s major bauxite exporter, is pressuring firms to build local refineries because it has limited alumina capacity. A deal with SPIC targets a bauxite-to-alumina plant producing about 1.2 million tons of alumina/year, alongside earlier bauxite export halts and power-plant support. | China will cut import tariffs effective Jan 1, 2025, including on recycled copper and aluminium raw materials. The Finance Ministry says the move aims to lower production costs and spur green development and domestic demand. This should support import availability and market sentiment for copper and aluminium over coming weeks. | China will cut import tariffs effective January 1, 2025, including on recycled copper and recycled aluminum raw materials. The Ministry of Finance said lower tariffs will reduce production costs, encourage innovation, and expand domestic demand tied to low-carbon, “green development” goals—supportive for both copper and aluminium market expectations.

MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration membrane that selectively captures over 99% of aluminum ions from cryolite waste streams and could allow recovered aluminium to be returned to production. The work is demonstrated in lab experiments and suggests future scale-up, aiming to reduce hazardous waste and mining needs for growing aluminium demand.

Brimstone says it will build a first-of-a-kind US plant to produce smelter-grade alumina from domestically sourced rocks, supported by nearly $8.7m in DOE funding. The project targets reducing reliance on imported bauxite and strengthening the US aluminium supply chain, potentially improving aluminium market sentiment. | Vietnam’s Đắk Nông province is attracting about US$8 billion for bauxite mining and processing, with plans for four new factories under government mineral planning. The province cites significant bauxite reserves and coordination to remove planning barriers, potentially improving near-term aluminium supply expectations. | Vitol plans to cut LME storage costs by withdrawing significant aluminium from Port Klang warehouses, leading to a sharp rise in cancelled warrants. A Cato researcher argues tariffs give domestic producers cover to raise pricing despite weak demand. | CNN reports that newly enacted 25% tariffs on aluminum and steel are quickly raising costs for craft brewer TailGate in Nashville. The brewery receives immediate price increase notices from suppliers for aluminum-based inputs like cans, and expects some pass-through to consumers while trying to protect margins. | MTM Critical Metals and Vedanta signed a non-binding MOU to test flash joule heating for recycling bauxite red mud (alumina waste). The notice emphasizes trade-compliance changes and could tighten aluminum supply from abroad, supporting higher near-term aluminium price expectations. | The EU plans to retaliate against U.S. steel and aluminum tariffs imposed March 12.
