The measure would use warnings, orders, and fines to protect recycling operations and community routines. | A leaked CBAM annex details provisional embedded-emissions benchmark calculations for EU imports of aluminium, including route-based distinctions between primary and secondary (scrap-heavy) material. The benchmarks are largely aligned with expectations, and market reaction was muted due to remaining uncertainty around default country values, so near-term impact on aluminium prices appears limited. | The PRNewswire piece on the aluminum bare wire conductor market cites tariffs that pushed primary aluminum prices higher, raising input costs for wire manufacturers and affecting trade flows (reduced imports from China, shifts in Canadian exports). It also notes some domestic smelter/refinery gains, but aluminum remained above pre-tariff levels by 2025. | Stäubli Electrical Connectors launched an enhanced BosCon connector for large-format aluminum PV trunk cables in utility-scale PV systems, including eBOS connections to combiner/disconnect boxes and inverters, plus BESS. The cULus-listed 1500V-rated connector targets safer high-current outdoor installations using 4/0 AWG through 600 MCM aluminum. | The European Commission is preparing measures to restrict aluminium scrap exports due to record outflows and domestic recycling feedstock shortages. EU scrap exports hit 1.26 Mt in 2024, aided by US primary aluminium tariffs that favor scrap. Producers seek export duties; alternatives include licensing or recycled-content targets. | Nippon Light Metal Holdings will develop an aluminum supply chain using recycled scrap sourced from its partner in India, shipping the material to its Kambara complex in Japan. The firm targets mass production using scrap-derived aluminum in fiscal 2026 for auto parts, implying longer-dated supply adjustments rather than immediate price moves.

Weekly review says aluminum scrap prices followed primary aluminum lower, shifting the scrap price center down. Despite softer primary prices and weaker wrought demand, low scrap-yard inventories and tight supply limited downside. Secondary ADC12 costs eased but tight raw material supply and cautious buyers support consolidation near 21,200–21,600 yuan/mt. | Trump signed an executive order that could allow Commerce to reduce Canada’s steel and aluminium import tariffs from 50% to 25% if conditions are met. Renta 4 estimates the tariff cut could improve ArcelorMittal’s EBITDA by up to $300 million annually, supporting a more constructive near-term outlook. | Another fire broke out at Novelis’ aluminum plant in Oswego, New York, which supplies Ford’s aluminum-intensive F-150 truck line. The plant had largely halted production after an earlier blaze, and Ford already revised forecasts for a gross hit of up to $2 billion. Restart plans may be delayed. | A five-alarm fire hit Novelis’ aluminum sheets facility in Scriba, New York, with dispatch audio indicating the cold mill as the source. The plant supplies 40% of U.S. automaker aluminum sheet demand, and this is the third fire in two months, compounding production disruption risk for the sector. | A second fire broke out at Novelis’ Oswego, New York aluminum plant after prior blazes that suspended operations and affected Ford output. The facility supplies automotive-grade aluminum for vehicles like the F-150. Although the latest fire is out, damage assessment is pending, raising short-term aluminium supply risk.

Century Aluminum, a major US aluminium producer, has urged Trump’s trade official to list Australia’s $22.7 billion “Future Made in Australia” industrial policy as a foreign trade barrier. The company argues the subsidies unfairly help Australian rivals and could shift competition in global aluminium markets, potentially affecting near-term expectations. | American Industrial Partners plans to sell or list Aluminium Dunkerque, France’s largest aluminium smelter producing about 300,000 tonnes/year. A union official links interest to the plant’s 10-year power contract with EDF, improving cost visibility. Potential offers from major firms are cited, but no deals are confirmed. | A second fire broke out at Novelis’s aluminum factory in Oswego, New York, supplying automakers, after an earlier fire had already interrupted Ford pickup and SUV production. The plant said the morning fire is under control with workers evacuated, but crews remain onsite, indicating possible continued output disruption. | A major fire struck Novelis’ aluminum plant in Scriba for the second time in recent months, disrupting production in the hot mill and roll shop sections. Firefighting crews worked for hours and damage assessment is pending. Given Novelis’ role supplying sheets to automakers, further delays could worsen near-term aluminum supply tightness. | A five-alarm fire struck Novelis’ aluminum hot mill and roll shop in Scriba/Oswego County, forcing evacuation and fire-watch operations. Damage is not yet fully assessed, and the article notes it follows a September fire that disrupted auto supply chains and sheet production. Restart timing may slip, affecting near-term aluminum sheet supply to automakers. | Monster Beverage said US tariffs on imported aluminum for beverage cans have raised the Midwest aluminium premium and increased supplier can costs. The 50% Section 232 duty is cited as the main driver of record premiums. Monster expects only modest tariff impact on earnings, using price actions and mitigation strategies. | Ford reaffirmed its annual EBIT guidance after a new fire at Novelis’ New York aluminum plant. The September blaze had already disrupted production of Ford’s aluminum-bodied trucks and led to a prior profit forecast cut. Ford paused F-150 Lightning output and expects partial offsets next year, but timelines remain unchanged. | Novelis reopened its Oswego County aluminum plant after a five-alarm fire damaged a hot mill section, resuming cold mill and heat-treatment operations and restarting shipments to Ford. The outage had disrupted automotive supply chains, and the company is using alternate sources while rebuilding the hot mill. | Novelis’ Scriba, New York aluminum-sheet plant suffered another fire, its third in two months, starting in the hot mill. The facility supplies about 40% of US automotive aluminum sheets and may stay offline until next month. Automakers, including Ford, face further production delays. | Israeli competition authorities oppose proposed anti-dumping levies on aluminum profiles imported from China, arguing the conditions for dumping, injury, and causal link are not proven and that falling demand likely explains local damage. The dispute may affect final policy; if levies are avoided, pressure for higher aluminum-related construction costs should ease. | Novelis resumed operations at its aluminum plant in Scriba after a fire on Nov. 20, following two earlier fires in September and October. The plant supplies Ford’s aluminum for F-150 pickups, and supply constraints may raise costs and reduce availability. Repeated disruption increases short-term aluminum supply risk.

Alcoa withdrew part of its Perth Hills bauxite exploration plans after community and stakeholder opposition focused on drinking water security and jarrah forest impacts. The company still has other mine and alumina-expansion proposals before the WA EPA, but the decision reduces near-term potential incremental bauxite/alumina supply. | SMM reports China’s aluminium operating capacity growth in 2025 is limited, though production still expected to rise modestly (1.7% YoY) after resumption from technological transformations and prior cuts. Globally, several smelter restarts add capacity, but temporary outages (e.g., Iceland power/equipment failure) offset. Overall impact on prices is mixed. | SMM reports Southeast Asia secondary aluminium scrap and recycled alloy prices rose through October-November alongside LME aluminium strength, while flooding from the northeast monsoon restricted scrap trading in Thailand and Malaysia. Higher scrap costs raised ADC12 production costs, reducing purchases. Traders increasingly stockpile for 2026 CBAM/tariff-driven price expectations. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) said it has produced 50 million tonnes of cast aluminium since 1979 and sold 2.74 million tonnes in 2024. It also highlighted future recycling and planned new US primary capacity, but provided no immediate supply or demand disruption. | Ford reiterated its full-year EBIT outlook after a second fire at Novelis’ Oswego, New York aluminium plant. The earlier October incident had implied up to $2bn impact, with planned recovery via higher truck output. The latest fire on 20 November was contained and operations resumed, but mitigation relies on alternate supplies and may affect aluminium deliveries. | The piece forecasts aluminum moving toward a 2025 deficit due to China capacity constraints, recovery in demand from renewables and EVs, and US tariffs supporting domestic prices. It also expects nickel prices to face pressure from a large 2025 surplus driven by Indonesian production and softer battery-driven demand, leading to mixed metal signals. | The piece forecasts aluminium trending toward a 2025 deficit, supported by China capacity constraints, recovering demand from renewables and EVs, and US steel/aluminum tariffs boosting domestic prices. It also expects nickel prices to face pressure from a large 2025 surplus driven by Indonesia and softer battery-sector demand.

A fire at Novelis’ Oswego, New York aluminum plant shut down the hot mill, suspended shipments of finished aluminum sheet, and disrupted supply to Ford and other automakers. The facility makes about 40% of US automotive-grade specialized aluminum sheet, idling Ford plants and risking revenue while recovery delays persist. | A federal jury awarded JW Aluminum $112 million after insurers denied coverage for a 2020 fire at its Goose Creek aluminum mill. Jurors found insurers breached an all-risk policy, including $32.3 million for repairs and $80 million for business-interruption losses, with possible appeal expected.

Europe is exporting record volumes of aluminum scrap, accelerated by higher U.S. tariffs on primary aluminum (scrap is exempt). Scrap shortages and idle recycling furnace capacity in Europe suggest reduced local availability. Higher demand for high-purity scrap strengthens the feedstock crunch, supportive for aluminum prices over coming weeks. | The article argues that new U.S. tariffs—especially 50% duties on steel and aluminum—raise input costs for manufacturers. It says U.S. steel prices have doubled versus other markets and aluminum prices have “soared in lockstep” with the tariffs, with no exemptions or carveouts, tightening supply and lifting near-term prices.

Secondary aluminum alloy and aluminum scrap prices stabilized at high levels this week, tracking primary aluminum. Tight scrap supply (low yard inventories, difficult recycling, and a sharp import drop) plus steady casting demand and year-end auto order front-loading supported prices. Import windows remained costly and constrained, keeping sentiment firm and volatility elevated.

The piece reviews 2025’s volatile rally in LME aluminium, noting prices have reached the highest since the 2022 Russia–Ukraine surge. It attributes support to tight primary supply near China’s ceiling, scrap supply pressure from CBAM and potential tariffs, and steadier demand, while expecting short-term upside. | The headline argues that AI-driven data centers are generating massive demand for aluminum, overwhelming the US aluminum industry. It suggests aluminum availability and production are being strained by rapid demand growth, which would typically support higher near-term aluminum prices as buyers compete for limited supply. | EGA, TAQA, DUBAL and EWEC signed agreements to decarbonise Abu Dhabi aluminium production. TAQA and DUBAL acquire Al Taweelah power and water assets, while EWEC purchases electricity under long-term PPAs and transmission capacity rises. EGA expects growing output of low-carbon “CelestiAL/MinimAL” aluminium, supporting supply and demand outlooks. | SMM data show overseas aluminium production rising and December operating rates projected to fall. The Indonesia project (PT Kalimantan Aluminium Industry) is ramping up after commissioning electrolysis cells, adding capacity and diluting global operating rates. This suggests increasing near-term aluminium supply pressure despite an Iceland smelter line outage earlier in the year. | The piece highlights divergence where oil falls but industrial metals rise. It says copper is leading, with aluminum and nickel also supported by supply disruptions and years of underinvestment in mining, alongside demand from electrification, grid upgrades and data centers. It forecasts continued strength into early 2026.

SMM expects China’s aluminum industry full costs to increase slightly month-on-month in December as electricity costs rise with the approaching dry season in hydropower-heavy regions. Prebaked anode costs are also projected to rise, offsetting a slight alumina cost decline. Power costs are forecast to dominate the increase. | EGA, Taqa and Dubal signed agreements with EWEC to decarbonise aluminium production at Al Taweelah by expanding renewable and cleaner power generation, including a new power purchase arrangement and grid interconnection capacity. The changes support future CelestiAL/MinimAL low-carbon output, but ramps begin from Q4 2025. | The article explains how the U.S. expanded Section 232 coverage through an inclusion process for steel and aluminum derivative products, starting in April/May 2025. It argues most additions are likely approved, increasing tariff reach on aluminum-bearing articles and thereby supporting aluminium-related pricing expectations through reduced import attractiveness. | The piece argues that tariffs and trade measures are changing with short notice, including detailed duties assessed on steel, aluminum, and their derivatives. It focuses on customs compliance and AI tooling for classification and enforcement, but the repeated aluminum tariff updates imply higher landed costs and likely near-term downside pressure for aluminium. | Copper is portrayed as facing a widening structural deficit driven by rapid demand growth from EVs, renewables and AI data centers, while mine supply gains remain limited. Disruptions, low ore grades, concentrate shortages and smelter output cuts (due to weak TCRCs) are tightening physical markets, keeping prices elevated.

SMM reports November alumina costs fell (full cost ~2,949 yuan/ton, cash ~2,779 yuan/ton). Entering December, alumina full costs are projected to slightly decline to 2,850–2,950 yuan/ton and cash costs to 2,700–2,800 yuan/ton, supported by weaker bauxite/caustic soda prices, but constrained by higher winter energy and a stronger USD. | The piece describes rising copper theft driven by higher copper prices and cites state bills and tighter scrap-metal rules aimed at stopping copper theft. It also notes electricity operators are replacing thousands of kilometres of copper cabling with aluminium due to theft costs. These factors can dampen copper demand expectations short term. | AGL says it continues discussions with Tomago Aluminium over energy supply as governments negotiate a plan to keep the Tomago smelter open beyond 2028. A long-term energy contract currently runs to end-2028, and an announcement is expected this month. New adjacent battery and power upgrades support supply reliability. | AGL says it continues talks with Tomago Aluminium to secure the smelter’s energy needs beyond current supply arrangements ending in 2028. With governments negotiating a plan to keep the plant operating past 2028, the discussions reduce closure risk and support expectations for aluminium output. | The article explains how the U.S. implemented an expanded Section 232 inclusions process for steel and aluminum derivative articles. BIS began accepting requests in May 2025 and is likely to add more aluminum derivative HTS codes to the tariff scope. Broader tariff coverage can reduce import competition and support aluminum prices. | The piece argues Trump’s 50% aluminum tariff is being undercut by importers using undervaluation and mislabeling to pay duties only on raw material rather than the full value of manufactured aluminum articles. It calls for enforcing the tariff based on weight to protect domestic aluminum jobs, implying reduced imports. | Aluminium prices are volatile but supported by supply tightening tied to China: output is near its capacity cap, export tax rebates were removed to cut shipments, and SHFE inventories are falling. The piece notes mixed industrial demand and some short-term easing, yet expects firm-to-rising prices from structural constraints. | AGL says it is still in discussions with Tomago Aluminium over its energy supply, as governments negotiate a plan to keep the Tomago smelter open beyond 2028. Tomago previously flagged potential closure without an economically sustainable long-term power contract. An energy battery project near the plant is also progressing.

Copper fell from an all-time peak on the LME, down 0.8% as a stronger dollar and risk-off caution weighed on dollar-priced commodities. Other base metals also declined, including aluminium and nickel. The retreat is viewed as temporary, with supply-squeeze worries and copper support above $11,000. | The piece describes frequent changes to US tariffs and trade deals with short notice, including detailed duties on steel and aluminum derivatives. It explains how customs brokers and CBP must rapidly update classifications and enforcement. These measures can increase landed costs for aluminium imports in the near term. | Copper surged to record highs as supply fears intensified: LME inventories fell sharply, warehouse withdrawals increased, and cash premiums widened alongside mine disruptions, lower output guidance, and coordinated Chinese smelter reductions. It also cites potential aluminium substitution in some power applications, but the dominant driver is tight copper physical supply. | Recycled aluminum demand is rising as energy costs keep primary smelting expensive and EU/automotive policies push higher recycled content. Producers including Novelis and Hydro are expanding recycling capacity, while China’s primary smelting cap increases reliance on scrap. These factors support aluminium market sentiment and likely near-term price strength.

London LME copper surged to record highs as a weaker dollar and tight on-warrant availability drove prices higher. The LME showed large net warehouse cancellations in Asia, pushing stocks to the lowest since July. Supply worries were reinforced by Glencore cutting 2026 copper guidance, alongside gains in other metals. | SMM reports copper plate/sheet and strip operating rates improved in November on a phased copper price pullback and downstream order release, with finished inventories slightly lower. December year-end production plans face resistance as copper hit new highs, and aluminium substitution demand increased, shrinking some cable shielding strip orders. | Rio Tinto outlined productivity and capital discipline plans alongside upgraded 2025 copper production guidance (860–875 kt) and lower unit cost guidance, implying improved supply outlook and margins. The company also targets aluminium & lithium as core businesses and expects higher output from its ramp-ups, supporting both copper and aluminium sentiment. | SMM’s survey shows China’s aluminum downstream operating rate falling WoW (to 61.9% overall; plate/sheet 65.0%; wire/cable 62.4%; extrusion 52.0%). High aluminum prices above 22,000 yuan/mt suppress spot orders and year-end stockpiling, while environmental restrictions curb output, keeping demand weak into December.

SMM reports falling operating rates across aluminum downstream processing: primary alloy steady but spot demand cautious; wire/cable, extrusion, and plate/sheet lower on weak orders and winter/off-season conditions. Aluminum prices surged above 22,000 yuan/mt, and environmental restrictions in Henan re-emerged, constraining production and deliveries. | SMM reports aluminum fluoride prices jumped 250–300 yuan/mt in early December after downstream aluminium enterprise tender prices were finalized at 10,200–10,300 yuan/mt. The increase is mainly cost-driven by rising sulfuric acid prices, while fluorite fell, slightly easing profitability pressure. Demand remains mediocre, keeping procurement weak. | Argus hosted a webinar on its newly launched US aluminium scrap price assessments for key auto scrap grades (baled clips). It states primary aluminium prices in the US have risen to record highs and that supply constraints, tariffs, and disruptions are increasing volatility in the automotive aluminium supply chain. | Constellium CEO warns the EU’s Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) could inflate costs for aluminium consumers and raise European physical premiums to a 10-month high. While not immediate, he argues the higher premiums could pressure competitiveness and eventually lead to gradual capacity decline in Europe.

Weda Bay Industrial Park (IWIP) rejected claims of nickel smuggling through its private airport in North Maluku. The firm said the shipments were permitted alumina samples sent to Jakarta for lab testing, detained temporarily due to missing documents. No seizures or investigations occurred, and samples remain under aviation security supervision. | SMM survey data shows secondary aluminum alloy operating rates rose in November but are constrained by tight aluminum scrap supply, high procurement costs, and cautious operations amid regional policy uncertainties. In December, stable demand may be offset by lower winter dismantling volumes and reduced imports, keeping scrap tight. A slight rate decline is expected. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) signed a two-year collaboration with Bahrain’s GARMCO to second technical staff for hands-on training at Alba’s facilities, focused on hydraulic and pneumatic systems. The deal is aimed at workforce development and knowledge sharing rather than changing aluminium production or trade flows. | ING expects aluminium to remain in deficit in 2026, supporting prices. China’s output is near its 45 mt capacity cap, limiting net exports, while outages and power-contract difficulties constrain restarts abroad. US inventories are very low after tariff-driven destocking, reinforcing tightness. Faster Indonesian ramp-ups are the main downside risk. | BIS will issue its decision in 2026 on nearly 100 requests to expand U.S. Section 232 national security tariffs to additional steel and aluminum derivative products. The decision was previously expected within 60 days, but multiple deadlines were missed, citing prior approvals and EU trade discussions. Near-term aluminium price impact appears uncertain.

Fastmarkets reports intensified competition in Brazil’s primary aluminium billet market as wider domestic supply and more imports enter amid stable demand. High interest rates are slowing construction, reducing billet demand pace. The CIF billet premium fell through 2025, while near-term market momentum is expected to soften further, limiting upside. | Sustainability Forum Middle East announced GARMCO as a 2026 Forum Partner. The piece highlights the company’s “Green Initiative,” including recycling and energy-efficient production, plus a clean-energy deal to build a 6 MW solar power project at its Bahrain facilities, supporting decarbonization and potentially easing aluminium production economics.

Mexico’s Chamber of Deputies and Senate approved reforms to its General Import and Export Tax Law that change aluminium tariff schedules. Duties on some primary aluminium HS codes are removed, while new levies are added for various aluminium bars, wires and structures (often around 35%). Implementation is set for 2026, affecting trade flows.

The piece is investment-strategy commentary, not a discrete supply event, but it references broker upgrades to base-metal outlooks and a more constructive stance. It highlights copper exposure as having strong fundamentals and notes Citi is most bullish on copper and aluminium, supporting near-term bullish sentiment. | Mexico’s legislature approved reforms to its import and export tax law that will change aluminium import duties starting in 2026. Tariffs on some primary aluminium HS codes are removed, while many downstream product codes face new duties (often 35%, with some at 5%). This reshapes trade flows with China, the US and others. | Weekly review shows aluminum scrap tracking primary aluminum with prices near highs. Tight imported and domestic scrap supply and continued cost support underpin ADC12, while environmental-protection production restrictions in regions like Chongqing/Henan reduce alloy operating rates and curb downstream procurement. Result: ADC12 likely remains in a high, highly volatile trading range. | The piece discusses BHP’s recent output, noting copper production up 4% to 494 kt and frames company performance as sensitive to commodity price swings. It also describes decarbonization and other projects (potash, power network) without any aluminium-specific developments that would likely move metal prices immediately. | Gamme Telco, part of Mitjavila, launched the Electra Design 3 all-aluminum solar carport for residential and commercial use. The structure fits nearly all standard PV modules without special panels and includes concealed wiring and rainwater management. The announcement suggests incremental aluminium demand but provides no volume data, limiting near-term price impact.

The PM says federal and NSW governments are finalising a multibillion-dollar support deal to keep the energy-intensive Tomago Aluminium smelter operating beyond 2028. The plan includes a long-term Snowy Hydro-linked power purchasing arrangement for competitive electricity, helping protect jobs and sustain output of about 600,000 tonnes annually. | Australia’s PM says talks are finalising a multibillion-dollar scheme to secure Tomago Aluminium’s future beyond 2028 via long-term power purchasing with Snowy Hydro. The smelter requires continuous electricity; guarantees on pricing and renewables/storage are expected to keep production around 600,000 tonnes annually. | Australia’s PM says a rescue deal is being negotiated to keep the Tomago aluminium smelter operating beyond 2028 by securing a 10-year power purchasing agreement with discounted electricity. It addresses the planned doubling of energy costs, supports jobs, and aims to keep production internationally competitive. | Australia’s PM says federal and NSW talks with Tomago Aluminium are advancing to finalize a multibillion-dollar, 10-year energy deal via Snowy Hydro to keep the Tomago smelter operating beyond 2028. The plant needs large, stable power, and the agreement aims to secure affordable renewable electricity and storage. | Australia’s PM said governments are finalising a multibillion-dollar deal to secure the Tomago Aluminium smelter’s future beyond 2028 by underwriting renewable generation, storage and transmission via a Snowy Hydro scheme. The plan aims for competitively priced electricity, keeping output running and supporting regional jobs and supply. | Australia’s PM says governments and Tomago Aluminium are finalising a multibillion-dollar scheme-backed power deal with Snowy Hydro to keep the energy-intensive Tomago smelter running beyond 2028. The agreement would underwrite renewable generation, storage and transmission at a competitive electricity price, protecting output and jobs. | Australia’s PM says federal and NSW talks with Tomago Aluminium are advancing to finalise a multibillion-dollar, 10-year energy deal via Snowy Hydro to underwrite renewable generation and storage. The move aims to keep the Hunter smelter operating beyond 2028 by securing affordable, stable electricity. | Australia’s PM says governments are finalising a multibillion-dollar arrangement to keep the Tomago aluminium smelter running beyond 2028. The deal underwrites renewable generation and storage for long-term, competitively priced power, addressing Tomago’s inability to secure a sustainable electricity contract and reducing supply disruption risk. | The federal government is finalising a deal to keep the Tomago Aluminium smelter open beyond 2028 after the firm warned rising energy prices would make operations unviable. A long-term power purchase agreement is being negotiated to lower electricity costs. The outcome is expected to support Australia’s aluminium output and jobs. | Australia’s federal government agreed to keep Rio Tinto’s Tomago aluminium smelter operating by funding cheaper power amid spiralling energy costs. The deal secures over 1,000 jobs and stabilizes output from the plant producing a large share of Australia’s aluminium, reducing short-term supply disruption risk. | Prime Minister Albanese says talks to secure Tomago Aluminium’s future are moving forward, finalising a multibillion-dollar energy deal to keep the smelter open beyond 2028. The project targets long-term renewable power and storage via Snowy Hydro, protecting output and thousands of jobs. | Rio Tinto agreed to keep its troubled Tomago aluminium smelter running after a Commonwealth-backed deal would provide subsidised electricity from Snowy Hydro and other renewables, firmed by gas. The arrangement reduces the likelihood of near-term shutdowns, supporting aluminium supply expectations and near-term sentiment. | Australia has unveiled a rescue bid for Rio Tinto’s Tomago aluminium smelter, signaling efforts to avert potential shutdown or prolonged disruption. By supporting production and containing near-term supply risk, the move is likely to be supportive for aluminium prices over the coming days to weeks. | Australia’s PM says talks to secure a multibillion-dollar energy deal for the Tomago Aluminium smelter are progressing, aiming to keep the plant operating beyond 2028. The energy-intensive facility would receive long-term power via a Snowy Hydro-linked structure, improving input cost certainty and reducing shutdown risk. | Australia’s PM says talks to secure a multibillion-dollar energy deal for Tomago Aluminium are moving forward, aiming to keep the Hunter smelter operating beyond 2028. The scheme would underwrite renewable generation and storage for about 10 years, protecting output and industrial jobs and supporting electricity affordability. | South Africa’s infrastructure is hit by copper cable theft, with Eskom reporting hundreds of transformer failures from vandalism and illegal connections. SOEW warns emergency repairs rely on cheap, non-compliant Chinese cable imports, potentially worsening blackouts and raising calls for anti-dumping and enforcement, with stolen copper often sold to foreign buyers. | Australia’s federal and NSW governments struck a deal to keep Rio Tinto’s Tomago aluminium smelter operating beyond 2028 by securing clean energy and energy price guarantees after the coal power contract ends. This avoids likely closure and preserves supply, supporting aluminium market expectations in the near term. | The Albanese government agreed to underwrite a power deal from Snowy Hydro to keep Rio Tinto’s Tomago aluminium smelter open long-term despite high electricity costs. The agreement reduces risk of closure after 2028, supporting ongoing production from Australia’s largest aluminium smelter. | Australia’s federal government announced a taxpayer-funded electricity supply deal to keep Rio Tinto’s Tomago aluminium smelter operating beyond 2028, citing high energy costs for aluminium. The subsidy via Snowy Hydro and renewables aims to prevent closure, but details on long-term competitiveness and risks remain uncertain. | Australia’s government stepped in with a deal that saved more than 1,000 jobs at the country’s biggest aluminium smelter. By averting operational disruption and potential capacity loss, the news is supportive of near-term aluminium supply expectations, which can underpin aluminium pricing over the next few weeks. | EU draft plans would tighten the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), raising expected CO2-related costs for certain imports, including unwrought aluminium. From 2026, benchmark emissions for aluminium would be set at 1.423 tCO2 per ton, increasing charges under default values for major suppliers and pressuring import demand. | Australia and NSW plan to secure Tomago Aluminium’s smelter beyond 2028 by arranging reliable, competitively priced power through a long-term fixed-price PPA and concessional financing for renewables and storage. Tomago’s 590,000 tpa capacity faces electricity contract expiry, so the move reduces supply and cost uncertainty.

Australia’s government is finalising a deal to keep the Tomago Aluminium smelter operating in New South Wales. This follows the company saying in October it struggled to see operations as commercially viable beyond 2028. The announcement precedes a gas-market review expected within weeks, relevant to smelter costs and supply. | Reuters reports China’s aluminium production rose 2.5% year-on-year to 3.79 million tonnes in November, and output for nonferrous metals including copper and nickel increased as well. Higher aluminium supply from China’s dominant producer can pressure nearby aluminium prices as markets anticipate greater availability. | Guinea and other African countries are starting multiple alumina refinery projects to expand refining capacity beyond existing bauxite supply. New builds in Boké, Boffa and planned initiatives in Ghana and Cameroon aim to grow alumina output, supported by higher aluminum demand expectations. LME alumina is cited above bauxite prices. | AKFA Aluminum Solutions plans its first U.S. plant in Bowling Green, Kentucky, a full-cycle aluminum production hub with extrusion, anodizing and finishing. The facility will use recycled aluminum billets and energy-efficient systems, expanding U.S. processing capacity and likely supporting near-term aluminum demand expectations.

SMM reports 2026 aluminum ingot long-term contract prices are broadly stable versus the SMM A00 benchmark but with regional premiums/discounts and generally lower order-signing willingness. Factors include high aluminum price volatility, weaker downstream construction demand, and limited incremental global supply, leading to tighter seller terms and declining contract volumes. | South32 said Mozal Aluminium in Mozambique will move to care and maintenance around 15 March 2026 after failing to secure a new electricity supply agreement at an internationally competitive price. The drought and power constraints from HCB and Eskom are cited. This reduces primary aluminium supply prospects and may pressure prices lower. | Guinea is reportedly in talks with Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) for a bauxite supply agreement via Guinea’s Nimba Mining after Guinea revoked EGA subsidiary Guinea Alumina’s licence. A deal could avert threatened litigation. EGA is seeking alternative ore supply; Nimba has shipped and plans renewed bauxite exports. | Rio Tinto describes its NZAS Tiwai Point aluminium smelter in New Zealand and electricity arrangements using renewable hydroelectricity that secure continued operation and low-carbon aluminium output. It also notes the smelter is largely exported. The supply outlook for aluminium is therefore more stable in the near term. | Guinea’s military-led government revoked EGA subsidiary GAC’s licence and transferred assets to Nimba Mining amid a refinery dispute. Supply-deal talks could avert threatened litigation and help secure bauxite/alumina feedstock for EGA, whose UAE refinery is engineered for GAC ore, supporting aluminium supply expectations.

AustruckX has launched the AXCS-EV, an all-aluminium curtainsider truck body engineered for electric trucks. By using lightweight aluminium alloys and replacing some steel components with alloy hardware, it cuts tare weight and increases payload. The company says orders are now open for compatible electric truck models, supporting incremental aluminium demand. | The piece links U.S. policy to near-term metals price pressure, citing higher aluminium import tariffs and proposed copper tariffs, plus sanctions that prohibit Russian-origin aluminium, copper and nickel imports from mid-April 2024. It also highlights infrastructure “Buy America” demand for copper and aluminium, boosting expectations of tighter supply and higher prices. | The piece describes a “great divergence” in commodities where industrial metals tied to the green transition outperform. It highlights copper’s strong 2025 rally driven by EVs, renewables and data centers, notes a structural aluminium deficit, and points to rising nickel demand from EV and storage growth, all supporting bullish near-term price sentiment. | The EU expands its Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism to cover 180 steel and aluminium-intensive downstream products. Importers must declare embedded CO2 and buy EU emission certificates if above EU standards. CBAM aims to level competition versus lower-standard producers and is expected to raise €1.4bn annually, with part funding for EU makers. | Vedanta Aluminium expanded its Lanjigarh alumina refinery in Odisha from 2 to 5 MTPA and reported a record 254 kilotonnes in November, with strong month-on-month growth. The increased alumina supply supports domestic smelting and reduces import dependence, improving near-term expectations for aluminium availability and costs. | South32 plans to put its Mozal aluminium smelter in Mozambique into care and maintenance around March 15, 2026 after failing to agree electricity pricing with key power suppliers. Market participants expect tighter duty-free “green” supply for Europe, pushing European aluminium premiums higher as traders bid up Rotterdam and U.S. Midwest levels.

Vedanta Aluminium said it ramped up its Lanjigarh refinery to 5 million tonnes per annum and set a new alumina production record. The company framed this as strengthening India’s aluminium self-reliance. Increased refining output can support near-term aluminium supply expectations and pricing. | SMM data show China’s proportion of liquid aluminium fell for six straight weeks to 76.34%, driven by policy-driven local alloying and limited holiday downtime, while consumption weakens in the off-season. With spot discounts widening and production expected to rise, supply gains and softer demand point to aluminium prices under pressure. | India’s AM Green and Japan’s Mitsui signed a non-binding agreement to explore investment and offtake for low-carbon green aluminium. AM Green plans a 1 MTPA solar/wind/hydro-powered smelter and a 2 MTPA alumina refinery in Andhra Pradesh. The pact is expected to support green aluminium demand signals. | Weekly review reports aluminum scrap prices hovering at highs, with tight supply and CNY pre-stocking supporting seller pricing despite some demand weakening from environmental production restrictions. ADC12 spot prices remain cost-supported and range-bound amid volatile primary aluminum and cautious downstream die-casting procurement, suggesting high-level fluctuation rather than a clear directional move. | AM Green and Mitsui signed a non-binding agreement to explore investment and offtake for AM Green’s green aluminium value chain. AM Green is developing a 1 MTPA solar/wind/hydro-powered aluminium smelter and a 2 MTPA alumina refinery in Andhra Pradesh, India, targeting low-carbon aluminium demand. | South Africa’s NTCSA says it is still negotiating a new electricity supply agreement for the Mozal aluminium smelter in Mozambique, due to expire on 15 March 2026. Mozal needs a substantially lower electricity price to remain competitive. NTCSA is working with stakeholders to avoid cost impacts on consumers while keeping talks open. | Bowling Green Fire Department units responded to a structure fire at Kobelco Aluminum early Thursday morning. The fire has been brought under control, with causes and damage still under investigation. If production was affected, the incident could tighten near-term aluminum supply expectations. | AM Green and Mitsui signed an MoU to explore investments in low-carbon aluminium, including a 1 million tpa aluminium smelter and a 2 million tpa alumina refinery powered by wind/solar and pumped hydro. The pact includes potential trading of low-carbon aluminium, but production timing is not specified.

Australia’s federal and NSW governments agreed a rescue plan for Rio Tinto’s Tomago primary aluminium smelter, threatened by rising energy costs. The plan targets a fixed-price power purchase agreement and financing for renewable energy and storage ahead of the current contract expiring in 2028, avoiding potential closure and output loss. | Copper rose toward record highs as analysts pointed to tight mine supply constraints, with Goldman reiterating a bullish long-run view and Commerzbank expecting a move toward $12,000 over the next month. Nickel gained on Indonesia’s proposed ore output reduction, while aluminium climbed after South32 said it would shut Mozal. | CESTAT Mumbai set aside customs orders that enhanced the declared assessable value of imported aluminium scrap using NIDB comparisons and a DGoV valuation circular. The tribunal held Rule 12 requires proper notice and substantiated evidence, and the circular is only a guideline. The enhancement was quashed, benefiting the importer. | U.S. CBP issued guidance on calculating Section 232 tariffs by valuing embedded steel, aluminum, and copper content. For mixed products, tariffs apply to the invoice value paid for the metal content, with limited deductions for fabrication and treatments. Copper alloying value can’t be deducted, potentially increasing landed costs and dampening demand. | CBP issued guidance on valuing steel, aluminium and copper content under U.S. Section 232 tariffs. For mixed products, duties are based on the invoice value of the contained metal, with fabrication/labor costs generally not deductible. Copper alloying elements also can’t be deducted, raising uncertainty and potentially increasing effective costs for importers. | Guinea’s bauxite exports to China surged to 127.11 million tonnes in the first 10 months of 2025. Growth in bauxite supply to alumina refineries has left them facing oversupply and depressed alumina prices, expected to persist. Market pressure is pushing cost transparency and FOB pricing for Guinea bauxite. | Kutcho Copper Corp. announced a non-brokered $1,000,000 flow-through financing to fund exploration on its Kutcho copper-zinc property in British Columbia. Proceeds will be used for Canadian exploration and critical mineral mining expenditures through Dec. 31, 2026. The release focuses on capital raising rather than near-term production changes.

The piece argues AI and data-center expansion are driving heavy copper demand (including claims of up to 50,000 tons per hyperscale site) and links rising prices to supply pressure from copper theft and infrastructure disruptions. It also discusses silver shortages and battery-related silver use, but only copper is directly price-relevant here.

SMM reports that aluminium ingot shipping backlogs in Xinjiang have largely eased, but concentrated arrivals triggered phased inventory buildup in warehouses, especially Gongyi, where withdrawals are weak due to environmental protection-related production cuts. Supply chain costs normalize after highway transport ends. Late-December inventories may rise above 600,000 mt, pressuring aluminium prices. | U.S. Q3 GDP grew 4.3% versus 3.3% forecast, keeping “higher for longer” rate expectations elevated. Meanwhile, copper surged to a record $12,031.50/ton on LME amid supply deficits from disruptions in Chile and Peru and importers stockpiling ahead of potential tariffs. Precious metals also rose sharply. | The Democratic Republic of Congo suspended artisanal copper processing and ordered entities to halt operations and certify mineral origins amid a corruption crackdown. The decree aims to improve transparency and prevent illegal exports. While cobalt supply issues are prominent, the article provides no quantified estimate of copper output changes or price effects. | Northern Health issued a ‘Do Not Consume’ order for Granisle’s water after tests found aluminium concentrations above Canadian guidelines. The authority says aluminium likely comes from coagulant leaking from treatment equipment into distribution lines. Repairs and flushing are required, with no clear timeline, and testing continues. | AKFA Aluminum Solutions plans a full-cycle aluminum production hub in Bowling Green, Kentucky, including extrusion, anodizing and finishing. The firm expects up to 100,000 tons annually and received Kentucky tax incentives to support the investment. The expansion can influence short-term aluminum supply expectations, though the ramp timeline is unclear.

Copper prices are surging toward $12,000/ton on the LME amid tight physical supply: inventories at multi-year lows, mine outages/production drops (Cobre Panama closure, lower Codelco output), and a U.S. 50% tariff on refined copper that triggered front-running and record premiums. The market expects persistent tightness and some substitution toward aluminum. | Villagers in Guinea’s Kindia region accuse an Indian company operating a nearby bauxite mine of polluting local water and reducing crop yields. With Guinea holding the world’s largest bauxite reserves, the reported environmental harm could prompt scrutiny or disruptions that affect near-term bauxite/aluminium supply expectations. | Producers of raw materials rose as recent weakness in the U.S. dollar pushed metals futures to record highs. Copper topped $12,000 a ton in London for the first time, linked to tariffs and currency-market moves. The roundup also highlights record-testing gold and higher silver, citing geopolitical hedging demand. | ALUKO Group plans an integrated aluminum manufacturing facility in Halls, Tennessee, repurposing a former Tupperware building. The plant will serve EV, solar, and construction customers and create 285 jobs, supported by state and TVA. The new capacity/demand outlook is supportive for aluminum market sentiment.

Axis International is seeking $28.9 billion in arbitration after Guinea revoked its bauxite mine permit. Guinea has tightened control over mining permits for higher revenue and local processing. The dispute highlights potential near-term disruption to bauxite supply from a major reserve holder, which can affect alumina and aluminium markets. | Copper surged to record levels near $13,000/t before paring gains on Monday as investors took profits and the LME reopened after a holiday. The rally reflects shortage worries, weaker dollar, and risk-on sentiment, with tighter US stocks and mine disruptions cited. Aluminium and nickel moved only marginally. | Indonesia’s 2025 reforms keep the bauxite export ban in place while adding annual RKAB output quotas, SIMBARA monitoring, and stronger enforcement in forest areas with sizable per-hectare fines. It also cites surging domestic demand from EV, renewable energy, and national infrastructure programs, supporting expectations for higher aluminum demand and supply discipline. | Copper prices surged to near record highs as London caught up with China and US gains. The rally was attributed to shortage worries, tighter US inventories, mine disruptions, a weaker dollar, and risk-on sentiment. SHFE also got support from reports China will curb copper capacity growth. Aluminium slightly dipped; nickel rose. | Copper prices are described as surging to record levels in 2025 due to AI-driven demand, tariff policy shocks (including a 50% tariff on semi-finished products with refined cathode exemptions), and supply disruptions from mine outages, earthquakes, mudslides, and labor blockades. Smelter margins collapse (TC/RCs to zero) intensify refined supply tightness; aluminium substitution is mentioned as limited. | Mining-related stocks fell as gold, silver and copper prices reversed lower late in 2025. The article also says CME Group increased margin requirements for metals contracts after a normal volatility review, effective Monday. Shares of Newmont, Barrick, Freeport-McMoRan and Southern Copper declined as copper dropped 4.6%.

SMM data show overseas aluminium production rose in December as Indonesia’s new smelter projects began ramping up, lowering the overall operating rate dilution effect. The Indonesian PT Kalimantan Aluminium project started commissioning in late November. Rio Tinto secured a post-2028 energy approach for Tomago, while Mozal faces a planned maintenance shutdown around mid-March. | SMM says high copper prices (LME peak, SHFE >100,000 yuan/mt) are freezing demand: operating rates across copper rod, wires/cables, sheet/strip and pipes/tubes fall, while social inventory of copper cathode surges from 159,000 mt to 238,900 mt in nine days (+70,500 mt). Aluminium substitution is beginning to be discussed. | SMM reports domestic aluminium ingot inventories rising sharply in late December and expects continued slight increases into early January 2026. Causes include resumed Xinjiang shipping, higher electrolytic production/casting volumes, traditional off-season demand weakness, and tighter environmental controls slowing warehouse withdrawals. High prices further suppress restocking, limiting inventory digestion.

London copper rose nearly 1% to $12,537/tonne, continuing into the new year after a record 2025 rally. LME aluminium was little changed but reached its highest since May 2022 near $2,998/tonne. The piece attributes support to a weaker US dollar and firmer China demand signals. | ING expects 2026 to be characterized by tightness in copper and aluminium. Copper is forecast in a large deficit due to disruptions at Grasberg, Kamoa-Kakula, and El Teniente plus tariff-related trade distortions. Aluminium should also remain in deficit from China’s capacity cap and power constraints, while nickel stays surplus. | ING’s Metals Outlook for 2026 expects a refined copper deficit (~600kt) from high demand and outages/disruptions in Indonesia, DRC and Chile, alongside tariff-driven trade flow distortions. Aluminium is also projected in deficit (~200kt) due to China’s capacity cap and power constraints. Nickel is seen in surplus, with regulatory scrutiny and softer demand. | London copper rose nearly 1% to about $12,537/tonne, after a strong 2025 and record levels, supported by a weaker US dollar and demand from AI and renewables. LME aluminium was little changed but reached its highest since May 2022 around $2,998/tonne, indicating bullish near-term sentiment. | Aluminium rose above $3,000/ton for the first time since 2022 as investors priced tighter supply after China capped smelting capacity and Europe faced output constraints from higher electricity prices, drawing down inventories. The piece also notes copper gains on tight supply and nickel strength following a Vale Indonesia mining halt. | Aluminum prices reportedly hit $3,000 for the first time since 2022, with the move attributed to supply concerns. Tighter expected availability in the near term would reduce prompt supply and typically supports higher aluminium benchmarks as traders adjust for a potential supply shortfall.

China’s NDRC policy urges tighter regulation and faster consolidation in resource-intensive sectors like alumina and copper smelting. The article describes China’s alumina overcapacity, high inventories, and rising energy/environment compliance costs, with stricter approvals and integration expected. Near term, surplus and inventory pressure point to weaker aluminium sentiment, and any copper effect is indirect. | The article discusses Venezuela’s aluminum chain amid a U.S. strike on Caracas on Jan 3 and concerns about supply disruption. Venezuela has very low operating rates and minimal exports, so even if localized disruptions occur in the bauxite/alumina/smelting cluster, near-term global aluminum impact is expected to be limited and mainly sentiment-driven. | Copper surged above $13,000/t, with the rally driven by intensifying supply concerns after a strike at Chile’s Mantoverde mine, expectations of deficits, and a sharp fall in LME-approved warehouse stocks. Aluminium also rose on worries about shortages, including China’s output cap.

Malaysia reports a surge in copper theft and vandalism linked to higher copper prices, with thieves targeting telco and power cables and other public infrastructure items. Unlicensed foreign scrap collectors offer higher cash prices, complicating enforcement and potentially tightening copper scrap supply. Aluminium scrap collection is also referenced but less directly. | SMM says alumina full costs fell 1.93% month-on-month in December, helped by a weaker US dollar, lower caustic soda prices, and slightly cheaper thermal coal. Bauxite inventories rose and long-term ore contract prices fell. January is expected to bring further cost declines, easing cash-cost pressure for many plants. | The article argues that the copper-to-gold ratio has broken out, signaling a shift toward risk-on economic momentum. It points to copper pushing above $6/lb and gaining about 18% over three months, implying improving growth expectations. It extrapolates this macro shift to potential bitcoin strength into 2026. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) said it produced a record 1.623Mt of aluminium in 2025, slightly above 2024, surpassing its annual target despite an industrial fire. The company also reported two consecutive years of LTI-free operations and plans to build on performance. | Copper hit record highs and nickel jumped to a 19-month peak as supply disruptions and delays raised concerns about near-term tightness. A strike at Capstone’s Mantoverde mine in Chile and delays to Tongling’s Ecuador project were cited. Nickel gains were reinforced by Indonesia plans to cut ore output. Aluminium rose as well. | Aluminum surged above $3,000/t on the LME, with buyers citing enforced China primary output caps, high European smelter energy costs, and “green aluminum” premiums tightening LME-grade availability. The rally is echoed by record copper and improved nickel prices, reflecting electrification-driven demand and a carbon-border-driven supply vacuum. | Early January 2026, the article links a U.S. extraction operation in Venezuela and broader monetary easing to a surge in commodities. Copper breaks $13,000/ton as AI-driven data center demand is said to have depleted inventories and left a projected 400,000-ton deficit, indicating bullish near-term price pressure.

On Jan 7, industrial metals declined as CME raised margin requirements for precious metals futures, triggering leveraged deleveraging. Investors rotated from “safe havens” into equities as AI-driven risk-on sentiment grew and the US dollar strengthened, pressuring dollar-denominated commodities including copper (notably around $4.50/lb). | Aluminum prices are rising as AI-driven data centers intensify electricity demand, pushing up power availability and costs. The report cites smelter concerns, including operations at major producers facing challenges due to soaring electricity prices. Given aluminum’s heavy electricity requirements, the market anticipates supply disruptions.

Shares of Nalco and Hindalco slid up to 5% after a six-day rally as investors booked profits and global metal prices retreated. The Nifty Metal index dropped 3% for a second day. The move followed declines in other metals such as gold and silver, signaling weaker sentiment toward copper and aluminium. | Analysts forecast aluminium prices to remain firm in 2026, citing China’s output cap at a 45-million-tonne ceiling that restricts supply growth. Demand support from EVs, renewables and infrastructure is expected to rise, while Europe smelter shutdowns and Guinea raw-material bottlenecks limit offsetting supply. | JPMorgan downgraded Alcoa to underweight, citing valuation risk after a 74% stock rally and expecting aluminum prices to ‘level out.’ The note points to higher Chinese aluminum inventories versus December averages, resistance to higher prices, Indonesia supply risks, and tariffs as near-term overhangs. | The report says secondary aluminum scrap and ADC12 spot prices surged in line with sharp primary aluminum gains, but downstream buying resistance increased as costs outpaced finished alloy prices. Secondary producers’ operating rates declined, transactions slowed, and pre–Chinese New Year stocking weakened. It expects prices to fluctuate at highs with correction pressure. | Core Silver reported drill hole LAV25-012 intersected additional copper-bearing intervals, confirming a 450-metre southern extension of the Laverdiere Cu-Mo-Ag porphyry system. While the results may modestly support copper-focused market sentiment, there is no indication of near-term changes to mined output, inventories, or refined metal flows. | Reuters reports US aluminium consumers facing record high prices as Trump’s doubled 50% tariff on imports and low US inventories amplify supply tightness. LME aluminium is up more than 20% over 12 months, while US premiums and shipments adjust, shrinking stocks below 300,000 tons, raising costs for manufacturers and inflation concerns. | GIADEC says it has secured strategic investors for VALCO’s modernisation and an alumina refinery in Ghana. The projects are framed as interdependent: the refinery would feed the smelter and support downstream aluminium manufacturing, with plans to raise VALCO output substantially from 2026 onward. | Bernstein forecasts copper averaging $11,500/tonne in Q1 2026 after record highs, attributing gains to major supply disruptions, a sizable ICSG deficit, and strong AI data-center demand. The report warns of possible later momentum fade and notes substitution toward aluminum if copper stays elevated. | Constellation Brands reported a Q3 beat but warned that a 50% U.S. aluminum import tariff keeps pressuring packaging costs. The tariff has pushed the Midwest Premium to record levels, driving higher can prices. Management and peers discuss lightweighting and possible shifts from aluminum cans to PET/glass if premiums stay elevated.

Copper prices on the LME are described as hitting record highs above $13,000/mt, driven by tight inventories, mine and regulatory supply setbacks, and a surge in AI/data-center demand. A projected 2026 deficit and “copper gap” reinforce bullish expectations, while substitution toward aluminum is discussed as a possible ceiling on copper gains.

SMM reports LME aluminium cash prices jumped in late 2025/early 2026, lifting regional scrap costs in Malaysia and Thailand. Raw material prices for ADC12 feedstock surged faster than finished ADC12 ingot prices, compressing margins. Multiple producers plan January cuts or early Lunar New Year downtime, reducing output ahead of demand recovery.

A Scottish whisky distillery is testing whether aluminium can replace glass bottles, with university researchers examining chemical interaction, safety/contamination, and sensory outcomes. Results are promising for flavour, but liners must prevent aluminium leaching. The work is exploratory, suggesting only limited near-term aluminium demand impact. | Atlantic Alumina Company (ATALCO) announced a $450m public-private investment to expand its Gramercy, Louisiana alumina refinery, the only operating US alumina plant, and to build the country’s first large-scale primary gallium production circuit. The deal aims to raise domestic alumina output and reduce reliance on China-driven supplies. | SMM reports that copper prices rose by 1,455 yuan/mt and aluminium by 1,570 yuan/mt versus the prior trading day, citing strong copper and aluminium performance above key levels. Raw material cost pressure and narrowing zinc-aluminium spreads accompany higher die-casting alloy processing fees, supporting continued near-term strength. | The piece argues early-2026 aluminum prices are surging on a “price squeeze,” citing depleted LME inventories, China’s output ceiling, weak exports, power-driven smelter outages in Europe, and high U.S./Mexico import tariffs. It expects continued backwardation and higher costs for downstream users, while smelters benefit. | Prime Minister Robert Fico wants to restart aluminium production at Slovakia’s Slovalco plant after three years of shutdown. The idle period followed soaring electricity prices after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and costly emissions allowances. He is seeking an EU-level pause on carbon permits, aiming to resume production at the site.

Private equity and the U.S. government will invest $450 million to upgrade the Atlantic Alumina (Gramercy) refinery. Officials say the project will raise alumina production capacity toward 1 million tons and improve problematic red-mud waste handling. As alumina is central to aluminum output, the plan is likely supportive for aluminum prices in coming weeks. | GIADEC, Metalloïd Resources Investment and GIBDLC secured a US$60m medium-term facility to accelerate mining at Ghana’s Nyinahin bauxite concession. Funding will support early mining across hills one to three, scaling production progressively to develop a more integrated aluminium value chain from bauxite extraction. | IIT Bhubaneswar researchers developed hyperspectral satellite-based technology to prospect bauxite in Odisha’s Koraput and Rayagada districts. Since bauxite is the primary ore of aluminium, the work aims to make exploration faster and cheaper and support sustainable resource planning. It does not signal near-term output changes.

Clark County in Nevada is responding to widespread copper streetlight wire theft that has left neighborhoods without lights. Officials expanded repair crews, allocated funding for replacement, and introduced measures including tighter recycling rules and a switch toward aluminum wiring and solar streetlights in some areas. | PIF and Riyadh-based Red Sea Aluminium Holdings sign an agreement to build a fully integrated aluminium complex in Yanbu. RSAH will bring advanced smelting and casting technologies and develop large downstream processing facilities. Construction of phase one is already under way by China Minmetals’ contractor, with a total expected cost of SR38bn. | Jefferson Parish reports widespread streetlight thefts, with copper wiring stolen from roughly 300 light poles over eight months and repairs estimated up to $1.5 million. Authorities are ordering parts, refurbishing poles, and moving some wiring to aluminum, which is cheaper and less valuable as scrap. | Saudi PIF agreed with Red Sea Aluminium Holdings to develop an integrated downstream aluminium complex in Yanbu, including advanced smelting technologies and a major continuous casting facility. The project aims to produce high-value downstream aluminium products for global and local demand, supporting expectations of expanded aluminium supply. | Amarc reports expanded drilling results and continued advancement of its copper-gold projects in British Columbia, including the AuRORA discovery within the JOY district and progress at the DUKE and IKE districts. It highlights staged JV funding with Freeport and expects transactions. No aluminium-specific developments are mentioned. | The article argues copper is in a structural supply deficit, with LME inventories at decade lows and further upside risk from production disruptions (e.g., Grasberg mud accident) and rising demand from AI data centers and power-grid expansion. It forecasts continued parabolic copper prices and discusses some substitution toward aluminium, but stresses copper scarcity. | Reuters reported Glencore is in takeover discussions with Rio Tinto that could form a $207B mining group. Rio is also pursuing cost reductions and targeting higher copper production. These developments may affect market sentiment around copper supplies, but the article lacks concrete near-term supply or demand shocks for aluminium. | Venezuela’s aluminium smelting is operating well below its installed capacity because of technical and infrastructure problems, leading to high off-spec production. This likely constrains exports and could alter flows between Brazil, Europe and the US, but traded volumes in Brazil are small, implying limited near-term aluminium price impact.

Prospect Resources reported new exploration intercepts at its Mumbezhi copper project in Zambia, confirming gold by-product potential. The article notes copper prices have firmed above $13,000/t and gold is near records, with plans to update resources and begin early studies. It signals potential longer-term value, not immediate supply changes. | SMM reports China’s secondary aluminum operating rate fell to 44.1% in December amid weaker demand after mid-month, tight scrap supply and high costs, and environmental-related production restrictions. With demand still soft into January and high aluminum prices limiting cost pass-through and stockpiling, January operating rates are expected to drop further. | Venezuela’s state smelters (CVG Venalum and CVG Alcasa) have largely shut down amid political turmoil and severe infrastructure/technology failures, leaving mostly off-grade output. With imports constrained by tariffs and quality issues, the U.S. and Brazil face a supply vacuum, lifting regional aluminum premiums and supporting higher prices. | SMM reports weaker December aluminum rod production and declining in-factory inventories, with processing fees largely bottoming and returning to more reasonable regional levels. Downstream demand remains cautious due to high aluminum prices, and plants expect slower January operating rates. Wire/cable output is stable, with modest near-term recovery potential. | SMM reports aluminum billet processing fees across Chinese regions fell into negative territory after A00 aluminum prices surged post-holiday, reflecting profit-realization and faster destocking by billet producers and traders. By mid-January, fees began rebounding toward parity, but remained negative in parts, with a forecast return to 0–200 yuan/mt. | The European Commission imposed anti-dumping duties of 88.7% to 110.6% on Chinese fused alumina imports and added a quota for duty-free volumes. The EU says the move addresses unfair dumping harming domestic supply and protects downstream industrial risks. The restriction is likely to support aluminium-linked input prices. | Rio Tinto said its fair value estimates rose as copper and aluminium prices strengthened. The company’s sales are heavily tied to China, and it expects earnings could decline if commodity demand softens after the China boom. Nonetheless, the update signals near-term support from higher copper and aluminium pricing assumptions. | Rio Tinto’s fair value estimate rose as market prices for copper and aluminum strengthened. The piece notes Rio’s heavy exposure to China demand and suggests that after the earlier China boom, commodity demand may soften, but the immediate catalyst cited is the increase in copper and aluminum prices. | A Business Strategy and Outlook note says Rio Tinto’s fair value estimates rose as copper and aluminium prices strengthened. It highlights Rio Tinto’s exposure to these metals and that earnings expectations depend heavily on global, especially China, demand. The cited catalyst is the recent firming in copper and aluminium prices. | Rio Tinto’s fair value estimate was raised as copper and aluminium prices strengthened. The piece highlights Rio’s major exposure to China demand and notes earnings sensitivity to global growth. While it discusses a longer-term demand slowdown after the China boom, the immediate catalyst is higher copper and aluminium prices. | Authorities report a Hessmer man accused of stealing AT&T copper wire, cutting phone service lines and damaging poles. The thefts allegedly involved about $100,000 in copper over a week, plus $30,000 damage. Police also found aluminum sheathing and wires at the scene, posing a railroad hazard. | Angolan President inaugurated a Chinese-backed electrolytic aluminum plant in the Barra do Dande Free Trade Zone. The first phase is a $250 million investment aiming to produce 120,000 tonnes of electrolytic aluminum annually. The project is planned in phases with job creation and workforce training for local industry.

AFASL warns a policy gap in Sri Lanka’s BOI Negative List allows duty-free imports of fully fabricated aluminium doors, windows and façade systems, while duty protection applies mainly to raw extrusions. The resulting import undercutting is said to threaten local jobs and reduce domestic value-chain demand, pressuring expectations for aluminium. | NALCO expects India’s aluminium demand to stay strong as upcoming budget thrusts on infrastructure and power sector expansion boost consumption of conductors, cables and construction-linked uses. The firm is scaling up alumina output and is ahead of targets, with steady aluminium production plans despite subdued prices. | NALCO’s CMD said India’s aluminium demand should stay strong as the government is expected to keep prioritising infrastructure development and power sector expansion in the upcoming Union Budget. The statement implies improved demand outlook for aluminium, which could support short-term aluminium price expectations. | The article weighs scenarios for Indonesia’s 2026 bauxite RKAB quotas (18, 22, or 25 Mt) against 23–25 Mt projected demand tied to alumina refinery expansions. A most-likely 18–22 Mt outcome implies tighter supply discipline and potential bauxite price normalization, supporting higher near-term expectations for aluminium-related feedstock costs.

BHP reported strong first-half FY26 results and lifted group copper production guidance after record output at several copper assets, including Escondida and Antamina. The company cited a favorable pricing environment and improved operational delivery, while iron ore and coal also performed well. | Nanshan Aluminum shares rose after announcing a USD437 million plan to build a 250,000-ton electrolytic aluminum plant in Indonesia’s Bintan Industrial Estate. Construction is expected to take two years and is meant to leverage local bauxite resources and meet downstream demand. It follows a similar facility announced in April 2023. | The piece reports copper prices surging while the copper-to-aluminum ratio exceeds 4.2, prompting manufacturers to accelerate substitution toward aluminum across HVAC and electrical uses. It attributes copper strength to supply disruptions and geopolitics, while highlighting tight aluminum supply/cap constraints in China. Net effect: lower near-term copper demand prospects and price pressure. | The piece says the copper-to-aluminium price ratio has exceeded 4.2 after copper supply shocks and aluminium supply constraints in China, accelerating copper-to-aluminium substitution in HVAC/EV-related uses. It describes near-term aluminium demand tailwinds and highlights looming aluminium capacity/surplus risk, creating mixed price pressures. | LME copper surged past $13,000/tonne, driven by decade-low inventories, widening refined deficits, and geopolitical shock following US action in Venezuela raising South America supply-risk premiums. Analysts cite record scarcity and AI/energy-transition demand growth. The outlook suggests further volatility and potential substitution to aluminium only if copper remains very high. | The piece describes a 2026 industrial metals rally, highlighting copper at $5.84/lb and attributing strength to mine disruption at Freeport’s Grasberg, permit delays in North America and Chile, and rising demand from AI data centers and electrification. It anticipates continued volatility but overall bullish price pressure into early 2026. | Rio Tinto reported higher 2025 output, with copper production rising 11% to 883 thousand tonnes and exceeding guidance, helped by the Oyu Tolgoi underground ramp-up. Aluminium production increased 3% to 3.38 million tonnes. Q4 shipments also improved, suggesting steadier supply outlook.

China’s export tax rebate adjustment (ending the 13% rebate for aluminum semis and copper semis as of Dec 1, 2024) is linked to a fall in 2025 aluminum semi exports and a shift toward processing trade with imported materials. SMM expects higher processing trade share in 2026, supporting export competitiveness. | Rio Tinto said 2025 copper equivalent output rose 8% and copper production climbed 11% YoY, with shipments up 5%, helped by Oyu Tolgoi ramp-up. The company also reported increased aluminium output (up 3% for the year) alongside bauxite growth, implying improved supply momentum. | China’s customs data show unwrought aluminum alloy imports fell in 2025 but December imports rose MoM, while exports increased YoY. The weak import trend was tied to inverted domestic/overseas price spreads and higher overseas alloy ingot prices. Late-Dec/early-Jan ADC12 prices rose, reopening the import window and supporting aluminium demand expectations. | NITI Aayog released a decarbonisation roadmap for aluminium (and cement/MSMEs), projecting a major rise in aluminium output and highlighting high electricity-driven emissions. It recommends renewable power, dedicated RE grids, and transmission utility reforms for near-term action, aiming to reduce emissions and manage potential future export restrictions.

SMM expects China’s alumina capacity to rise to 118.92 million mt in 2026, with 8.6 million mt of new projects starting and 7.6 million mt in Guangxi. The added supply shifts Guangxi toward surplus, likely driving regional and national alumina price declines and increasing global competition, bearish for aluminium. | South32 reported 3% higher December half-year alumina output and a 2% rise in aluminium production, citing record Brazil Alumina performance and increased Hillside capacity testing. Mozal ceased pot relining ahead of a planned March 2026 care-and-maintenance shift. Copper production met expectations with strong overall first-half volumes. | South32 reported a 3% rise in alumina production and 2% higher aluminium output in the December 2025 half-year, citing record Brazil Alumina performance and Hillside capacity testing, plus Mozal pot relining ahead of planned care and maintenance. Copper production was in line with expectations, so near-term effects are likely small. | Abitibi Metals announced additional high-grade copper-gold intercepts from Phase 3 drilling at the B26 polymetallic deposit in Québec, including 12.83% CuEq over 5.0 metres. The company is expanding drilling into Phase 4 and highlights resource growth potential, implying incremental bullish sentiment for copper, but no confirmed near-term supply change.

SMM cites China’s aluminum wire and cable exports rising 42.9% YoY in 2025, with December also higher YoY. Growth is linked to renewed overseas spending on renewable energy and power grid modernization, supported by specific national policies in Australia and Tanzania. Export momentum is expected to persist into 2026. | Prospect Ridge reports final IP survey results identifying a large chargeability/resistivity/magnetic target at its Excalibur copper-gold porphyry project and expects to finalize drill targets and begin a maiden diamond program in June 2026, subject to permits. The update is sentiment-supportive for copper but no near-term supply effect is stated.
